Benchmark Results for claude-opus-4-6-HighReasoning-Tools

Overall Summary

Test 1: Travelling Salesman Problem Solver (C++) Score: 5.00 / 21
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the Travelling Salesman Problem (TSP) in C++.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY number of cities from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 10-40 cities (brute-force feasible for smallest cases)
- **Medium**: 100-1000 cities (requires heuristics)
- **Large**: 5000-100000 cities (requires efficient heuristics)
- **Extreme**: 500000-5000000 cities (requires very fast approximations)
- **Epic**: 10000000+ cities (requires highly optimized approximations)

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with city counts ranging from 10 to 10000000. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N (number of cities)
Lines 2..N+1: x y coordinates (floats)

**Output format (stdout):**
One line with N integers: the visit order of city indices (0-indexed), starting at city 0.
Do NOT include returning to city 0 (return is implicit).

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on problem size
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 300 seconds even for 1000,000,000 cities
3. **Quality**: Should produce reasonable routes at all scales
4. **Correctness**: Output must be a valid permutation of 0..N-1 starting with 0

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function that reads stdin and writes stdout.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on N.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: 10 Cities
View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a list of cities, find the shortest round-trip route that visits every city exactly once and returns to the start. This is one of the most famous hard problems in computer science — checking every possible route is only feasible for a handful of cities, so the AI must use clever shortcuts to find a good route quickly.

Subpasses scale from 10 cities up to 1 000, so brute-force is impossible for the larger cases. The AI's route length is compared against a simple baseline; beating it earns full marks.

 1.0000
[10 cities] Route distance: 2449.07, Baseline: 2922.93, Ratio: 0.84x, Time: 0.1s - excellent (within 10% of baseline)

TSP Solver - 10 Cities

View Code (8315 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <cmath>
#include <algorithm>
#include <numeric>
#include <chrono>

using namespace std;

struct Point { float x, y; };

static inline float dist(const Point& a, const Point& b) {
    float dx = a.x - b.x, dy = a.y - b.y;
    return sqrtf(dx*dx + dy*dy);
}

struct GridIndex {
    int gw, gh;
    float minx, miny, cellw, cellh;
    vector<vector<int>> cells;

    void build(const vector<Point>& pts, int n) {
        int gridSize = max(1, (int)sqrt((double)n / 4.0));
        gw = gh = gridSize;
        minx = miny = 1e18f;
        float maxx = -1e18f, maxy = -1e18f;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            if (pts[i].x < minx) minx = pts[i].x;
            if (pts[i].y < miny) miny = pts[i].y;
            if (pts[i].x > maxx) maxx = pts[i].x;
            if (pts[i].y > maxy) maxy = pts[i].y;
        }
        float pad = 1e-6f;
        cellw = (maxx - minx + pad) / gw;
        cellh = (maxy - miny + pad) / gh;
        if (cellw < 1e-12f) cellw = 1.0f;
        if (cellh < 1e-12f) cellh = 1.0f;
        cells.resize((size_t)gw * gh);
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            int cx = min((int)((pts[i].x - minx) / cellw), gw - 1);
            int cy = min((int)((pts[i].y - miny) / cellh), gh - 1);
            cells[(size_t)cy * gw + cx].push_back(i);
        }
    }

    int findNearest(const vector<Point>& pts, int qi, const vector<bool>& used) const {
        float qx = pts[qi].x, qy = pts[qi].y;
        int cx = min((int)((qx - minx) / cellw), gw - 1);
        int cy = min((int)((qy - miny) / cellh), gh - 1);
        float bestD = 1e30f;
        int bestIdx = -1;
        for (int radius = 0; radius <= max(gw, gh); radius++) {
            if (bestIdx >= 0 && radius > 1) {
                float minPossible = (float)(radius - 1) * min(cellw, cellh);
                if (minPossible > bestD) break;
            }
            int x0 = max(0, cx - radius), x1 = min(gw - 1, cx + radius);
            int y0 = max(0, cy - radius), y1 = min(gh - 1, cy + radius);
            for (int yy = y0; yy <= y1; yy++) {
                for (int xx = x0; xx <= x1; xx++) {
                    if (radius > 0 && xx > x0 && xx < x1 && yy > y0 && yy < y1) continue;
                    for (int idx : cells[(size_t)yy * gw + xx]) {
                        if (used[idx]) continue;
                        float d = dist(pts[qi], pts[idx]);
                        if (d < bestD) { bestD = d; bestIdx = idx; }
                    }
                }
            }
            if (bestIdx >= 0 && radius >= 1) break;
        }
        return bestIdx;
    }
};

vector<int> greedyNNSimple(const vector<Point>& pts, int n) {
    vector<int> tour;
    tour.reserve(n);
    vector<bool> used(n, false);
    tour.push_back(0);
    used[0] = true;
    for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) {
        int last = tour.back();
        float bestD = 1e30f;
        int bestIdx = -1;
        for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) {
            if (!used[j]) {
                float d = dist(pts[last], pts[j]);
                if (d < bestD) { bestD = d; bestIdx = j; }
            }
        }
        tour.push_back(bestIdx);
        used[bestIdx] = true;
    }
    return tour;
}

vector<int> greedyNN(const vector<Point>& pts, int n, const GridIndex& grid) {
    vector<int> tour;
    tour.reserve(n);
    vector<bool> used(n, false);
    tour.push_back(0);
    used[0] = true;
    for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) {
        int nxt = grid.findNearest(pts, tour.back(), used);
        tour.push_back(nxt);
        used[nxt] = true;
    }
    return tour;
}

void twoOpt(const vector<Point>& pts, vector<int>& tour, int n, double timeLimit) {
    auto start = chrono::steady_clock::now();
    bool improved = true;
    while (improved) {
        improved = false;
        for (int i = 0; i < n - 2; i++) {
            for (int j = i + 2; j < n; j++) {
                int a = tour[i], b = tour[i+1];
                int c = tour[j], d = tour[(j+1) % n];
                float oldD = dist(pts[a], pts[b]) + dist(pts[c], pts[d]);
                float newD = dist(pts[a], pts[c]) + dist(pts[b], pts[d]);
                if (newD < oldD - 1e-10f) {
                    reverse(tour.begin() + i + 1, tour.begin() + j + 1);
                    improved = true;
                }
            }
            if (i % 100 == 0) {
                double elapsed = chrono::duration<double>(chrono::steady_clock::now() - start).count();
                if (elapsed > timeLimit) return;
            }
        }
    }
}

void twoOptLarge(const vector<Point>& pts, vector<int>& tour, int n, const GridIndex& grid, double timeLimit) {
    auto start = chrono::steady_clock::now();
    vector<int> pos(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) pos[tour[i]] = i;

    bool improved = true;
    int passCount = 0;
    while (improved) {
        improved = false;
        passCount++;
        for (int idx = 0; idx < n; idx++) {
            int i = idx;
            int a = tour[i];
            int inext = (i + 1) % n;
            int b = tour[inext];
            float dab = dist(pts[a], pts[b]);

            int cx = min((int)((pts[a].x - grid.minx) / grid.cellw), grid.gw - 1);
            int cy = min((int)((pts[a].y - grid.miny) / grid.cellh), grid.gh - 1);

            int maxRad = min(5, max(grid.gw, grid.gh));
            bool found = false;
            for (int radius = 0; radius <= maxRad && !found; radius++) {
                int x0 = max(0, cx - radius), x1 = min(grid.gw - 1, cx + radius);
                int y0 = max(0, cy - radius), y1 = min(grid.gh - 1, cy + radius);
                for (int yy = y0; yy <= y1 && !found; yy++) {
                    for (int xx = x0; xx <= x1 && !found; xx++) {
                        if (radius > 0 && xx > x0 && xx < x1 && yy > y0 && yy < y1) continue;
                        for (int c : grid.cells[(size_t)yy * grid.gw + xx]) {
                            int j = pos[c];
                            if (abs(j - i) <= 1 || (i == 0 && j == n-1) || (j == 0 && i == n-1)) continue;
                            int jj = j, ii = i;
                            if (jj < ii) { swap(ii, jj); }
                            int inxt = ii + 1;
                            int jnxt = (jj + 1) % n;
                            int aa = tour[ii], bb = tour[inxt];
                            int cc = tour[jj], dd = tour[jnxt];
                            float oldCost = dist(pts[aa], pts[bb]) + dist(pts[cc], pts[dd]);
                            float newCost = dist(pts[aa], pts[cc]) + dist(pts[bb], pts[dd]);
                            if (newCost < oldCost - 1e-10f) {
                                reverse(tour.begin() + inxt, tour.begin() + jj + 1);
                                for (int k = inxt; k <= jj; k++) pos[tour[k]] = k;
                                improved = true;
                                found = true;
                                break;
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            }

            if (idx % 5000 == 0) {
                double elapsed = chrono::duration<double>(chrono::steady_clock::now() - start).count();
                if (elapsed > timeLimit) return;
            }
        }
    }
}

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int n;
    cin >> n;
    vector<Point> pts(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) cin >> pts[i].x >> pts[i].y;

    vector<int> tour;

    if (n <= 1) {
        tour.resize(n);
        iota(tour.begin(), tour.end(), 0);
    } else if (n <= 3000) {
        tour = greedyNNSimple(pts, n);
        double tl = 250.0;
        if (n <= 500) tl = 60.0;
        twoOpt(pts, tour, n, tl);
    } else {
        GridIndex grid;
        grid.build(pts, n);
        tour = greedyNN(pts, n, grid);

        if (n <= 500000) {
            twoOptLarge(pts, tour, n, grid, 200.0);
        } else if (n <= 5000000) {
            twoOptLarge(pts, tour, n, grid, 150.0);
        }
    }

    if (tour[0] != 0) {
        int zp = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) if (tour[i] == 0) { zp = i; break; }
        rotate(tour.begin(), tour.begin() + zp, tour.end());
    }

    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        if (i) cout << ' ';
        cout << tour[i];
    }
    cout << '\n';
    return 0;
}

Test 2: Chinese Postman Problem Solver (C#) Score: 10.50 / 13
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the Chinese Postman Problem (Route Inspection Problem) in C#.

You must write a C# solver that can handle ANY graph size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 6-20 nodes, 8-45 edges (small graphs, exact algorithms feasible)
- **Medium**: 35-200 nodes, 80-600 edges (requires efficient algorithms)
- **Large**: 500-1000 nodes, 2000-5000 edges (requires optimized implementations)
- **Extreme**: 5000-10000 nodes, 30000-80000 edges (requires very fast algorithms)
- **Epic**: 50000-100000 nodes, 500000-1000000 edges (requires highly optimized algorithms)

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with graphs ranging from 6 nodes to 100000 nodes. The same function must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N M (number of nodes, number of edges)
Lines 2..M+1: u v w (edge endpoints, 0-indexed, weight integer)

**Output format (stdout):**
A sequence of node indices (whitespace-separated) representing a route that starts at node 0,
traverses EVERY edge at least once, and returns to node 0.
You may optionally prefix with a single integer L (route length). If present, it must match the number of nodes listed.

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on graph size and complexity
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for large graphs
3. **Correctness**: Must traverse every edge at least once

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Imagine a mail carrier who must walk along every street in a neighbourhood and return home. Some streets may need to be walked twice if the network doesn't allow a perfect loop — the goal is to minimise that extra walking.

The AI must figure out which streets (edges) to re-traverse and produce a complete route. Subpasses increase the graph size, making the problem harder to solve efficiently.

 1.0000
[6 nodes, 8 edges] Route distance: 511, Baseline: 684, Ratio: 0.75x, Time: 0.1s - excellent (within 5% of baseline)

Chinese Postman Problem - 6 nodes, 8 edges

View Code (7304 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using System.Text;

class Program
{
    static byte[] _buf = new byte[1 << 16];
    static int _bp, _bl;
    static Stream _sin;

    static int RB()
    {
        if (_bp >= _bl) { _bl = _sin.Read(_buf, 0, _buf.Length); _bp = 0; if (_bl <= 0) return -1; }
        return _buf[_bp++];
    }

    static int RI()
    {
        int c = RB();
        while (c == ' ' || c == '\n' || c == '\r' || c == '\t') c = RB();
        bool neg = c == '-';
        if (neg) c = RB();
        int v = 0;
        while (c >= '0' && c <= '9') { v = v * 10 + c - '0'; c = RB(); }
        return neg ? -v : v;
    }

    static void Main()
    {
        _sin = Console.OpenStandardInput();
        int N = RI(), M = RI();

        if (M == 0) { Console.WriteLine("0"); return; }

        var eu = new int[M];
        var ev = new int[M];
        var ew = new int[M];
        var deg = new int[N];
        var adj = new List<(int v, int w)>[N];
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) adj[i] = new List<(int, int)>();

        for (int i = 0; i < M; i++)
        {
            eu[i] = RI(); ev[i] = RI(); ew[i] = RI();
            deg[eu[i]]++;
            deg[ev[i]]++;
            adj[eu[i]].Add((ev[i], ew[i]));
            adj[ev[i]].Add((eu[i], ew[i]));
        }

        var odd = new List<int>();
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++)
            if (deg[i] % 2 == 1) odd.Add(i);

        var extra = new List<(int u, int v)>();
        int K = odd.Count;
        if (K > 0)
        {
            if (K <= 20)
                ExactMatch(N, adj, odd, K, extra);
            else
                GreedyMatch(N, adj, odd, K, extra);
        }

        int TE = M + extra.Count;
        var ea = new List<(int to, int idx)>[N];
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) ea[i] = new List<(int, int)>();
        for (int i = 0; i < M; i++)
        {
            ea[eu[i]].Add((ev[i], i));
            ea[ev[i]].Add((eu[i], i));
        }
        for (int i = 0; i < extra.Count; i++)
        {
            int idx = M + i;
            ea[extra[i].u].Add((extra[i].v, idx));
            ea[extra[i].v].Add((extra[i].u, idx));
        }

        var used = new bool[TE];
        var ptr = new int[N];
        var stk = new Stack<int>();
        var cir = new List<int>(TE + 1);
        stk.Push(0);

        while (stk.Count > 0)
        {
            int u = stk.Peek();
            bool found = false;
            while (ptr[u] < ea[u].Count)
            {
                var (to, idx) = ea[u][ptr[u]];
                ptr[u]++;
                if (!used[idx])
                {
                    used[idx] = true;
                    stk.Push(to);
                    found = true;
                    break;
                }
            }
            if (!found)
            {
                stk.Pop();
                cir.Add(u);
            }
        }

        var sb = new StringBuilder(cir.Count * 5);
        for (int i = 0; i < cir.Count; i++)
        {
            if (i > 0) sb.Append(' ');
            sb.Append(cir[i]);
        }
        using var sw = new StreamWriter(Console.OpenStandardOutput(), Encoding.ASCII, 1 << 16);
        sw.WriteLine(sb.ToString());
    }

    static void ExactMatch(int N, List<(int v, int w)>[] adj, List<int> odd, int K, List<(int u, int v)> extra)
    {
        var ds = new long[K][];
        var ps = new int[K][];
        for (int i = 0; i < K; i++)
            (ds[i], ps[i]) = DijkstraFull(N, adj, odd[i]);

        int full = (1 << K) - 1;
        var dp = new long[1 << K];
        var match = new int[1 << K];
        Array.Fill(dp, long.MaxValue);
        dp[0] = 0;

        for (int mask = 0; mask < (1 << K); mask++)
        {
            if (dp[mask] == long.MaxValue) continue;
            int lo = 0;
            while ((mask & (1 << lo)) != 0) lo++;
            for (int j = lo + 1; j < K; j++)
            {
                if ((mask & (1 << j)) != 0) continue;
                int nm = mask | (1 << lo) | (1 << j);
                long c = dp[mask] + ds[lo][odd[j]];
                if (c < dp[nm])
                {
                    dp[nm] = c;
                    match[nm] = (lo << 16) | j;
                }
            }
        }

        int cm = full;
        while (cm != 0)
        {
            int pk = match[cm];
            int ii = pk >> 16, jj = pk & 0xFFFF;
            cm ^= (1 << ii) | (1 << jj);
            TracePath(odd[ii], odd[jj], ps[ii], extra);
        }
    }

    static void GreedyMatch(int N, List<(int v, int w)>[] adj, List<int> odd, int K, List<(int u, int v)> extra)
    {
        var um = new bool[N];
        foreach (var v in odd) um[v] = true;
        int rem = K;

        var dist = new long[N];
        var prev = new int[N];
        Array.Fill(dist, long.MaxValue);
        Array.Fill(prev, -1);

        var vis = new List<int>(1024);
        var pq = new PriorityQueue<int, long>();
        int oi = 0;

        while (rem > 1)
        {
            while (oi < odd.Count && !um[odd[oi]]) oi++;
            if (oi >= odd.Count) break;
            int src = odd[oi++];
            um[src] = false;

            vis.Clear();
            pq.Clear();
            dist[src] = 0;
            vis.Add(src);
            pq.Enqueue(src, 0);
            int near = -1;

            while (pq.Count > 0)
            {
                pq.TryDequeue(out int u, out long d);
                if (d > dist[u]) continue;
                if (u != src && um[u]) { near = u; break; }
                foreach (var (v, w) in adj[u])
                {
                    long nd = d + w;
                    if (nd < dist[v])
                    {
                        if (dist[v] == long.MaxValue) vis.Add(v);
                        dist[v] = nd;
                        prev[v] = u;
                        pq.Enqueue(v, nd);
                    }
                }
            }

            if (near != -1)
            {
                um[near] = false;
                rem -= 2;
                TracePath(src, near, prev, extra);
            }
            else
            {
                rem--;
            }

            foreach (var v in vis) { dist[v] = long.MaxValue; prev[v] = -1; }
        }
    }

    static (long[], int[]) DijkstraFull(int N, List<(int v, int w)>[] adj, int src)
    {
        var dist = new long[N];
        var prev = new int[N];
        Array.Fill(dist, long.MaxValue);
        Array.Fill(prev, -1);
        dist[src] = 0;
        var pq = new PriorityQueue<int, long>();
        pq.Enqueue(src, 0);
        while (pq.Count > 0)
        {
            pq.TryDequeue(out int u, out long d);
            if (d > dist[u]) continue;
            foreach (var (v, w) in adj[u])
            {
                long nd = d + w;
                if (nd < dist[v])
                {
                    dist[v] = nd;
                    prev[v] = u;
                    pq.Enqueue(v, nd);
                }
            }
        }
        return (dist, prev);
    }

    static void TracePath(int from, int to, int[] prev, List<(int u, int v)> extra)
    {
        int cur = to;
        while (cur != from && cur != -1)
        {
            int p = prev[cur];
            if (p >= 0) extra.Add((p, cur));
            cur = p;
        }
    }
}

Test 3: Graph Layout - Edge Crossing Minimization (Rust) Score: 1.25 / 16
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a Graph Layout problem (Edge Crossing Minimization) in Rust.

You must write a Rust solver that can handle ANY graph size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 6-15 nodes, small trees and cycles (planar, 0 crossings achievable)
- **Medium**: 20-100 nodes, dense planar and non-planar graphs
- **Large**: 500-1000 nodes, random dense graphs (many crossings unavoidable)
- **Extreme**: 5000-10000 nodes, very large random graphs

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with graphs ranging from 6 nodes to 200,000 nodes. The same solver must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N M (number of nodes, number of edges)
Lines 2..M+1: u v (undirected edge endpoints, 0-indexed)

**Output format (stdout):**
Output N lines, one per node, with: x y coordinates (floats).
You may optionally prefix with a single integer N on the first line.

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on graph size and density
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for large graphs
3. **Quality**: Should produce reasonable layouts with minimal crossings
4. **Robustness**: Handle disconnected components, self-loops, and parallel edges gracefully

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a graph (a set of dots connected by lines), arrange the dots on a 2D canvas so that as few lines cross each other as possible. A clean, readable layout is the goal — tangled spaghetti is the enemy.

Minimising crossings is extremely hard in general. The AI must place nodes intelligently; the baseline simply puts them in a circle. Subpasses increase the number of nodes and edges.

 1.0000
[tree, 6 nodes, 5 edges] Crossings: 0, Baseline: 1, Time: 0.0s - perfect (0 crossings)

Graph Layout - tree (6 nodes, 5 edges)

View Code (13383 chars)
use std::io::{self, Read, Write, BufWriter};

fn main() {
    let mut input = String::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut input).unwrap();
    let stdout = io::stdout();
    let mut out = BufWriter::new(stdout.lock());

    let mut iter = input.split_whitespace();
    let n: usize = iter.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let m: usize = iter.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();

    let mut adj: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![vec![]; n];
    for _ in 0..m {
        let u: usize = iter.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        let v: usize = iter.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        if u == v || u >= n || v >= n {
            continue;
        }
        adj[u].push(v);
        adj[v].push(u);
    }

    for i in 0..n {
        adj[i].sort_unstable();
        adj[i].dedup();
    }

    let mut x = vec![0.0f64; n];
    let mut y = vec![0.0f64; n];

    if n <= 1 {
        for i in 0..n {
            writeln!(out, "{:.6} {:.6}", i as f64, 0.0).unwrap();
        }
        return;
    }

    let mut comp = vec![usize::MAX; n];
    let mut comp_id = 0usize;
    let mut comp_sizes: Vec<usize> = Vec::new();
    let mut queue = Vec::new();
    for start in 0..n {
        if comp[start] != usize::MAX {
            continue;
        }
        let mut size = 0usize;
        queue.push(start);
        comp[start] = comp_id;
        while let Some(node) = queue.pop() {
            size += 1;
            for &nb in &adj[node] {
                if comp[nb] == usize::MAX {
                    comp[nb] = comp_id;
                    queue.push(nb);
                }
            }
        }
        comp_sizes.push(size);
        comp_id += 1;
    }

    let num_comps = comp_sizes.len();
    let mut comp_nodes: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![vec![]; num_comps];
    for i in 0..n {
        comp_nodes[comp[i]].push(i);
    }

    let mut comp_positions: Vec<Vec<(f64, f64)>> = Vec::with_capacity(num_comps);

    for c in 0..num_comps {
        let nodes = &comp_nodes[c];
        let cn = nodes.len();

        if cn == 1 {
            comp_positions.push(vec![(0.0, 0.0)]);
            continue;
        }

        if cn == 2 {
            comp_positions.push(vec![(0.0, 0.0), (1.0, 0.0)]);
            continue;
        }

        let mut local_id = vec![0usize; n];
        for (i, &node) in nodes.iter().enumerate() {
            local_id[node] = i;
        }
        let mut local_adj: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![vec![]; cn];
        for &node in nodes {
            let li = local_id[node];
            for &nb in &adj[node] {
                if comp[nb] == c {
                    local_adj[li].push(local_id[nb]);
                }
            }
        }

        let positions = if cn <= 5000 {
            layout_force_directed(&local_adj, cn)
        } else {
            layout_multilevel(&local_adj, cn)
        };

        comp_positions.push(positions);
    }

    let mut offset_x = 0.0f64;
    for c in 0..num_comps {
        let positions = &comp_positions[c];
        let nodes = &comp_nodes[c];

        let mut min_x = f64::MAX;
        let mut max_x = f64::MIN;
        let mut min_y = f64::MAX;
        let mut max_y = f64::MIN;
        for &(px, py) in positions.iter() {
            if px < min_x { min_x = px; }
            if px > max_x { max_x = px; }
            if py < min_y { min_y = py; }
            if py > max_y { max_y = py; }
        }

        let width = max_x - min_x;
        for (i, &node) in nodes.iter().enumerate() {
            x[node] = positions[i].0 - min_x + offset_x;
            y[node] = positions[i].1 - min_y;
        }
        offset_x += width + 2.0;
    }

    writeln!(out, "{}", n).unwrap();
    for i in 0..n {
        writeln!(out, "{:.6} {:.6}", x[i], y[i]).unwrap();
    }
}

fn layout_force_directed(adj: &[Vec<usize>], n: usize) -> Vec<(f64, f64)> {
    let mut x = vec![0.0f64; n];
    let mut y = vec![0.0f64; n];

    let mut max_deg_node = 0;
    let mut max_deg = 0;
    for i in 0..n {
        if adj[i].len() > max_deg {
            max_deg = adj[i].len();
            max_deg_node = i;
        }
    }

    let mut dist = vec![usize::MAX; n];
    let mut bfs_queue = std::collections::VecDeque::new();
    dist[max_deg_node] = 0;
    bfs_queue.push_back(max_deg_node);
    while let Some(node) = bfs_queue.pop_front() {
        for &nb in &adj[node] {
            if dist[nb] == usize::MAX {
                dist[nb] = dist[node] + 1;
                bfs_queue.push_back(nb);
            }
        }
    }

    let mut rng_state: u64 = 12345;
    let mut next_rand = || -> f64 {
        rng_state = rng_state.wrapping_mul(6364136223846793005).wrapping_add(1442695040888963407);
        ((rng_state >> 33) as f64) / (2147483648.0)
    };

    let k = (n as f64).sqrt();
    for i in 0..n {
        if dist[i] != usize::MAX {
            x[i] = dist[i] as f64 * k * 0.5 + next_rand() * k * 0.1;
            y[i] = next_rand() * k * (n as f64).sqrt() * 0.5;
        } else {
            x[i] = next_rand() * k * (n as f64).sqrt();
            y[i] = next_rand() * k * (n as f64).sqrt();
        }
    }

    let iterations = if n <= 50 { 500 } else if n <= 200 { 300 } else if n <= 1000 { 200 } else { 100 };
    let mut temp = k * (n as f64).sqrt() * 0.1;
    let cooling = 0.92;
    let use_barnes_hut = n > 200;

    for _iter in 0..iterations {
        let mut dx = vec![0.0f64; n];
        let mut dy = vec![0.0f64; n];

        if use_barnes_hut {
            barnes_hut_repulsion(&x, &y, n, k, &mut dx, &mut dy);
        } else {
            for i in 0..n {
                for j in (i + 1)..n {
                    let ddx = x[i] - x[j];
                    let ddy = y[i] - y[j];
                    let dist_sq = ddx * ddx + ddy * ddy;
                    let d = dist_sq.sqrt().max(0.01);
                    let force = k * k / d;
                    let fx = ddx / d * force;
                    let fy = ddy / d * force;
                    dx[i] += fx;
                    dy[i] += fy;
                    dx[j] -= fx;
                    dy[j] -= fy;
                }
            }
        }

        for i in 0..n {
            for &j in &adj[i] {
                if j > i { continue; }
                let ddx = x[i] - x[j];
                let ddy = y[i] - y[j];
                let d = (ddx * ddx + ddy * ddy).sqrt().max(0.01);
                let force = d / k;
                let fx = ddx / d * force;
                let fy = ddy / d * force;
                dx[i] -= fx;
                dy[i] -= fy;
                dx[j] += fx;
                dy[j] += fy;
            }
        }

        for i in 0..n {
            let disp = (dx[i] * dx[i] + dy[i] * dy[i]).sqrt().max(0.0001);
            let scale = temp.min(disp) / disp;
            x[i] += dx[i] * scale;
            y[i] += dy[i] * scale;
        }

        temp *= cooling;
    }

    (0..n).map(|i| (x[i], y[i])).collect()
}

struct QuadTree {
    cx: f64,
    cy: f64,
    mass: f64,
    size: f64,
    min_x: f64,
    min_y: f64,
    children: [Option<Box<QuadTree>>; 4],
    is_leaf: bool,
    node_x: f64,
    node_y: f64,
}

impl QuadTree {
    fn new(min_x: f64, min_y: f64, size: f64) -> Self {
        QuadTree {
            cx: 0.0, cy: 0.0, mass: 0.0, size,
            min_x, min_y,
            children: [None, None, None, None],
            is_leaf: true,
            node_x: 0.0, node_y: 0.0,
        }
    }

    fn insert(&mut self, px: f64, py: f64) {
        if self.mass == 0.0 {
            self.cx = px;
            self.cy = py;
            self.mass = 1.0;
            self.node_x = px;
            self.node_y = py;
            self.is_leaf = true;
            return;
        }

        if self.is_leaf && self.mass == 1.0 {
            if self.size < 1e-6 {
                self.mass += 1.0;
                self.cx = (self.cx * (self.mass - 1.0) + px) / self.mass;
                self.cy = (self.cy * (self.mass - 1.0) + py) / self.mass;
                return;
            }
            let old_x = self.node_x;
            let old_y = self.node_y;
            self.is_leaf = false;
            self.insert_into_child(old_x, old_y);
        }

        self.mass += 1.0;
        self.cx = (self.cx * (self.mass - 1.0) + px) / self.mass;
        self.cy = (self.cy * (self.mass - 1.0) + py) / self.mass;
        self.insert_into_child(px, py);
    }

    fn insert_into_child(&mut self, px: f64, py: f64) {
        let half = self.size / 2.0;
        let mid_x = self.min_x + half;
        let mid_y = self.min_y + half;
        let idx = if px < mid_x { 0 } else { 1 } + if py < mid_y { 0 } else { 2 };
        let child_min_x = if px < mid_x { self.min_x } else { mid_x };
        let child_min_y = if py < mid_y { self.min_y } else { mid_y };

        if self.children[idx].is_none() {
            self.children[idx] = Some(Box::new(QuadTree::new(child_min_x, child_min_y, half)));
        }
        self.children[idx].as_mut().unwrap().insert(px, py);
    }

    fn compute_force(&self, px: f64, py: f64, k: f64, theta: f64, fx: &mut f64, fy: &mut f64) {
        if self.mass == 0.0 { return; }

        let ddx = px - self.cx;
        let ddy = py - self.cy;
        let dist_sq = ddx * ddx + ddy * ddy;

        if self.is_leaf || self.size / dist_sq.sqrt().max(0.001) < theta {
            if dist_sq < 1e-8 { return; }
            let d = dist_sq.sqrt();
            let force = k * k * self.mass / d;
            *fx += ddx / d * force;
            *fy += ddy / d * force;
            return;
        }

        for child in &self.children {
            if let Some(c) = child {
                c.compute_force(px, py, k, theta, fx, fy);
            }
        }
    }
}

fn barnes_hut_repulsion(x: &[f64], y: &[f64], n: usize, k: f64, dx: &mut [f64], dy: &mut [f64]) {
    let mut min_x = f64::MAX;
    let mut max_x = f64::MIN;
    let mut min_y = f64::MAX;
    let mut max_y = f64::MIN;
    for i in 0..n {
        if x[i] < min_x { min_x = x[i]; }
        if x[i] > max_x { max_x = x[i]; }
        if y[i] < min_y { min_y = y[i]; }
        if y[i] > max_y { max_y = y[i]; }
    }
    let size = (max_x - min_x).max(max_y - min_y).max(1.0) * 1.01;

    let mut tree = QuadTree::new(min_x - 0.005 * size, min_y - 0.005 * size, size);
    for i in 0..n {
        tree.insert(x[i], y[i]);
    }

    let theta = 1.2;
    for i in 0..n {
        let mut fx = 0.0;
        let mut fy = 0.0;
        tree.compute_force(x[i], y[i], k, theta, &mut fx, &mut fy);
        dx[i] += fx;
        dy[i] += fy;
    }
}

fn layout_multilevel(adj: &[Vec<usize>], n: usize) -> Vec<(f64, f64)> {
    if n <= 5000 {
        return layout_force_directed(adj, n);
    }

    let mut matched = vec![false; n];
    let mut coarse_id = vec![0usize; n];
    let mut coarse_n = 0usize;

    for i in 0..n {
        if matched[i] { continue; }
        let mut best = usize::MAX;
        let mut best_weight = 0;
        for &nb in &adj[i] {
            if !matched[nb] && adj[nb].len() > best_weight {
                best = nb;
                best_weight = adj[nb].len();
            }
        }
        if best != usize::MAX {
            coarse_id[i] = coarse_n;
            coarse_id[best] = coarse_n;
            matched[i] = true;
            matched[best] = true;
        } else {
            coarse_id[i] = coarse_n;
            matched[i] = true;
        }
        coarse_n += 1;
    }

    let mut coarse_adj: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![vec![]; coarse_n];
    for i in 0..n {
        for &nb in &adj[i] {
            let ci = coarse_id[i];
            let cj = coarse_id[nb];
            if ci != cj {
                coarse_adj[ci].push(cj);
            }
        }
    }
    for i in 0..coarse_n {
        coarse_adj[i].sort_unstable();
        coarse_adj[i].dedup();
    }

    let coarse_positions = layout_multilevel(&coarse_adj, coarse_n);

    let k = (n as f64).sqrt();
    let mut rng_state: u64 = 67890;
    let mut next_rand = || -> f64 {
        rng_state = rng_state.wrapping_mul(6364136223846793005).wrapping_add(1442695040888963407);
        ((rng_state >> 33) as f64) / (2147483648.0) - 0.5
    };

    let mut x = vec![0.0f64; n];
    let mut y = vec![0.0f64; n];
    for i in 0..n {
        let (cx, cy) = coarse_positions[coarse_id[i]];
        x[i] = cx + next_rand() * k * 0.05;
        y[i] = cy + next_rand() * k * 0.05;
    }

    let iterations = 50;
    let mut temp = k * 0.5;
    let cooling = 0.9;

    for _iter in 0..iterations {
        let mut dx = vec![0.0f64; n];
        let mut dy = vec![0.0f64; n];

        barnes_hut_repulsion(&x, &y, n, k, &mut dx, &mut dy);

        for i in 0..n {
            for &j in &adj[i] {
                if j > i { continue; }
                let ddx = x[i] - x[j];
                let ddy = y[i] - y[j];
                let d = (ddx * ddx + ddy * ddy).sqrt().max(0.01);
                let force = d / k;
                let fx = ddx / d * force;
                let fy = ddy / d * force;
                dx[i] -= fx;
                dy[i] -= fy;
                dx[j] += fx;
                dy[j] += fy;
            }
        }

        for i in 0..n {
            let disp = (dx[i] * dx[i] + dy[i] * dy[i]).sqrt().max(0.0001);
            let scale = temp.min(disp) / disp;
            x[i] += dx[i] * scale;
            y[i] += dy[i] * scale;
        }

        temp *= cooling;
    }

    (0..n).map(|i| (x[i], y[i])).collect()
}

Test 4: Regular Tetrahedron Packing in Polyhedra (C++) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a 3D Packing problem: Pack regular tetrahedrons into polyhedra (C++).

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY container size from trivial to ludicrous scale.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: V edge_length
Lines 2..V+1: x y z (container vertices)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: K (number of tetrahedrons placed)
Lines 2..K+1: cx cy cz rx ry rz (center + Euler angles in radians)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on container volume and complexity
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for massive containers
3. **Quality**: Maximize number of tetrahedrons while ensuring no overlaps

**Key Geometry (edge length e=1.0):**
- Height: h = e * sqrt(2/3) ≈ 0.8165
- Circumradius: R = e * sqrt(3/8) ≈ 0.6124
- Inradius: r = e / sqrt(24) ≈ 0.2041

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.

Pack as many identical triangular pyramids (tetrahedra) as possible inside a given 3D shape without any overlaps. Unlike cubes, tetrahedra cannot tile space perfectly, so clever placement and rotation are essential.

The AI must handle 3D collision detection and geometry. Subpasses use increasingly complex container shapes. The baseline uses a simple grid placement strategy.

 0.0000
[small_cube] Packed: 1 legal tetrahedrons (of 8 total placements), Baseline: 34, Expected min: 1, Time: 0.1s - minimal (1 tetrahedrons)

Tetrahedron Packing - small_cube (8 vertices)

View Code (8172 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <cmath>
#include <algorithm>
#include <array>
#include <chrono>

using namespace std;

struct Vec3 {
    double x, y, z;
    Vec3() : x(0), y(0), z(0) {}
    Vec3(double a, double b, double c) : x(a), y(b), z(c) {}
    Vec3 operator+(const Vec3& o) const { return {x+o.x, y+o.y, z+o.z}; }
    Vec3 operator-(const Vec3& o) const { return {x-o.x, y-o.y, z-o.z}; }
    Vec3 operator*(double s) const { return {x*s, y*s, z*s}; }
    double dot(const Vec3& o) const { return x*o.x + y*o.y + z*o.z; }
    Vec3 cross(const Vec3& o) const {
        return {y*o.z - z*o.y, z*o.x - x*o.z, x*o.y - y*o.x};
    }
    double norm() const { return sqrt(x*x + y*y + z*z); }
    Vec3 normalized() const { double n = norm(); return n>1e-15 ? Vec3{x/n,y/n,z/n} : Vec3{0,0,0}; }
};

struct Mat3 {
    double m[3][3];
    Vec3 operator*(const Vec3& v) const {
        return {m[0][0]*v.x+m[0][1]*v.y+m[0][2]*v.z,
                m[1][0]*v.x+m[1][1]*v.y+m[1][2]*v.z,
                m[2][0]*v.x+m[2][1]*v.y+m[2][2]*v.z};
    }
};

Mat3 eulerToMat(double rx, double ry, double rz) {
    double cx=cos(rx),sx=sin(rx),cy=cos(ry),sy=sin(ry),cz=cos(rz),sz=sin(rz);
    Mat3 r;
    r.m[0][0]=cy*cz; r.m[0][1]=-cy*sz; r.m[0][2]=sy;
    r.m[1][0]=sx*sy*cz+cx*sz; r.m[1][1]=-sx*sy*sz+cx*cz; r.m[1][2]=-sx*cy;
    r.m[2][0]=-cx*sy*cz+sx*sz; r.m[2][1]=cx*sy*sz+sx*cz; r.m[2][2]=cx*cy;
    return r;
}

array<Vec3,4> baseTetraVerts() {
    double s = 1.0/sqrt(8.0);
    return {Vec3(s,s,s), Vec3(s,-s,-s), Vec3(-s,s,-s), Vec3(-s,-s,s)};
}

array<Vec3,4> getTetraVerts(Vec3 center, double rx, double ry, double rz) {
    static auto base = baseTetraVerts();
    Mat3 rot = eulerToMat(rx,ry,rz);
    array<Vec3,4> v;
    for(int i=0;i<4;i++) v[i] = center + rot*base[i];
    return v;
}

struct Plane { Vec3 normal; double d; };

vector<Plane> containerPlanes;

void buildConvexHullPlanes(const vector<Vec3>& verts) {
    containerPlanes.clear();
    int n=(int)verts.size();
    if(n<4) return;
    for(int i=0;i<n;i++) {
        for(int j=i+1;j<n;j++) {
            for(int k=j+1;k<n;k++) {
                Vec3 e1=verts[j]-verts[i], e2=verts[k]-verts[i];
                Vec3 nm=e1.cross(e2);
                double len=nm.norm();
                if(len<1e-12) continue;
                nm=nm*(1.0/len);
                double d=nm.dot(verts[i]);
                bool allBelow=true, allAbove=true;
                for(int l=0;l<n;l++) {
                    if(l==i||l==j||l==k) continue;
                    double val=nm.dot(verts[l])-d;
                    if(val>1e-9) allBelow=false;
                    if(val<-1e-9) allAbove=false;
                }
                if(allBelow) containerPlanes.push_back({nm,d});
                else if(allAbove) containerPlanes.push_back({nm*-1.0,-d});
            }
        }
    }
    vector<Plane> unique;
    for(auto& p:containerPlanes) {
        bool dup=false;
        for(auto& q:unique)
            if(abs(p.normal.dot(q.normal)-1.0)<1e-6 && abs(p.d-q.d)<1e-6) {dup=true;break;}
        if(!dup) unique.push_back(p);
    }
    containerPlanes=unique;
}

bool pointInContainer(const Vec3& p) {
    for(auto& pl:containerPlanes)
        if(pl.normal.dot(p)>pl.d+1e-9) return false;
    return true;
}

bool tetraInContainer(const array<Vec3,4>& v) {
    for(auto& p:v) if(!pointInContainer(p)) return false;
    return true;
}

array<double,3> findEulerAngles(const array<Vec3,4>& target) {
    auto base=baseTetraVerts();
    double B[3][3]={{base[0].x,base[1].x,base[2].x},{base[0].y,base[1].y,base[2].y},{base[0].z,base[1].z,base[2].z}};
    double A[3][3]={{target[0].x,target[1].x,target[2].x},{target[0].y,target[1].y,target[2].y},{target[0].z,target[1].z,target[2].z}};
    double det=B[0][0]*(B[1][1]*B[2][2]-B[1][2]*B[2][1])-B[0][1]*(B[1][0]*B[2][2]-B[1][2]*B[2][0])+B[0][2]*(B[1][0]*B[2][1]-B[1][1]*B[2][0]);
    if(abs(det)<1e-15) return{0,0,0};
    double Bi[3][3];
    Bi[0][0]=(B[1][1]*B[2][2]-B[1][2]*B[2][1])/det; Bi[0][1]=(B[0][2]*B[2][1]-B[0][1]*B[2][2])/det; Bi[0][2]=(B[0][1]*B[1][2]-B[0][2]*B[1][1])/det;
    Bi[1][0]=(B[1][2]*B[2][0]-B[1][0]*B[2][2])/det; Bi[1][1]=(B[0][0]*B[2][2]-B[0][2]*B[2][0])/det; Bi[1][2]=(B[0][2]*B[1][0]-B[0][0]*B[1][2])/det;
    Bi[2][0]=(B[1][0]*B[2][1]-B[1][1]*B[2][0])/det; Bi[2][1]=(B[0][1]*B[2][0]-B[0][0]*B[2][1])/det; Bi[2][2]=(B[0][0]*B[1][1]-B[0][1]*B[1][0])/det;
    double R[3][3];
    for(int i=0;i<3;i++) for(int j=0;j<3;j++){R[i][j]=0;for(int k=0;k<3;k++) R[i][j]+=A[i][k]*Bi[k][j];}
    double ry=asin(max(-1.0,min(1.0,R[0][2])));
    double cy2=cos(ry);
    double rx,rz;
    if(abs(cy2)>1e-6){rx=atan2(-R[1][2],R[2][2]);rz=atan2(-R[0][1],R[0][0]);}
    else{rx=atan2(R[2][1],R[1][1]);rz=0;}
    return{rx,ry,rz};
}

int main(){
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);
    auto t0=chrono::steady_clock::now();
    auto elapsed=[&](){return chrono::duration<double>(chrono::steady_clock::now()-t0).count();};

    int V; double edgeLen;
    cin>>V>>edgeLen;
    vector<Vec3> verts(V);
    for(int i=0;i<V;i++) cin>>verts[i].x>>verts[i].y>>verts[i].z;
    for(auto& v:verts) v=v*(1.0/edgeLen);

    buildConvexHullPlanes(verts);

    Vec3 lo(1e18,1e18,1e18), hi(-1e18,-1e18,-1e18);
    for(auto& v:verts){
        lo.x=min(lo.x,v.x);lo.y=min(lo.y,v.y);lo.z=min(lo.z,v.z);
        hi.x=max(hi.x,v.x);hi.y=max(hi.y,v.y);hi.z=max(hi.z,v.z);
    }

    double a=sqrt(2.0); // FCC lattice constant for unit edge tetrahedra

    // FCC tetrahedra: 8 per unit cell
    // Up-type centroids: (a/4,a/4,a/4), (3a/4,3a/4,a/4), (3a/4,a/4,3a/4), (a/4,3a/4,3a/4)
    // Down-type centroids: (3a/4,a/4,a/4), (a/4,3a/4,a/4), (a/4,a/4,3a/4), (3a/4,3a/4,3a/4)

    // Up tetra relative vertices (centered at origin):
    double q=a/4.0;
    array<Vec3,4> upRel={Vec3(-q,-q,-q),Vec3(q,q,-q),Vec3(q,-q,q),Vec3(-q,q,q)};
    // Down tetra: e.g. centroid at (3a/4,a/4,a/4), vertices (a/2,a/2,0),(a,0,0),(a,a/2,a/2),(a/2,0,a/2)
    // Relative: (-q,q,-q),(q,-q,-q),(q,q,q),(-q,-q,q)
    array<Vec3,4> downRel={Vec3(-q,q,-q),Vec3(q,-q,-q),Vec3(q,q,q),Vec3(-q,-q,q)};

    auto upAng=findEulerAngles(upRel);
    auto downAng=findEulerAngles(downRel);

    struct Off{double ox,oy,oz;int t;};
    vector<Off> offsets={
        {q,q,q,0},{3*q,3*q,q,0},{3*q,q,3*q,0},{q,3*q,3*q,0},
        {3*q,q,q,1},{q,3*q,q,1},{q,q,3*q,1},{3*q,3*q,3*q,1}
    };

    int ni=(int)ceil((hi.x-lo.x)/a)+1;
    int nj=(int)ceil((hi.y-lo.y)/a)+1;
    int nk=(int)ceil((hi.z-lo.z)/a)+1;

    // Cap grid
    long long gs=(long long)ni*nj*nk*8;
    if(gs>30000000){
        double sc=cbrt((double)gs/30000000.0);
        ni=max(1,(int)(ni/sc));nj=max(1,(int)(nj/sc));nk=max(1,(int)(nk/sc));
    }

    struct Result{Vec3 c;double rx,ry,rz;};

    auto tryShift=[&](double sx,double sy,double sz)->vector<Result>{
        vector<Result> res;
        for(int k=-1;k<=nk&&elapsed()<24.0;k++){
            for(int j=-1;j<=nj;j++){
                for(int i=-1;i<=ni;i++){
                    for(auto& off:offsets){
                        Vec3 c(lo.x+i*a+off.ox+sx, lo.y+j*a+off.oy+sy, lo.z+k*a+off.oz+sz);
                        auto& ang=(off.t==0)?upAng:downAng;
                        auto v=getTetraVerts(c,ang[0],ang[1],ang[2]);
                        if(tetraInContainer(v)) res.push_back({c,ang[0],ang[1],ang[2]});
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return res;
    };

    auto best=tryShift(0,0,0);

    // Try some shifted lattices to find better packing
    if(elapsed()<18.0){
        double step=a/4.0;
        for(int si=0;si<4&&elapsed()<18.0;si++){
            for(int sj=0;sj<4&&elapsed()<18.0;sj++){
                for(int sk=0;sk<4&&elapsed()<18.0;sk++){
                    if(si==0&&sj==0&&sk==0) continue;
                    auto trial=tryShift(si*step,sj*step,sk*step);
                    if(trial.size()>best.size()) best=trial;
                }
            }
        }
    }

    cout<<best.size()<<"\n";
    cout<<fixed;
    cout.precision(10);
    for(auto& r:best){
        cout<<r.c.x*edgeLen<<" "<<r.c.y*edgeLen<<" "<<r.c.z*edgeLen
            <<" "<<r.rx<<" "<<r.ry<<" "<<r.rz<<"\n";
    }
    return 0;
}

Test 5: Hamiltonian Path on Grid with Obstacles (Rust) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a Hamiltonian Path problem on 2D grids with obstacles in Rust.

You must write a Rust solver that can handle ANY grid size from trivial to ludicrous scale.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: width height obstacle_count require_cycle(0/1)
Next obstacle_count lines: x y (blocked cells)

**Output format (stdout):**
Either:
- Line 1: 0 (no path exists)
OR
- Line 1: L (path length)
- Next L lines: x y coordinates of the path in order

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on grid size and obstacle density
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for 2500 cell grids
3. **Correctness**: Must find valid paths or correctly report impossibility

**Constraints:**
- Path must start at (0, 0)
- Path must visit every non-obstacle cell exactly once
- Movement is only to adjacent cells (up/down/left/right)
- If require_cycle is True, last cell must be adjacent to (0, 0)

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Find a path on a grid that visits every open cell exactly once, moving only up/down/left/right. Some cells are blocked by obstacles. Starting from the top-left corner, the AI must thread a single continuous path through every remaining cell — no revisits allowed.

This is an NP-complete problem, so brute-force search blows up quickly. Subpasses increase the grid size and obstacle count. The baseline uses a simple depth-first search.

 0.0000
[4x4, 1 obstacles] Compilation failed:
error: expected identifier, found reserved keyword `gen`
--> C:\Users\Ash\AppData\Local\Temp\tmpagl4m0cy.rs:209:13

209 | let mut gen = 0u32;
| ^^^ expe

Hamiltonian Path - 4x4 grid, 1 obstacles

View Code (11931 chars)
use std::io::{self, Read, Write, BufWriter};
use std::collections::VecDeque;
use std::time::{Instant, Duration};

const DX: [i32; 4] = [1, -1, 0, 0];
const DY: [i32; 4] = [0, 0, 1, -1];

fn main() {
    let mut input = String::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut input).unwrap();
    let out = io::stdout();
    let mut out = BufWriter::new(out.lock());

    let mut it = input.split_whitespace();
    let w: usize = p(&mut it);
    let h: usize = p(&mut it);
    let nobs: usize = p(&mut it);
    let cyc: bool = p::<u8>(&mut it) != 0;

    if w == 0 || h == 0 {
        writeln!(out, "0").unwrap();
        return;
    }

    let n = w * h;
    let mut blocked = vec![false; n];
    for _ in 0..nobs {
        let x: usize = p(&mut it);
        let y: usize = p(&mut it);
        blocked[y * w + x] = true;
    }

    if blocked[0] {
        writeln!(out, "0").unwrap();
        return;
    }

    let total = n - nobs;

    if total <= 1 {
        if total == 1 && !cyc {
            writeln!(out, "1").unwrap();
            writeln!(out, "0 0").unwrap();
        } else {
            writeln!(out, "0").unwrap();
        }
        return;
    }

    if !connected(&blocked, w, h, total) {
        writeln!(out, "0").unwrap();
        return;
    }

    let (ec, oc) = parity(&blocked, w, h);
    if cyc {
        if total % 2 != 0 || ec != oc {
            writeln!(out, "0").unwrap();
            return;
        }
    } else if ec != (total + 1) / 2 || oc != total / 2 {
        writeln!(out, "0").unwrap();
        return;
    }

    if nobs == 0 {
        if !cyc {
            emit(&mut out, &snake(w, h));
        } else if let Some(c) = make_cycle(w, h) {
            emit(&mut out, &c);
        } else {
            writeln!(out, "0").unwrap();
        }
        return;
    }

    let t0 = Instant::now();
    let lim = Duration::from_secs(270);

    if total <= 100 {
        if let Some(path) = bt_solve(w, h, &blocked, total, cyc, t0, lim) {
            emit(&mut out, &path);
            return;
        }
        if t0.elapsed() < lim {
            writeln!(out, "0").unwrap();
            return;
        }
    }

    if let Some(path) = warnsdorff_multi(w, h, &blocked, total, cyc, t0, lim) {
        emit(&mut out, &path);
        return;
    }

    if total > 100 && total <= 300 && t0.elapsed() < Duration::from_secs(250) {
        if let Some(path) = bt_solve(w, h, &blocked, total, cyc, t0, Duration::from_secs(285)) {
            emit(&mut out, &path);
            return;
        }
    }

    writeln!(out, "0").unwrap();
}

fn p<T: std::str::FromStr>(it: &mut std::str::SplitWhitespace) -> T
where T::Err: std::fmt::Debug {
    it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap()
}

fn connected(blocked: &[bool], w: usize, h: usize, total: usize) -> bool {
    let mut seen = vec![false; w * h];
    seen[0] = true;
    let mut q = VecDeque::new();
    q.push_back(0usize);
    let mut cnt = 1usize;
    while let Some(pos) = q.pop_front() {
        let (x, y) = (pos % w, pos / w);
        for d in 0..4 {
            let nx = x as i32 + DX[d];
            let ny = y as i32 + DY[d];
            if nx >= 0 && nx < w as i32 && ny >= 0 && ny < h as i32 {
                let np = ny as usize * w + nx as usize;
                if !blocked[np] && !seen[np] {
                    seen[np] = true;
                    cnt += 1;
                    q.push_back(np);
                }
            }
        }
    }
    cnt == total
}

fn parity(blocked: &[bool], w: usize, h: usize) -> (usize, usize) {
    let (mut e, mut o) = (0, 0);
    for y in 0..h {
        for x in 0..w {
            if !blocked[y * w + x] {
                if (x + y) % 2 == 0 { e += 1; } else { o += 1; }
            }
        }
    }
    (e, o)
}

fn snake(w: usize, h: usize) -> Vec<(usize, usize)> {
    let mut v = Vec::with_capacity(w * h);
    for y in 0..h {
        if y % 2 == 0 {
            for x in 0..w { v.push((x, y)); }
        } else {
            for x in (0..w).rev() { v.push((x, y)); }
        }
    }
    v
}

fn make_cycle(w: usize, h: usize) -> Option<Vec<(usize, usize)>> {
    let n = w * h;
    if n % 2 != 0 { return None; }
    if n < 2 { return None; }
    if n == 2 { return Some(snake(w, h)); }
    if w < 2 || h < 2 { return None; }

    let mut v = Vec::with_capacity(n);
    if w % 2 == 0 {
        for y in 0..h { v.push((0, y)); }
        for x in 1..w { v.push((x, h - 1)); }
        let mut up = true;
        for c in (1..w).rev() {
            if up {
                for y in (0..h - 1).rev() { v.push((c, y)); }
            } else {
                for y in 0..h - 1 { v.push((c, y)); }
            }
            up = !up;
        }
    } else {
        for x in 0..w { v.push((x, 0)); }
        for y in 1..h { v.push((w - 1, y)); }
        let mut left = true;
        for r in (1..h).rev() {
            if left {
                for x in (0..w - 1).rev() { v.push((x, r)); }
            } else {
                for x in 0..w - 1 { v.push((x, r)); }
            }
            left = !left;
        }
    }
    Some(v)
}

fn emit(out: &mut impl Write, path: &[(usize, usize)]) {
    writeln!(out, "{}", path.len()).unwrap();
    for &(x, y) in path {
        writeln!(out, "{} {}", x, y).unwrap();
    }
}

fn bt_solve(w: usize, h: usize, blocked: &[bool], total: usize, cyc: bool,
            t0: Instant, lim: Duration) -> Option<Vec<(usize, usize)>> {
    let mut vis = blocked.to_vec();
    vis[0] = true;
    let mut path = vec![(0usize, 0usize)];
    let mut ck = 0u64;
    let mut scratch = vec![0u32; w * h];
    let mut gen = 0u32;
    let mut stk_buf = Vec::with_capacity(w * h);
    if bt_rec(w, h, &mut vis, &mut path, total, cyc, t0, lim, &mut ck,
              &mut scratch, &mut gen, &mut stk_buf) {
        Some(path)
    } else {
        None
    }
}

fn bt_rec(w: usize, h: usize, vis: &mut Vec<bool>, path: &mut Vec<(usize, usize)>,
          total: usize, cyc: bool, t0: Instant, lim: Duration, ck: &mut u64,
          scratch: &mut Vec<u32>, gen: &mut u32, stk_buf: &mut Vec<usize>) -> bool {
    *ck += 1;
    if *ck & 0xFFFF == 0 && t0.elapsed() > lim { return false; }

    let (cx, cy) = *path.last().unwrap();
    let rem = total - path.len();

    if rem == 0 {
        return !cyc || cx + cy == 1;
    }

    let mut nbrs = [(0usize, 0usize, 0usize); 4];
    let mut nc = 0;
    for d in 0..4 {
        let nx = cx as i32 + DX[d];
        let ny = cy as i32 + DY[d];
        if nx >= 0 && nx < w as i32 && ny >= 0 && ny < h as i32 {
            let (ux, uy) = (nx as usize, ny as usize);
            if !vis[uy * w + ux] {
                let deg = udeg(vis, w, h, ux, uy);
                nbrs[nc] = (ux, uy, deg);
                nc += 1;
            }
        }
    }

    if nc == 0 { return false; }

    nbrs[..nc].sort_unstable_by_key(|t| t.2);

    for i in 0..nc {
        let (nx, ny, _) = nbrs[i];
        let np = ny * w + nx;
        vis[np] = true;
        path.push((nx, ny));

        let ok = prune_ok(vis, w, h, nx, ny, rem - 1, cyc, scratch, gen, stk_buf);

        if ok && bt_rec(w, h, vis, path, total, cyc, t0, lim, ck, scratch, gen, stk_buf) {
            return true;
        }

        path.pop();
        vis[np] = false;
    }

    false
}

fn udeg(vis: &[bool], w: usize, h: usize, x: usize, y: usize) -> usize {
    let mut d = 0;
    for i in 0..4 {
        let nx = x as i32 + DX[i];
        let ny = y as i32 + DY[i];
        if nx >= 0 && nx < w as i32 && ny >= 0 && ny < h as i32 {
            if !vis[ny as usize * w + nx as usize] { d += 1; }
        }
    }
    d
}

fn prune_ok(vis: &[bool], w: usize, h: usize, cx: usize, cy: usize, rem: usize, cyc: bool,
            scratch: &mut Vec<u32>, gen: &mut u32, stk_buf: &mut Vec<usize>) -> bool {
    if rem == 0 { return true; }

    for d in 0..4 {
        let nx = cx as i32 + DX[d];
        let ny = cy as i32 + DY[d];
        if nx >= 0 && nx < w as i32 && ny >= 0 && ny < h as i32 {
            let np = ny as usize * w + nx as usize;
            if !vis[np] && udeg(vis, w, h, nx as usize, ny as usize) == 0 && rem > 1 {
                return false;
            }
        }
    }

    if cyc {
        let mut adj = 0;
        if w > 1 && !vis[1] { adj += 1; }
        if h > 1 && !vis[w] { adj += 1; }
        if adj == 0 { return false; }
    }

    if rem <= 80 {
        return conn_from(vis, w, h, cx, cy, rem, scratch, gen, stk_buf);
    }

    true
}

fn conn_from(vis: &[bool], w: usize, h: usize, sx: usize, sy: usize, expected: usize,
             scratch: &mut Vec<u32>, gen: &mut u32, stk: &mut Vec<usize>) -> bool {
    if expected == 0 { return true; }
    *gen = gen.wrapping_add(1);
    if *gen == 0 {
        scratch.iter_mut().for_each(|v| *v = 0);
        *gen = 1;
    }
    let g = *gen;

    let mut start = usize::MAX;
    for d in 0..4 {
        let nx = sx as i32 + DX[d];
        let ny = sy as i32 + DY[d];
        if nx >= 0 && nx < w as i32 && ny >= 0 && ny < h as i32 {
            let np = ny as usize * w + nx as usize;
            if !vis[np] { start = np; break; }
        }
    }
    if start == usize::MAX { return false; }

    scratch[start] = g;
    stk.clear();
    stk.push(start);
    let mut cnt = 1usize;
    while let Some(pos) = stk.pop() {
        let (px, py) = (pos % w, pos / w);
        for d in 0..4 {
            let nx = px as i32 + DX[d];
            let ny = py as i32 + DY[d];
            if nx >= 0 && nx < w as i32 && ny >= 0 && ny < h as i32 {
                let np = ny as usize * w + nx as usize;
                if !vis[np] && scratch[np] != g {
                    scratch[np] = g;
                    cnt += 1;
                    if cnt == expected { return true; }
                    stk.push(np);
                }
            }
        }
    }
    cnt == expected
}

struct Rng(u64);
impl Rng {
    fn new(s: u64) -> Self { Rng(s | 1) }
    fn next(&mut self) -> u64 {
        self.0 ^= self.0 << 13;
        self.0 ^= self.0 >> 7;
        self.0 ^= self.0 << 17;
        self.0
    }
    fn upto(&mut self, n: usize) -> usize { (self.next() % n as u64) as usize }
}

fn warnsdorff_multi(w: usize, h: usize, blocked: &[bool], total: usize, cyc: bool,
                     t0: Instant, lim: Duration) -> Option<Vec<(usize, usize)>> {
    let mut rng = Rng::new(0xCAFE_BABE_DEAD_BEEF);
    loop {
        if t0.elapsed() > lim { return None; }
        if let Some(p) = w_once(w, h, blocked, total, cyc, &mut rng) { return Some(p); }
    }
}

fn w_once(w: usize, h: usize, blocked: &[bool], total: usize, cyc: bool,
          rng: &mut Rng) -> Option<Vec<(usize, usize)>> {
    let mut vis = blocked.to_vec();
    vis[0] = true;
    let mut path = Vec::with_capacity(total);
    path.push((0, 0));
    let (mut x, mut y) = (0usize, 0usize);

    for step in 1..total {
        let mut best = usize::MAX;
        let mut cands = [(0usize, 0usize); 4];
        let mut cc = 0usize;

        for d in 0..4 {
            let nx = x as i32 + DX[d];
            let ny = y as i32 + DY[d];
            if nx >= 0 && nx < w as i32 && ny >= 0 && ny < h as i32 {
                let (ux, uy) = (nx as usize, ny as usize);
                if !vis[uy * w + ux] {
                    let mut deg = udeg(&vis, w, h, ux, uy);
                    if cyc && step < total - 1 && ux + uy == 1 { deg += 100; }

                    if deg < best {
                        best = deg;
                        cc = 1;
                        cands[0] = (ux, uy);
                    } else if deg == best && cc < 4 {
                        cands[cc] = (ux, uy);
                        cc += 1;
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        if cc == 0 { return None; }
        let (nx, ny) = cands[if cc == 1 { 0 } else { rng.upto(cc) }];
        vis[ny * w + nx] = true;
        path.push((nx, ny));
        x = nx;
        y = ny;
    }

    if cyc && x + y != 1 { return None; }
    Some(path)
}

Test 6: Orbital TSP - Space Station Route Planning (C#) Score: 7.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving an Orbital Travelling Salesman Problem in C#.

You must write a C# solver that can handle ANY number of stations from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 2-5 stations (simple brute-force permutations feasible)
- **Medium**: 6-10 stations (requires heuristics, some optimization)
- **Large**: 15-20 stations (complex orbital mechanics, needs efficient algorithms)
- **Extreme**: 50-100 stations (very complex, requires advanced heuristics)

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with 2 to 100 stations. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N mu
Line 2: start orbit: x y z vx vy vz
Lines 3..N+2: station orbits: x y z vx vy vz

**Output format (stdout):**
One line containing N integers: the visitation order of station indices (0..N-1).


**Starting position (epoch T=0):**
Position: (7197.9, 3733.4, -6152.3) km
Velocity: (4.567, -4.577, -1.601) km/s
Altitude: ~3807 km

**Physics constants:**
- Earth gravitational parameter μ = 398600.4418 km³/s²
- Earth radius = 6371 km

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on number of stations
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for 100 stations
3. **Accuracy**: Should provide reasonable delta-V estimates

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Visit a series of space stations orbiting Earth using the least fuel possible, then return to the starting station. Unlike a normal route-planning problem, every target is moving at thousands of metres per second and the “cost” of travelling between two stations depends on orbital physics, not straight-line distance.

The AI must combine route optimisation with orbital mechanics. Subpasses add more stations and trickier orbit geometries. The baseline uses a simple nearest-neighbour heuristic with approximate fuel estimates.

 1.0000
[2 stations] Order: [0, 1], Delta-V: 14.30 km/s, Baseline: 14.25 km/s, Time: 0.1s - excellent (≤ baseline)

Orbital TSP - 2 stations

View Code (9833 chars)
using System;
using System.Diagnostics;
using System.Globalization;

class Program
{
    static double MU;

    static void Main()
    {
        var ci = CultureInfo.InvariantCulture;
        var sw = Stopwatch.StartNew();

        string[] parts = Console.ReadLine().Trim().Split(new[] { ' ', '\t' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
        int N = int.Parse(parts[0]);
        MU = double.Parse(parts[1], ci);

        double[] startState = ReadState(ci);
        double[][] stationStates = new double[N][];
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++)
            stationStates[i] = ReadState(ci);

        if (N == 1) { Console.WriteLine("0"); return; }

        double[] startOE = ToOE(startState);
        double[][] oe = new double[N][];
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++)
            oe[i] = ToOE(stationStates[i]);

        double[] fromStart = new double[N];
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++)
            fromStart[i] = TransferCost(startOE, oe[i]);

        double[,] cost = new double[N, N];
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++)
            for (int j = 0; j < N; j++)
                if (i != j)
                    cost[i, j] = TransferCost(oe[i], oe[j]);

        int[] bestPerm;
        if (N <= 10)
            bestPerm = BruteForce(fromStart, cost, N);
        else
            bestPerm = IteratedLocalSearch(fromStart, cost, N, sw, 240000);

        Console.WriteLine(string.Join(" ", bestPerm));
    }

    static double[] ReadState(CultureInfo ci)
    {
        string[] p = Console.ReadLine().Trim().Split(new[] { ' ', '\t' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
        double[] r = new double[6];
        for (int i = 0; i < 6; i++) r[i] = double.Parse(p[i], ci);
        return r;
    }

    static double Clamp(double v) { return v < -1.0 ? -1.0 : (v > 1.0 ? 1.0 : v); }

    static double[] ToOE(double[] s)
    {
        double x = s[0], y = s[1], z = s[2], vx = s[3], vy = s[4], vz = s[5];
        double r = Math.Sqrt(x * x + y * y + z * z);
        double v = Math.Sqrt(vx * vx + vy * vy + vz * vz);

        double hx = y * vz - z * vy, hy = z * vx - x * vz, hz = x * vy - y * vx;
        double h = Math.Sqrt(hx * hx + hy * hy + hz * hz);

        double nx = -hy, ny = hx;
        double nmag = Math.Sqrt(nx * nx + ny * ny);

        double rdv = x * vx + y * vy + z * vz;
        double c = v * v - MU / r;
        double ex = (c * x - rdv * vx) / MU;
        double ey = (c * y - rdv * vy) / MU;
        double ez = (c * z - rdv * vz) / MU;
        double e = Math.Sqrt(ex * ex + ey * ey + ez * ez);

        double energy = v * v / 2.0 - MU / r;
        double a;
        if (Math.Abs(e - 1.0) > 1e-8)
            a = -MU / (2.0 * energy);
        else
            a = h * h / MU;

        double inc = Math.Acos(Clamp(hz / h));

        double raan = 0;
        if (nmag > 1e-10)
        {
            raan = Math.Acos(Clamp(nx / nmag));
            if (ny < 0) raan = 2 * Math.PI - raan;
        }

        double argp = 0;
        if (nmag > 1e-10 && e > 1e-10)
        {
            argp = Math.Acos(Clamp((nx * ex + ny * ey) / (nmag * e)));
            if (ez < 0) argp = 2 * Math.PI - argp;
        }

        return new double[] { a, e, inc, raan, argp };
    }

    static double TransferCost(double[] from, double[] to)
    {
        double a1 = from[0], e1 = from[1], i1 = from[2], raan1 = from[3];
        double a2 = to[0], e2 = to[1], i2 = to[2], raan2 = to[3];

        double absA1 = Math.Abs(a1);
        double absA2 = Math.Abs(a2);
        if (absA1 < 1.0) absA1 = 1.0;
        if (absA2 < 1.0) absA2 = 1.0;

        double v1 = Math.Sqrt(MU / absA1);
        double v2 = Math.Sqrt(MU / absA2);

        double dvA = Math.Abs(v1 - v2);

        double dRaan = raan1 - raan2;
        double cosP = Math.Cos(i1) * Math.Cos(i2) + Math.Sin(i1) * Math.Sin(i2) * Math.Cos(dRaan);
        double planeAngle = Math.Acos(Clamp(cosP));
        double vAvg = (v1 + v2) / 2.0;
        double dvP = 2.0 * vAvg * Math.Sin(planeAngle / 2.0);

        double dvE = Math.Abs(e1 - e2) * vAvg * 0.5;

        double dArgp = Math.Abs(from[4] - to[4]);
        if (dArgp > Math.PI) dArgp = 2 * Math.PI - dArgp;
        double dvW = dArgp / Math.PI * vAvg * Math.Max(e1, e2) * 0.2;

        return Math.Sqrt(dvA * dvA + dvP * dvP) + dvE + dvW;
    }

    static double PathCost(double[] fromStart, double[,] cost, int[] perm)
    {
        double total = fromStart[perm[0]];
        for (int i = 0; i < perm.Length - 1; i++)
            total += cost[perm[i], perm[i + 1]];
        return total;
    }

    static int[] BruteForce(double[] fromStart, double[,] cost, int N)
    {
        int[] perm = new int[N];
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) perm[i] = i;
        int[] best = (int[])perm.Clone();
        double bestCost = PathCost(fromStart, cost, perm);

        int[] c = new int[N];
        int idx = 0;
        while (idx < N)
        {
            if (c[idx] < idx)
            {
                int swapIdx = (idx % 2 == 0) ? 0 : c[idx];
                int tmp = perm[swapIdx];
                perm[swapIdx] = perm[idx];
                perm[idx] = tmp;

                double pc = PathCost(fromStart, cost, perm);
                if (pc < bestCost)
                {
                    bestCost = pc;
                    best = (int[])perm.Clone();
                }

                c[idx]++;
                idx = 0;
            }
            else
            {
                c[idx] = 0;
                idx++;
            }
        }
        return best;
    }

    static int[] NearestNeighbor(double[] fromStart, double[,] cost, int N, int firstStation)
    {
        int[] perm = new int[N];
        bool[] visited = new bool[N];
        perm[0] = firstStation;
        visited[firstStation] = true;

        for (int step = 1; step < N; step++)
        {
            int best = -1;
            double bc = double.MaxValue;
            for (int j = 0; j < N; j++)
            {
                if (!visited[j] && cost[perm[step - 1], j] < bc)
                {
                    bc = cost[perm[step - 1], j];
                    best = j;
                }
            }
            perm[step] = best;
            visited[best] = true;
        }
        return perm;
    }

    static void TwoOptTimed(double[] fromStart, double[,] cost, int[] perm, int N, Stopwatch sw, int timeLimit)
    {
        bool improved = true;
        while (improved && sw.ElapsedMilliseconds < timeLimit)
        {
            improved = false;
            for (int i = 0; i < N - 1; i++)
            {
                if (sw.ElapsedMilliseconds >= timeLimit) break;
                for (int j = i + 1; j < N; j++)
                {
                    double oldCost, newCost;

                    if (i == 0)
                    {
                        oldCost = fromStart[perm[0]];
                        newCost = fromStart[perm[j]];
                    }
                    else
                    {
                        oldCost = cost[perm[i - 1], perm[i]];
                        newCost = cost[perm[i - 1], perm[j]];
                    }

                    if (j + 1 < N)
                    {
                        oldCost += cost[perm[j], perm[j + 1]];
                        newCost += cost[perm[i], perm[j + 1]];
                    }

                    if (newCost < oldCost - 1e-12)
                    {
                        Array.Reverse(perm, i, j - i + 1);
                        improved = true;
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }

    static int[] IteratedLocalSearch(double[] fromStart, double[,] cost, int N, Stopwatch sw, int timeLimit)
    {
        int[] bestPerm = null;
        double bestCost = double.MaxValue;

        int maxStarts = N <= 30 ? N : 30;
        for (int s = 0; s < maxStarts && sw.ElapsedMilliseconds < timeLimit / 3; s++)
        {
            int[] perm = NearestNeighbor(fromStart, cost, N, s);
            TwoOptTimed(fromStart, cost, perm, N, sw, timeLimit);
            double pc = PathCost(fromStart, cost, perm);
            if (pc < bestCost)
            {
                bestCost = pc;
                bestPerm = (int[])perm.Clone();
            }
        }

        if (bestPerm == null)
        {
            bestPerm = new int[N];
            for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) bestPerm[i] = i;
        }

        var rng = new Random(42);
        while (sw.ElapsedMilliseconds < timeLimit)
        {
            int[] perm = (int[])bestPerm.Clone();

            if (N >= 8)
            {
                int c0, c1, c2;
                do
                {
                    c0 = rng.Next(1, N);
                    c1 = rng.Next(1, N);
                    c2 = rng.Next(1, N);
                } while (c0 == c1 || c1 == c2 || c0 == c2);

                if (c0 > c1) { int t = c0; c0 = c1; c1 = t; }
                if (c1 > c2) { int t = c1; c1 = c2; c2 = t; }
                if (c0 > c1) { int t = c0; c0 = c1; c1 = t; }

                int[] np = new int[N];
                int pos = 0;
                for (int i = 0; i < c0; i++) np[pos++] = perm[i];
                for (int i = c1; i < c2; i++) np[pos++] = perm[i];
                for (int i = c0; i < c1; i++) np[pos++] = perm[i];
                for (int i = c2; i < N; i++) np[pos++] = perm[i];
                perm = np;
            }
            else
            {
                int a = rng.Next(N), b = rng.Next(N);
                int tmp = perm[a]; perm[a] = perm[b]; perm[b] = tmp;
            }

            TwoOptTimed(fromStart, cost, perm, N, sw, timeLimit);
            double pc = PathCost(fromStart, cost, perm);
            if (pc < bestCost)
            {
                bestCost = pc;
                bestPerm = (int[])perm.Clone();
            }
        }

        return bestPerm;
    }
}
