Test 7: CSG Union of Two Polyhedra (C++) Score: 0.05 / 12
Typical Prompt:
You are implementing a CSG (Constructive Solid Geometry) Union operation in C++.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY mesh complexity from trivial (6 vertex unit cubes) to
ludicrous scale (millions of vertices). The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: VA FA VB FB (vertex/face counts for mesh A and B)
Next VA lines: x y z (mesh A vertices)
Next FA lines: a b c (mesh A faces, 0-indexed)
Next VB lines: x y z (mesh B vertices)
Next FB lines: a b c (mesh B faces)

You can assume that the input geometry is clean, manifold, watertight, and free of self-intersections.

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: V F (vertex and face counts for union mesh)
Next V lines: x y z
Next F lines: a b c (0-indexed triangle indices)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on mesh complexity
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for very complex meshes
3. **Correctness**: Must output correct union geometry. Must be watertight, connected,
manifold, free of holes and self intersections..

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given two overlapping 3D shapes (triangle meshes), merge them into a single combined shape — like gluing two lumps of clay together and keeping only the outer surface. The output mesh must be watertight with no self-intersections.

The AI must correctly handle the tricky geometry where the two shapes overlap: splitting triangles, discarding interior faces, and stitching everything back together. Subpasses use increasingly complex shape pairs.

 0.0500
[overlapping_boxes] Result vol: 8.00, Expected (MC): ~15.04, Inputs: 8.00 + 8.00, Time: 0.1s - poor (< 50% error). Topology: clean

CSG Union - overlapping_boxes

View Code (19121 chars)
#include <cstdio>
#include <cmath>
#include <vector>
#include <array>
#include <algorithm>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <numeric>

using namespace std;

const double EPS = 1e-10;
const double MEPS = 1e-8;

struct V3 {
    double x,y,z;
    V3():x(0),y(0),z(0){}
    V3(double a,double b,double c):x(a),y(b),z(c){}
    V3 operator+(const V3&o)const{return{x+o.x,y+o.y,z+o.z};}
    V3 operator-(const V3&o)const{return{x-o.x,y-o.y,z-o.z};}
    V3 operator*(double s)const{return{x*s,y*s,z*s};}
    V3 operator/(double s)const{return{x/s,y/s,z/s};}
    double dot(const V3&o)const{return x*o.x+y*o.y+z*o.z;}
    V3 cross(const V3&o)const{return{y*o.z-z*o.y,z*o.x-x*o.z,x*o.y-y*o.x};}
    double len2()const{return x*x+y*y+z*z;}
    double len()const{return sqrt(len2());}
    V3 norm()const{double l=len();return l>0?*this/l:V3();}
};

struct AABB {
    V3 mn{1e30,1e30,1e30}, mx{-1e30,-1e30,-1e30};
    void add(const V3&p){
        mn.x=min(mn.x,p.x);mn.y=min(mn.y,p.y);mn.z=min(mn.z,p.z);
        mx.x=max(mx.x,p.x);mx.y=max(mx.y,p.y);mx.z=max(mx.z,p.z);
    }
    void add(const AABB&o){add(o.mn);add(o.mx);}
    bool hit(const AABB&o)const{
        return mn.x<=o.mx.x&&mx.x>=o.mn.x&&mn.y<=o.mx.y&&mx.y>=o.mn.y&&mn.z<=o.mx.z&&mx.z>=o.mn.z;
    }
    V3 center()const{return(mn+mx)*0.5;}
};

struct BVHNode {
    AABB box;
    int left=-1,right=-1;
    int start,count;
};

struct Mesh {
    vector<V3> v;
    vector<array<int,3>> f;
};

struct BVH {
    vector<BVHNode> nodes;
    vector<int> idx;
    const Mesh* mesh;

    AABB tbox(int fi)const{
        AABB b;auto&f=mesh->f[fi];
        b.add(mesh->v[f[0]]);b.add(mesh->v[f[1]]);b.add(mesh->v[f[2]]);
        return b;
    }

    int build(vector<int>&ids,int s,int e,int dep){
        int ni=(int)nodes.size();
        nodes.push_back({});
        nodes[ni].start=s;nodes[ni].count=e-s;
        AABB box;
        for(int i=s;i<e;i++)box.add(tbox(ids[i]));
        nodes[ni].box=box;
        if(e-s<=4)return ni;
        V3 ext=box.mx-box.mn;
        int ax=0;
        if(ext.y>ext.x)ax=1;
        if(ext.z>(ax==0?ext.x:ext.y))ax=2;
        int mid=(s+e)/2;
        nth_element(ids.begin()+s,ids.begin()+mid,ids.begin()+e,[&](int a,int b){
            auto ca=tbox(a).center(),cb=tbox(b).center();
            return(ax==0?ca.x:ax==1?ca.y:ca.z)<(ax==0?cb.x:ax==1?cb.y:cb.z);
        });
        int left=build(ids,s,mid,dep+1);
        int right=build(ids,mid,e,dep+1);
        nodes[ni].left=left;
        nodes[ni].right=right;
        nodes[ni].count=0;
        return ni;
    }

    void init(const Mesh&m){
        mesh=&m;
        idx.resize(m.f.size());
        iota(idx.begin(),idx.end(),0);
        if(!m.f.empty()){
            nodes.reserve(m.f.size()*2);
            build(idx,0,(int)idx.size(),0);
        }
    }

    void query(int ni,const AABB&q,vector<int>&res)const{
        if(ni<0||!nodes[ni].box.hit(q))return;
        auto&nd=nodes[ni];
        if(nd.count>0){
            for(int i=nd.start;i<nd.start+nd.count;i++)
                if(tbox(idx[i]).hit(q))res.push_back(idx[i]);
            return;
        }
        query(nd.left,q,res);
        query(nd.right,q,res);
    }

    bool rayHitAABB(const V3&o,const V3&id,const AABB&b)const{
        double tn=-1e30,tf=1e30;
        for(int a=0;a<3;a++){
            double oa=a==0?o.x:a==1?o.y:o.z;
            double ia=a==0?id.x:a==1?id.y:id.z;
            double bmin=a==0?b.mn.x:a==1?b.mn.y:b.mn.z;
            double bmax=a==0?b.mx.x:a==1?b.mx.y:b.mx.z;
            if(fabs(ia)<1e-30){
                if(oa<bmin||oa>bmax)return false;
            } else {
                double t1=(bmin-oa)*ia,t2=(bmax-oa)*ia;
                if(t1>t2){double t=t1;t1=t2;t2=t;}
                tn=max(tn,t1);tf=min(tf,t2);
                if(tn>tf)return false;
            }
        }
        return tf>=0;
    }

    bool rayTri(const V3&o,const V3&d,int fi,double&t)const{
        auto&f=mesh->f[fi];
        V3 v0=mesh->v[f[0]],v1=mesh->v[f[1]],v2=mesh->v[f[2]];
        V3 e1=v1-v0,e2=v2-v0;
        V3 h=d.cross(e2);
        double a=e1.dot(h);
        if(fabs(a)<EPS)return false;
        double ff=1.0/a;
        V3 s=o-v0;
        double u=ff*s.dot(h);
        if(u<-EPS||u>1+EPS)return false;
        V3 q=s.cross(e1);
        double v=ff*d.dot(q);
        if(v<-EPS||u+v>1+EPS)return false;
        t=ff*e2.dot(q);
        return true;
    }

    int countRay(int ni,const V3&o,const V3&d,const V3&id)const{
        if(ni<0)return 0;
        auto&nd=nodes[ni];
        if(!rayHitAABB(o,id,nd.box))return 0;
        int c=0;
        if(nd.count>0){
            for(int i=nd.start;i<nd.start+nd.count;i++){
                double t;
                if(rayTri(o,d,idx[i],t)&&t>EPS)c++;
            }
            return c;
        }
        return countRay(nd.left,o,d,id)+countRay(nd.right,o,d,id);
    }
};

bool isInside(const V3&p,const BVH&bvh){
    if(bvh.nodes.empty())return false;
    V3 dirs[3]={V3(1,.3171,.1413).norm(),V3(.1413,1,.3171).norm(),V3(.3171,.1413,1).norm()};
    int votes=0;
    for(int i=0;i<3;i++){
        V3 id(1.0/dirs[i].x,1.0/dirs[i].y,1.0/dirs[i].z);
        int c=bvh.countRay(0,p,dirs[i],id);
        if(c&1)votes++;
    }
    return votes>=2;
}

struct VMerge {
    vector<V3> verts;
    unordered_map<long long,vector<int>> grid;
    double cs;
    VMerge(double c=MEPS*100):cs(c){}
    long long key(double x,double y,double z)const{
        long long a=(long long)floor(x/cs),b=(long long)floor(y/cs),c=(long long)floor(z/cs);
        return a*1000000007LL+b*1000003LL+c;
    }
    int add(const V3&p){
        for(int dx=-1;dx<=1;dx++)for(int dy=-1;dy<=1;dy++)for(int dz=-1;dz<=1;dz++){
            long long k=key(p.x+dx*cs,p.y+dy*cs,p.z+dz*cs);
            auto it=grid.find(k);
            if(it!=grid.end())for(int vi:it->second)
                if((verts[vi]-p).len2()<MEPS*MEPS)return vi;
        }
        int i=(int)verts.size();
        verts.push_back(p);
        grid[key(p.x,p.y,p.z)].push_back(i);
        return i;
    }
};

bool segTri(const V3&p0,const V3&p1,const V3&v0,const V3&v1,const V3&v2,double&t,double&u,double&v){
    V3 d=p1-p0,e1=v1-v0,e2=v2-v0;
    V3 h=d.cross(e2);
    double a=e1.dot(h);
    if(fabs(a)<EPS)return false;
    double f=1.0/a;
    V3 s=p0-v0;
    u=f*s.dot(h);
    if(u<-EPS||u>1+EPS)return false;
    V3 q=s.cross(e1);
    v=f*d.dot(q);
    if(v<-EPS||u+v>1+EPS)return false;
    t=f*e2.dot(q);
    return t>-EPS&&t<1+EPS;
}

int triTriIsect(const V3&a0,const V3&a1,const V3&a2,
                const V3&b0,const V3&b1,const V3&b2,V3&ip0,V3&ip1){
    vector<V3> pts;
    V3 ae[3][2]={{a0,a1},{a1,a2},{a2,a0}};
    V3 be[3][2]={{b0,b1},{b1,b2},{b2,b0}};
    for(int i=0;i<3;i++){
        double t,u,v;
        if(segTri(ae[i][0],ae[i][1],b0,b1,b2,t,u,v)){
            t=max(0.0,min(1.0,t));
            pts.push_back(ae[i][0]+(ae[i][1]-ae[i][0])*t);
        }
    }
    for(int i=0;i<3;i++){
        double t,u,v;
        if(segTri(be[i][0],be[i][1],a0,a1,a2,t,u,v)){
            t=max(0.0,min(1.0,t));
            pts.push_back(be[i][0]+(be[i][1]-be[i][0])*t);
        }
    }
    vector<V3> uniq;
    for(auto&p:pts){
        bool dup=false;
        for(auto&q:uniq)if((p-q).len2()<MEPS*MEPS){dup=true;break;}
        if(!dup)uniq.push_back(p);
    }
    if(uniq.size()<2)return (int)uniq.size()?(ip0=uniq[0],1):0;
    ip0=uniq[0];ip1=uniq[1];
    return 2;
}

int domAxis(const V3&n){
    double a=fabs(n.x),b=fabs(n.y),c=fabs(n.z);
    if(a>=b&&a>=c)return 0;
    if(b>=a&&b>=c)return 1;
    return 2;
}
void proj2(const V3&p,int ax,double&u,double&v){
    if(ax==0){u=p.y;v=p.z;}
    else if(ax==1){u=p.x;v=p.z;}
    else{u=p.x;v=p.y;}
}
double cross2(double ax,double ay,double bx,double by){return ax*by-ay*bx;}

bool computeChord(const V3&P0,const V3&P1,const V3&V0,const V3&V1,const V3&V2,V3&C0,V3&C1){
    V3 D=P1-P0;
    if(D.len2()<EPS*EPS)return false;
    V3 nrm=(V1-V0).cross(V2-V0);
    int ax=domAxis(nrm);
    double du,dv;proj2(D,ax,du,dv);
    V3 edges[3][2]={{V0,V1},{V1,V2},{V2,V0}};
    struct Hit{double t;V3 pt;};
    vector<Hit> hits;
    double p0u,p0v;proj2(P0,ax,p0u,p0v);
    for(int i=0;i<3;i++){
        double e0u,e0v,e1u,e1v;
        proj2(edges[i][0],ax,e0u,e0v);
        proj2(edges[i][1],ax,e1u,e1v);
        double deu=e1u-e0u,dev=e1v-e0v;
        double det=du*dev-dv*deu;
        if(fabs(det)<EPS)continue;
        double s=(du*(e0v-p0v)-dv*(e0u-p0u))/det;
        if(s<-MEPS||s>1+MEPS)continue;
        s=max(0.0,min(1.0,s));
        double t_val=(deu*(e0v-p0v)-dev*(e0u-p0u))/det;
        V3 hp=edges[i][0]+(edges[i][1]-edges[i][0])*s;
        hits.push_back({t_val,hp});
    }
    vector<Hit> uh;
    for(auto&h:hits){
        bool d=false;
        for(auto&u:uh)if((h.pt-u.pt).len2()<MEPS*MEPS){d=true;break;}
        if(!d)uh.push_back(h);
    }
    if(uh.size()<2)return false;
    sort(uh.begin(),uh.end(),[](const Hit&a,const Hit&b){return a.t<b.t;});
    C0=uh.front().pt;C1=uh.back().pt;
    return(C0-C1).len2()>MEPS*MEPS;
}

struct SubTri{int v[3];};

bool clipChordToTri(const V3&C0,const V3&C1,const V3&A,const V3&B,const V3&C,int ax,
                    double&tMin,double&tMax){
    tMin=0;tMax=1;
    double c0u,c0v,c1u,c1v;
    proj2(C0,ax,c0u,c0v);proj2(C1,ax,c1u,c1v);
    double du=c1u-c0u,dv=c1v-c0v;

    double au,av,bu,bv,cu,cv;
    proj2(A,ax,au,av);proj2(B,ax,bu,bv);proj2(C,ax,cu,cv);
    double sa=cross2(bu-au,bv-av,cu-au,cv-av);
    double flip=sa>0?1.0:-1.0;

    V3 triV[3]={A,B,C};
    for(int i=0;i<3;i++){
        int j=(i+1)%3;
        double e0u,e0v,e1u,e1v;
        proj2(triV[i],ax,e0u,e0v);proj2(triV[j],ax,e1u,e1v);
        double eu=e1u-e0u,ev=e1v-e0v;
        double nx=-ev*flip,ny=eu*flip;
        double num=nx*(e0u-c0u)+ny*(e0v-c0v);
        double den=nx*du+ny*dv;
        if(fabs(den)<EPS){
            if(num<-MEPS)return false;
        } else {
            double t=num/den;
            if(den>0)tMin=max(tMin,t);
            else tMax=min(tMax,t);
        }
    }
    return tMax-tMin>-MEPS;
}

void splitTriBySeg(const SubTri&tri,const V3*verts,int ax,
                   const V3&C0,const V3&C1,VMerge&vm,vector<SubTri>&out){
    V3 A=verts[tri.v[0]],B=verts[tri.v[1]],CC=verts[tri.v[2]];
    double tMin,tMax;
    if(!clipChordToTri(C0,C1,A,B,CC,ax,tMin,tMax)||tMax-tMin<EPS){
        out.push_back(tri);return;
    }
    V3 cp0=C0+(C1-C0)*max(0.0,tMin);
    V3 cp1=C0+(C1-C0)*min(1.0,tMax);
    if((cp0-cp1).len2()<MEPS*MEPS){out.push_back(tri);return;}

    int pi0=vm.add(cp0),pi1=vm.add(cp1);
    if(pi0==pi1){out.push_back(tri);return;}

    int tv[3]={tri.v[0],tri.v[1],tri.v[2]};

    // Check if pi0/pi1 coincide with existing triangle vertices
    int loc0=-1,loc1=-1;
    for(int i=0;i<3;i++){
        if(pi0==tv[i])loc0=i;
        if(pi1==tv[i])loc1=i;
    }

    // Find which edge each point is on (if not at vertex)
    auto findEdge=[&](const V3&p)->int{
        V3 triVerts[3]={A,B,CC};
        for(int i=0;i<3;i++){
            V3 e0=triVerts[i],e1=triVerts[(i+1)%3];
            V3 d=e1-e0;
            double l2=d.len2();
            if(l2<EPS*EPS)continue;
            double t=(p-e0).dot(d)/l2;
            if(t<-MEPS||t>1+MEPS)continue;
            t=max(0.0,min(1.0,t));
            V3 proj=e0+d*t;
            if((p-proj).len2()<MEPS*MEPS)return i;
        }
        return -1;
    };

    int edge0=(loc0>=0)?-1:findEdge(cp0);
    int edge1=(loc1>=0)?-1:findEdge(cp1);

    // Compute original orientation for consistent sub-triangle winding
    double au,av,bu,bv,cu,cv;
    proj2(A,ax,au,av);proj2(B,ax,bu,bv);proj2(CC,ax,cu,cv);
    double origOri=cross2(bu-au,bv-av,cu-au,cv-av);

    auto addTri=[&](int a,int b,int c){
        if(a==b||b==c||a==c)return;
        double a2u,a2v,b2u,b2v,c2u,c2v;
        proj2(vm.verts[a],ax,a2u,a2v);proj2(vm.verts[b],ax,b2u,b2v);proj2(vm.verts[c],ax,c2u,c2v);
        double o=cross2(b2u-a2u,b2v-a2v,c2u-a2u,c2v-a2v);
        if(fabs(o)<EPS*EPS)return;
        if(o*origOri<0)out.push_back({{a,c,b}});
        else out.push_back({{a,b,c}});
    };

    // Case VV: both at vertices -> no split
    if(loc0>=0&&loc1>=0){
        out.push_back(tri);return;
    }

    // Case VE: one at vertex, one on edge -> 2 sub-triangles
    if(loc0>=0&&edge1>=0){
        int ea=edge1,eb=(edge1+1)%3;
        int third=-1;
        for(int i=0;i<3;i++)if(i!=ea&&i!=eb)third=i;
        if(third<0){out.push_back(tri);return;}
        addTri(tv[third],tv[ea],pi1);
        addTri(tv[third],pi1,tv[eb]);
        return;
    }
    if(loc1>=0&&edge0>=0){
        int ea=edge0,eb=(edge0+1)%3;
        int third=-1;
        for(int i=0;i<3;i++)if(i!=ea&&i!=eb)third=i;
        if(third<0){out.push_back(tri);return;}
        addTri(tv[third],tv[ea],pi0);
        addTri(tv[third],pi0,tv[eb]);
        return;
    }

    // Case EE: both on different edges -> 3 sub-triangles
    if(edge0>=0&&edge1>=0){
        if(edge0==edge1){out.push_back(tri);return;}

        int e0a=edge0,e0b=(edge0+1)%3;
        int e1a=edge1,e1b=(edge1+1)%3;
        int shared=-1;
        if(e0a==e1a||e0a==e1b)shared=e0a;
        else if(e0b==e1a||e0b==e1b)shared=e0b;
        if(shared<0){out.push_back(tri);return;}

        int va_=-1,vb_=-1;
        for(int i=0;i<3;i++){
            if(i==shared)continue;
            if(i==e0a||i==e0b)va_=i;
            else vb_=i;
        }
        if(va_<0||vb_<0){out.push_back(tri);return;}

        addTri(tv[shared],pi0,pi1);
        addTri(pi0,tv[va_],tv[vb_]);
        addTri(pi0,tv[vb_],pi1);
        return;
    }

    // Fallback: couldn't classify endpoints properly, keep original
    out.push_back(tri);
}

int main(){
    int nva,nfa,nvb,nfb;
    scanf("%d%d%d%d",&nva,&nfa,&nvb,&nfb);

    Mesh A,B;
    A.v.resize(nva);A.f.resize(nfa);
    B.v.resize(nvb);B.f.resize(nfb);
    for(int i=0;i<nva;i++)scanf("%lf%lf%lf",&A.v[i].x,&A.v[i].y,&A.v[i].z);
    for(int i=0;i<nfa;i++)scanf("%d%d%d",&A.f[i][0],&A.f[i][1],&A.f[i][2]);
    for(int i=0;i<nvb;i++)scanf("%lf%lf%lf",&B.v[i].x,&B.v[i].y,&B.v[i].z);
    for(int i=0;i<nfb;i++)scanf("%d%d%d",&B.f[i][0],&B.f[i][1],&B.f[i][2]);

    BVH bvhA,bvhB;
    bvhA.init(A);bvhB.init(B);

    AABB bA,bB;
    for(auto&v:A.v)bA.add(v);
    for(auto&v:B.v)bB.add(v);

    if(!bA.hit(bB)){
        printf("%d %d\n",nva+nvb,nfa+nfb);
        for(auto&v:A.v)printf("%.17g %.17g %.17g\n",v.x,v.y,v.z);
        for(auto&v:B.v)printf("%.17g %.17g %.17g\n",v.x,v.y,v.z);
        for(auto&f:A.f)printf("%d %d %d\n",f[0],f[1],f[2]);
        for(auto&f:B.f)printf("%d %d %d\n",f[0]+nva,f[1]+nva,f[2]+nva);
        return 0;
    }

    VMerge vm;
    vector<int> mapAv(nva),mapBv(nvb);
    for(int i=0;i<nva;i++)mapAv[i]=vm.add(A.v[i]);
    for(int i=0;i<nvb;i++)mapBv[i]=vm.add(B.v[i]);

    vector<vector<pair<V3,V3>>> chordsA(nfa),chordsB(nfb);

    for(int fi=0;fi<nfa;fi++){
        AABB ab;
        ab.add(A.v[A.f[fi][0]]);ab.add(A.v[A.f[fi][1]]);ab.add(A.v[A.f[fi][2]]);
        vector<int> cands;
        if(!bvhB.nodes.empty())bvhB.query(0,ab,cands);
        for(int fj:cands){
            V3 ip0,ip1;
            int n=triTriIsect(A.v[A.f[fi][0]],A.v[A.f[fi][1]],A.v[A.f[fi][2]],
                              B.v[B.f[fj][0]],B.v[B.f[fj][1]],B.v[B.f[fj][2]],ip0,ip1);
            if(n==2){
                V3 ca0,ca1;
                if(computeChord(ip0,ip1,A.v[A.f[fi][0]],A.v[A.f[fi][1]],A.v[A.f[fi][2]],ca0,ca1))
                    chordsA[fi].push_back({ca0,ca1});
                V3 cb0,cb1;
                if(computeChord(ip0,ip1,B.v[B.f[fj][0]],B.v[B.f[fj][1]],B.v[B.f[fj][2]],cb0,cb1))
                    chordsB[fj].push_back({cb0,cb1});
            }
        }
    }

    bool anyIsect=false;
    for(int i=0;i<nfa&&!anyIsect;i++)if(!chordsA[i].empty())anyIsect=true;
    for(int i=0;i<nfb&&!anyIsect;i++)if(!chordsB[i].empty())anyIsect=true;

    if(!anyIsect){
        bool aInB=isInside(A.v[0],bvhB);
        bool bInA=isInside(B.v[0],bvhA);
        if(aInB){
            printf("%d %d\n",nvb,nfb);
            for(auto&v:B.v)printf("%.17g %.17g %.17g\n",v.x,v.y,v.z);
            for(auto&f:B.f)printf("%d %d %d\n",f[0],f[1],f[2]);
        } else if(bInA){
            printf("%d %d\n",nva,nfa);
            for(auto&v:A.v)printf("%.17g %.17g %.17g\n",v.x,v.y,v.z);
            for(auto&f:A.f)printf("%d %d %d\n",f[0],f[1],f[2]);
        } else {
            printf("%d %d\n",nva+nvb,nfa+nfb);
            for(auto&v:A.v)printf("%.17g %.17g %.17g\n",v.x,v.y,v.z);
            for(auto&v:B.v)printf("%.17g %.17g %.17g\n",v.x,v.y,v.z);
            for(auto&f:A.f)printf("%d %d %d\n",f[0],f[1],f[2]);
            for(auto&f:B.f)printf("%d %d %d\n",f[0]+nva,f[1]+nva,f[2]+nva);
        }
        return 0;
    }

    auto processMesh=[&](Mesh&M,vector<int>&mapV,vector<vector<pair<V3,V3>>>&chords,
                         int nf,const BVH&otherBvh,vector<array<int,3>>&result){
        for(int fi=0;fi<nf;fi++){
            auto&f=M.f[fi];
            int v0=mapV[f[0]],v1=mapV[f[1]],v2=mapV[f[2]];

            if(chords[fi].empty()){
                V3 cent=(M.v[f[0]]+M.v[f[1]]+M.v[f[2]])/3.0;
                if(!isInside(cent,otherBvh))
                    result.push_back({v0,v1,v2});
                continue;
            }

            V3 nrm=(M.v[f[1]]-M.v[f[0]]).cross(M.v[f[2]]-M.v[f[0]]);
            int ax=domAxis(nrm);

            vector<SubTri> subs={{{v0,v1,v2}}};

            // Deduplicate chords
            vector<pair<V3,V3>> uc;
            for(auto&c:chords[fi]){
                bool dup=false;
                for(auto&u:uc){
                    if(((c.first-u.first).len2()<MEPS*MEPS&&(c.second-u.second).len2()<MEPS*MEPS)||
                       ((c.first-u.second).len2()<MEPS*MEPS&&(c.second-u.first).len2()<MEPS*MEPS)){
                        dup=true;break;
                    }
                }
                if(!dup)uc.push_back(c);
            }

            for(auto&[c0,c1]:uc){
                vector<SubTri> next;
                for(auto&st:subs)
                    splitTriBySeg(st,vm.verts.data(),ax,c0,c1,vm,next);
                subs=next;
            }

            for(auto&st:subs){
                V3 cent=(vm.verts[st.v[0]]+vm.verts[st.v[1]]+vm.verts[st.v[2]])/3.0;
                V3 e1=vm.verts[st.v[1]]-vm.verts[st.v[0]];
                V3 e2=vm.verts[st.v[2]]-vm.verts[st.v[0]];
                if(e1.cross(e2).len2()<EPS*EPS)continue;
                if(!isInside(cent,otherBvh))
                    result.push_back({st.v[0],st.v[1],st.v[2]});
            }
        }
    };

    vector<array<int,3>> result;
    processMesh(A,mapAv,chordsA,nfa,bvhB,result);
    processMesh(B,mapBv,chordsB,nfb,bvhA,result);

    // Compact vertices
    vector<int> remap(vm.verts.size(),-1);
    int nxt=0;
    for(auto&f:result)for(int i=0;i<3;i++){
        if(remap[f[i]]<0)remap[f[i]]=nxt++;
        f[i]=remap[f[i]];
    }
    vector<V3> ov(nxt);
    for(int i=0;i<(int)vm.verts.size();i++)
        if(remap[i]>=0)ov[remap[i]]=vm.verts[i];

    printf("%d %d\n",nxt,(int)result.size());
    for(auto&v:ov)printf("%.17g %.17g %.17g\n",v.x,v.y,v.z);
    for(auto&f:result)printf("%d %d %d\n",f[0],f[1],f[2]);
    return 0;
}

Test 8: Maze Solver (Rust) Score: 1.00 / 14
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a maze solver in Rust that must handle ANY maze complexity from trivial to ludicrous scale.

Your program will be tested with mazes ranging from 5x5 to 50000x50000.
The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Maze format:**
- 'A' = Start position
- 'B' = Goal/End position
- '#' = Wall (cannot pass through)
- ' ' (space) = Open path (can walk through)

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: H W (height and width)
Next H lines: the maze row strings (each W characters)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: N (number of steps in path)
Next N lines: x y (0-indexed coordinates from top-left)
Path should include both start (A) and end (B) positions.
Movement is only up/down/left/right (no diagonals).
Output nothing (or 0) if no path exists.

**Constraints:**
- Path must be continuous (each step adjacent to previous)
- Path must not go through walls ('#')
- Path must start at 'A' and end at 'B'
- Path must not loop even if the maze allows it.
- If multiple solutions exist, return any one.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Find the shortest path through a maze from a start cell to an end cell. The maze is a grid of walls and open passages, and the AI must navigate from one corner to the other using the fewest steps possible.

Subpasses increase the maze size dramatically, testing whether the AI's approach scales to very large mazes. The baseline finds the true shortest path using breadth-first search.

 1.0000
[5x5] Path found, Time: 0.0s - path length 5

Maze Solver - 5x5

View Code (4317 chars)
use std::io::{self, Read, Write, BufWriter};
use std::collections::VecDeque;

fn write_usize(out: &mut impl Write, n: usize) {
    if n == 0 {
        out.write_all(b"0").unwrap();
        return;
    }
    let mut buf = [0u8; 20];
    let mut i = 20;
    let mut v = n;
    while v > 0 {
        i -= 1;
        buf[i] = b'0' + (v % 10) as u8;
        v /= 10;
    }
    out.write_all(&buf[i..]).unwrap();
}

fn main() {
    let mut input = Vec::new();
    io::stdin().lock().read_to_end(&mut input).unwrap();

    let stdout = io::stdout();
    let mut out = BufWriter::with_capacity(1 << 20, stdout.lock());

    let mut pos = 0usize;

    fn skip_ws(data: &[u8], pos: &mut usize) {
        while *pos < data.len()
            && (data[*pos] == b' ' || data[*pos] == b'\t'
                || data[*pos] == b'\r' || data[*pos] == b'\n')
        {
            *pos += 1;
        }
    }

    fn parse_num(data: &[u8], pos: &mut usize) -> usize {
        skip_ws(data, pos);
        let mut n = 0usize;
        while *pos < data.len() && data[*pos].is_ascii_digit() {
            n = n * 10 + (data[*pos] - b'0') as usize;
            *pos += 1;
        }
        n
    }

    let h = parse_num(&input, &mut pos);
    let w = parse_num(&input, &mut pos);

    while pos < input.len() && input[pos] != b'\n' {
        pos += 1;
    }
    if pos < input.len() {
        pos += 1;
    }

    let mut grid = vec![1u8; h * w];
    let mut start = 0usize;
    let mut end = 0usize;

    for r in 0..h {
        let row_start = pos;
        let mut line_end = pos;
        while line_end < input.len() && input[line_end] != b'\n' && input[line_end] != b'\r' {
            line_end += 1;
        }

        let cols = (line_end - row_start).min(w);
        for c in 0..cols {
            let idx = r * w + c;
            match input[row_start + c] {
                b'#' => {}
                b'A' => {
                    grid[idx] = 0;
                    start = idx;
                }
                b'B' => {
                    grid[idx] = 0;
                    end = idx;
                }
                _ => {
                    grid[idx] = 0;
                }
            }
        }

        pos = line_end;
        if pos < input.len() && input[pos] == b'\r' {
            pos += 1;
        }
        if pos < input.len() && input[pos] == b'\n' {
            pos += 1;
        }
    }

    drop(input);

    grid[start] = 6;
    let mut queue = VecDeque::with_capacity(4096);
    queue.push_back(start);

    let mut found = false;

    'bfs: while let Some(cur) = queue.pop_front() {
        if cur == end {
            found = true;
            break;
        }

        let r = cur / w;
        let c = cur % w;

        if r > 0 {
            let n = cur - w;
            if grid[n] == 0 {
                grid[n] = 3;
                if n == end { found = true; break 'bfs; }
                queue.push_back(n);
            }
        }
        if r + 1 < h {
            let n = cur + w;
            if grid[n] == 0 {
                grid[n] = 2;
                if n == end { found = true; break 'bfs; }
                queue.push_back(n);
            }
        }
        if c > 0 {
            let n = cur - 1;
            if grid[n] == 0 {
                grid[n] = 5;
                if n == end { found = true; break 'bfs; }
                queue.push_back(n);
            }
        }
        if c + 1 < w {
            let n = cur + 1;
            if grid[n] == 0 {
                grid[n] = 4;
                if n == end { found = true; break 'bfs; }
                queue.push_back(n);
            }
        }
    }

    if !found {
        out.write_all(b"0\n").unwrap();
        return;
    }

    let mut path = Vec::new();
    let mut cur = end;
    loop {
        path.push(cur);
        if cur == start {
            break;
        }
        cur = match grid[cur] {
            2 => cur - w,
            3 => cur + w,
            4 => cur - 1,
            5 => cur + 1,
            _ => break,
        };
    }
    path.reverse();

    write_usize(&mut out, path.len());
    out.write_all(b"\n").unwrap();
    for &p in &path {
        write_usize(&mut out, p / w);
        out.write_all(b" ").unwrap();
        write_usize(&mut out, p % w);
        out.write_all(b"\n").unwrap();
    }
}

Test 9: 1D Cutting Stock - Minimum Waste (C#) Score: 13.50 / 15
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the 1D Cutting Stock Problem in C#.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N stock_length
Line 2: N cut lengths separated by spaces

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: num_stocks
Next num_stocks lines: space-separated cut indices assigned to that stock (0-indexed)

**Example:**
For cuts=[30, 40, 50] and stock_length=100:
Input:
3 100
30 40 50

Output:
2
0 1
2

**Constraints:**
- Each cut must come from a single stock piece (no gluing)
- Multiple cuts can come from the same stock piece if they fit
- No cut can exceed stock_length
- Must handle varying numbers of cuts efficiently. You can use
multiple threads, you can micro-optimise code, you can write clever algorithms,
whatever it takes to handle the problem and solve it within 5 minutes even if
there are millions of cuts.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.

View chain of thought

Given fixed-length stock material and a shopping list of smaller pieces to cut, use as few stock pieces as possible. Every requested piece must be cut from somewhere, but any leftover material is wasted — so packing the cuts tightly matters.

This is essentially a bin-packing problem and is NP-hard. Subpasses scale from a handful of cuts up to millions, forcing the AI to write code that is both correct and fast. The baseline uses a simple first-fit-decreasing heuristic.

 1.0000
[5 cuts, stock=100] Stocks used: 2, Waste: 20, Time: 0.1s - (waste < 1 stock, no repack check)

Cutting Stock - 5 cuts, stock=100

View Code (5870 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using System.Text;

class Program
{
    static byte[] buf = new byte[1 << 16];
    static int bufPos, bufLen;
    static Stream stdin;

    static int ReadByte()
    {
        if (bufPos >= bufLen)
        {
            bufLen = stdin.Read(buf, 0, buf.Length);
            bufPos = 0;
            if (bufLen == 0) return -1;
        }
        return buf[bufPos++];
    }

    static int ReadInt()
    {
        int c;
        while ((c = ReadByte()) >= 0 && (c < '0' || c > '9')) ;
        if (c < 0) return -1;
        int v = c - '0';
        while ((c = ReadByte()) >= '0' && c <= '9')
            v = v * 10 + (c - '0');
        return v;
    }

    static int[] seg;
    static int SN;

    static void SegUp(int p, int d)
    {
        p += SN;
        seg[p] += d;
        for (p >>= 1; p >= 1; p >>= 1)
            seg[p] = seg[2 * p] + seg[2 * p + 1];
    }

    static int SegCeil(int lo)
    {
        return SegQ(1, 0, SN - 1, lo, SN - 1);
    }

    static int SegQ(int nd, int l, int r, int ql, int qr)
    {
        if (r < ql || l > qr || seg[nd] == 0) return -1;
        if (l == r) return l;
        int m = (l + r) >> 1;
        int res = SegQ(2 * nd, l, m, ql, qr);
        return res >= 0 ? res : SegQ(2 * nd + 1, m + 1, r, ql, qr);
    }

    static void Main()
    {
        stdin = Console.OpenStandardInput();
        int n = ReadInt();
        int stockLen = ReadInt();

        if (n == 0)
        {
            Console.Write("0\n");
            return;
        }

        var cuts = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
            cuts[i] = ReadInt();

        var order = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) order[i] = i;
        Array.Sort(order, (a, b) => cuts[b].CompareTo(cuts[a]));

        int[] binOf = new int[n];
        int numBins = 0;
        var capBins = new Dictionary<int, List<int>>();

        if (stockLen <= 16_000_000)
        {
            SN = 1;
            while (SN <= stockLen) SN <<= 1;
            seg = new int[2 * SN];

            for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
            {
                int ci = order[i];
                int sz = cuts[ci];
                int cap = SegCeil(sz);

                if (cap < 0)
                {
                    int bi = numBins++;
                    binOf[ci] = bi;
                    int nc = stockLen - sz;
                    if (nc > 0)
                    {
                        SegUp(nc, 1);
                        if (!capBins.TryGetValue(nc, out var lst))
                            capBins[nc] = lst = new List<int>();
                        lst.Add(bi);
                    }
                }
                else
                {
                    var lst = capBins[cap];
                    int bi = lst[lst.Count - 1];
                    lst.RemoveAt(lst.Count - 1);
                    SegUp(cap, -1);
                    if (lst.Count == 0) capBins.Remove(cap);

                    binOf[ci] = bi;
                    int nc = cap - sz;
                    if (nc > 0)
                    {
                        SegUp(nc, 1);
                        if (!capBins.TryGetValue(nc, out var nlst))
                            capBins[nc] = nlst = new List<int>();
                        nlst.Add(bi);
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        else
        {
            var uniqueCaps = new SortedSet<int>();

            for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
            {
                int ci = order[i];
                int sz = cuts[ci];
                int cap = -1;
                foreach (int c in uniqueCaps.GetViewBetween(sz, stockLen))
                {
                    cap = c;
                    break;
                }

                if (cap < 0)
                {
                    int bi = numBins++;
                    binOf[ci] = bi;
                    int nc = stockLen - sz;
                    if (nc > 0)
                    {
                        uniqueCaps.Add(nc);
                        if (!capBins.TryGetValue(nc, out var lst))
                            capBins[nc] = lst = new List<int>();
                        lst.Add(bi);
                    }
                }
                else
                {
                    var lst = capBins[cap];
                    int bi = lst[lst.Count - 1];
                    lst.RemoveAt(lst.Count - 1);
                    if (lst.Count == 0)
                    {
                        capBins.Remove(cap);
                        uniqueCaps.Remove(cap);
                    }

                    binOf[ci] = bi;
                    int nc = cap - sz;
                    if (nc > 0)
                    {
                        uniqueCaps.Add(nc);
                        if (!capBins.TryGetValue(nc, out var nlst))
                            capBins[nc] = nlst = new List<int>();
                        nlst.Add(bi);
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        seg = null;
        var stdout = Console.OpenStandardOutput();
        var writer = new StreamWriter(stdout, Encoding.ASCII, 1 << 16);

        var binHead = new int[numBins];
        var next = new int[n];
        Array.Fill(binHead, -1);
        for (int i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--)
        {
            int bi = binOf[i];
            next[i] = binHead[bi];
            binHead[bi] = i;
        }

        writer.Write(numBins);
        writer.Write('\n');
        for (int i = 0; i < numBins; i++)
        {
            bool first = true;
            int j = binHead[i];
            while (j >= 0)
            {
                if (!first) writer.Write(' ');
                writer.Write(j);
                first = false;
                j = next[j];
            }
            writer.Write('\n');
        }
        writer.Flush();
    }
}

Test 10: 2D Cutting Stock - Rectangle Packing (C++) Score: 5.00 / 13
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the 2D Cutting Stock Problem (Rectangle Bin Packing) in C++.

Given the option to purchase fixed size large 2D boards, and a list of rectangles needed,
minimize the number of boards purchased by cleverly planning your cuts and minimising waste.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY problem size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 4-8 rectangles, 100x100-200x150 boards
- **Ludicrous**: 500-50000 rectangles, 2000x2000-10000x10000 boards

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N board_width board_height
Next N lines: w h (rectangle dimensions)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: num_boards
Next N lines: board_index x y rotated (one per rectangle, rotated is 0 or 1)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on problem size and board dimensions
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for 50000 rectangles
3. **Quality**: Minimize number of boards used while producing valid placements

**Constraints:**
- Rectangles may be rotated 90° if it helps
- No overlapping allowed
- Rectangles must not exceed board boundaries

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:

Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Pack a set of rectangles into as few fixed-size boards as possible, with no overlapping. Rectangles may be rotated 90° to fit better. Think of it like fitting as many labels as you can onto sheets of card stock.

This 2D packing problem is NP-hard. Subpasses increase the number and variety of rectangles. The baseline stacks rectangles in simple horizontal rows.

 1.0000
[4 rectangles, 100x100 board] Boards: 1, Baseline: 1, Waste: 4100, Time: 0.4s - excellent (≤ baseline). (waste < 1 board, no repack check)

2D Cutting Stock - 4 rectangles, 100x100 board

View Code (7334 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <algorithm>
#include <climits>
#include <chrono>

struct Rect { int w, h, idx; };
struct Placement { int board, x, y, rotated; };
struct FreeRect { int x, y, w, h; };

class MaxRectsBin {
    int binW, binH;
    std::vector<FreeRect> free_;

public:
    MaxRectsBin(int w, int h) : binW(w), binH(h) {
        free_.push_back({0, 0, w, h});
    }

    bool place(int rw, int rh, int &px, int &py, bool &rot, int method) {
        int bs1 = INT_MAX, bs2 = INT_MAX;
        int bx = 0, by = 0;
        bool brot = false;
        bool found = false;

        for (auto &fr : free_) {
            for (int r = 0; r < 2; r++) {
                int tw = r ? rh : rw, th = r ? rw : rh;
                if (tw > fr.w || th > fr.h) continue;
                int s1, s2;
                calcScore(method, tw, th, fr, s1, s2);
                if (s1 < bs1 || (s1 == bs1 && s2 < bs2)) {
                    bs1 = s1; bs2 = s2;
                    bx = fr.x; by = fr.y;
                    brot = (r == 1);
                    found = true;
                }
            }
        }
        if (!found) return false;

        px = bx; py = by; rot = brot;
        int pw = rot ? rh : rw, ph = rot ? rw : rh;
        splitAndPrune(px, py, pw, ph);
        return true;
    }

private:
    void calcScore(int m, int rw, int rh, const FreeRect &fr, int &s1, int &s2) {
        int dw = fr.w - rw, dh = fr.h - rh;
        switch (m) {
            case 0: s1 = std::min(dw, dh); s2 = std::max(dw, dh); break;
            case 1: s1 = std::max(dw, dh); s2 = std::min(dw, dh); break;
            case 2: s1 = fr.w * fr.h - rw * rh; s2 = std::min(dw, dh); break;
            default: s1 = fr.y; s2 = fr.x; break;
        }
    }

    void splitAndPrune(int px, int py, int pw, int ph) {
        int n = (int)free_.size();
        for (int i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
            FreeRect &fr = free_[i];
            if (px >= fr.x + fr.w || px + pw <= fr.x ||
                py >= fr.y + fr.h || py + ph <= fr.y)
                continue;

            if (px > fr.x)
                free_.push_back({fr.x, fr.y, px - fr.x, fr.h});
            if (px + pw < fr.x + fr.w)
                free_.push_back({px + pw, fr.y, fr.x + fr.w - px - pw, fr.h});
            if (py > fr.y)
                free_.push_back({fr.x, fr.y, fr.w, py - fr.y});
            if (py + ph < fr.y + fr.h)
                free_.push_back({fr.x, py + ph, fr.w, fr.y + fr.h - py - ph});

            free_[i] = free_.back();
            free_.pop_back();
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < (int)free_.size(); i++) {
            for (int j = i + 1; j < (int)free_.size(); ) {
                if (contains(free_[i], free_[j])) {
                    free_[j] = free_.back(); free_.pop_back();
                } else if (contains(free_[j], free_[i])) {
                    free_[i] = free_[j];
                    free_[j] = free_.back(); free_.pop_back();
                    j = i + 1;
                } else {
                    j++;
                }
            }
        }

        if (free_.size() > 600) {
            std::sort(free_.begin(), free_.end(),
                [](const FreeRect &a, const FreeRect &b) {
                    return (long long)a.w * a.h > (long long)b.w * b.h;
                });
            free_.resize(600);
        }
    }

    static bool contains(const FreeRect &a, const FreeRect &b) {
        return b.x >= a.x && b.y >= a.y &&
               b.x + b.w <= a.x + a.w && b.y + b.h <= a.y + a.h;
    }
};

struct Result {
    int boards;
    std::vector<Placement> pl;
};

Result solve(const std::vector<Rect> &rects, int N, int W, int H,
             const std::vector<int> &order, int method) {
    std::vector<MaxRectsBin> bins;
    std::vector<Placement> pl(N);

    for (int oi = 0; oi < N; oi++) {
        int i = order[oi];
        int rw = rects[i].w, rh = rects[i].h;
        bool placed = false;

        for (int b = 0; b < (int)bins.size(); b++) {
            int px, py; bool rot;
            if (bins[b].place(rw, rh, px, py, rot, method)) {
                pl[i] = {b, px, py, rot ? 1 : 0};
                placed = true;
                break;
            }
        }
        if (!placed) {
            bins.emplace_back(W, H);
            int px, py; bool rot;
            if (bins.back().place(rw, rh, px, py, rot, method)) {
                pl[i] = {(int)bins.size() - 1, px, py, rot ? 1 : 0};
            } else {
                pl[i] = {(int)bins.size() - 1, 0, 0, 0};
            }
        }
    }
    return {(int)bins.size(), std::move(pl)};
}

int main() {
    std::ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(nullptr);

    int N, W, H;
    std::cin >> N >> W >> H;

    std::vector<Rect> rects(N);
    for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) {
        std::cin >> rects[i].w >> rects[i].h;
        rects[i].idx = i;
    }

    auto t0 = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
    auto elapsed = [&]() {
        return std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(
            std::chrono::steady_clock::now() - t0).count();
    };

    auto makeOrder = [&](auto cmp) {
        std::vector<int> ord(N);
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) ord[i] = i;
        std::sort(ord.begin(), ord.end(), cmp);
        return ord;
    };

    std::vector<int> orderArea = makeOrder([&](int a, int b) {
        long long aa = (long long)rects[a].w * rects[a].h;
        long long ab = (long long)rects[b].w * rects[b].h;
        if (aa != ab) return aa > ab;
        return std::max(rects[a].w, rects[a].h) > std::max(rects[b].w, rects[b].h);
    });

    std::vector<int> orderMaxSide = makeOrder([&](int a, int b) {
        int ma = std::max(rects[a].w, rects[a].h);
        int mb = std::max(rects[b].w, rects[b].h);
        if (ma != mb) return ma > mb;
        return (long long)rects[a].w * rects[a].h > (long long)rects[b].w * rects[b].h;
    });

    std::vector<int> orderHeight = makeOrder([&](int a, int b) {
        int ha = std::max(rects[a].h, rects[a].w);
        int hb = std::max(rects[b].h, rects[b].w);
        if (ha != hb) return ha > hb;
        return std::min(rects[a].w, rects[a].h) > std::min(rects[b].w, rects[b].h);
    });

    std::vector<int> orderPerim = makeOrder([&](int a, int b) {
        int pa = rects[a].w + rects[a].h;
        int pb = rects[b].w + rects[b].h;
        if (pa != pb) return pa > pb;
        return (long long)rects[a].w * rects[a].h > (long long)rects[b].w * rects[b].h;
    });

    std::vector<std::vector<int>*> sorts = {&orderArea, &orderMaxSide, &orderHeight, &orderPerim};
    int numMethods = 4;

    int bestBoards = INT_MAX;
    std::vector<Placement> bestPl;

    int timeBudget = (N > 5000) ? 15000 : 25000;

    for (auto *srt : sorts) {
        for (int m = 0; m < numMethods; m++) {
            if (elapsed() > timeBudget) break;
            auto res = solve(rects, N, W, H, *srt, m);
            if (res.boards < bestBoards) {
                bestBoards = res.boards;
                bestPl = std::move(res.pl);
            }
        }
        if (elapsed() > timeBudget) break;
    }

    std::cout << bestBoards << "\n";
    for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) {
        std::cout << bestPl[i].board << " " << bestPl[i].x << " "
                  << bestPl[i].y << " " << bestPl[i].rotated << "\n";
    }
    return 0;
}

Test 11: 2D Polygon Cutting Stock (Rust) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a 2D Polygon Cutting Stock Problem in Rust.

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with problems ranging from 2 simple pieces to 5000+ complex polygons.
The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: SV NP (number of stock polygon vertices, number of pieces)
Next SV lines: x y (stock polygon vertices)
For each piece:
Line: PV (number of vertices in this piece)
Next PV lines: x y (piece polygon vertices)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: num_stocks
Next NP lines: stock_index x y rotation (one per piece)
stock_index: 0-indexed stock polygon
x y: translation to apply
rotation: angle in radians

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on number and complexity of pieces
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for 5000+ complex pieces
3. **Quality**: Minimize stock usage while ensuring valid placements

**Constraints:**
- Each placed piece must fit entirely within the stock polygon
- Pieces on same stock must not overlap
- Pieces may be rotated to any angle

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

Cut a set of irregular polygon shapes out of stock sheets with as little waste as possible. Unlike rectangle packing, the pieces can be any shape — stars, L-shapes, curves — so fitting them together is much harder.

The AI must handle polygon geometry (containment, intersection, rotation) and plan efficient layouts. Subpasses increase the number and complexity of the pieces. The baseline uses a simple greedy placement strategy.

Test 12: 3D Bin Packing - Polyhedra in Box (C#) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a 3D Bin Packing problem in C#.

You must write a C# solver that can handle ANY problem size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: Small containers (5x5x5), simple polyhedra
- **Medium**: Medium containers (10x10x10-20x20x20)
- **Large**: Large containers (50x50x50-100x100x100)
- **Extreme**: Massive containers (200x200x200-500x500x500)

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: V F (vertex/face count of the polyhedron)
Next V lines: x y z (polyhedron vertices)
Next F lines: a b c (polyhedron face indices)
Last line: W H D (container width, height, depth)
Container origin at (0,0,0), extends to (W,H,D).

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: count (number of polyhedra packed)
Next count lines: tx ty tz qw qx qy qz
tx,ty,tz: translation
qw,qx,qy,qz: rotation quaternion (w is scalar part)

**Quaternion Rotation Reference:**
- Identity (no rotation): 1 0 0 0
- 90° around Z: 0.707 0 0 0.707
- To rotate a point: p' = q * p * q^(-1)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on container size and polyhedron complexity
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for massive containers
3. **Quality**: Maximize packing count while ensuring valid placements

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

Pack as many copies of an irregular 3D shape into a box as possible, without any overlaps or pieces poking outside. The shapes can be rotated freely, so finding the best orientations is part of the puzzle.

The AI must handle 3D collision detection and spatial reasoning. Subpasses use increasingly awkward shapes that resist neat stacking. The baseline uses a simple grid placement without rotation optimisation.

3D Bin Packing - 2x2x2 boxes in 10x10x10 container

View Code (6647 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Globalization;
using System.IO;

class Program
{
    static void Main()
    {
        CultureInfo.CurrentCulture = CultureInfo.InvariantCulture;

        var line1 = Console.ReadLine().Trim().Split(new[]{' ','\t'}, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
        int V = int.Parse(line1[0]);
        int F = int.Parse(line1[1]);

        double[][] vertices = new double[V][];
        for (int i = 0; i < V; i++)
        {
            var parts = Console.ReadLine().Trim().Split(new[]{' ','\t'}, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
            vertices[i] = new double[] {
                double.Parse(parts[0], CultureInfo.InvariantCulture),
                double.Parse(parts[1], CultureInfo.InvariantCulture),
                double.Parse(parts[2], CultureInfo.InvariantCulture)
            };
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < F; i++) Console.ReadLine();

        var lastLine = Console.ReadLine().Trim().Split(new[]{' ','\t'}, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
        double W = double.Parse(lastLine[0], CultureInfo.InvariantCulture);
        double H = double.Parse(lastLine[1], CultureInfo.InvariantCulture);
        double D = double.Parse(lastLine[2], CultureInfo.InvariantCulture);

        var rotations = Generate24Rotations();

        long bestCount = 0;
        double[] bestQuat = new double[]{1,0,0,0};
        double bestSx = 0, bestSy = 0, bestSz = 0;
        double bestMinX = 0, bestMinY = 0, bestMinZ = 0;
        int bestNx = 0, bestNy = 0, bestNz = 0;

        double eps = 1e-7;

        foreach (var q in rotations)
        {
            double minX = double.MaxValue, minY = double.MaxValue, minZ = double.MaxValue;
            double maxX = double.MinValue, maxY = double.MinValue, maxZ = double.MinValue;

            for (int i = 0; i < V; i++)
            {
                var r = RotatePoint(q, vertices[i]);
                if (r[0] < minX) minX = r[0];
                if (r[1] < minY) minY = r[1];
                if (r[2] < minZ) minZ = r[2];
                if (r[0] > maxX) maxX = r[0];
                if (r[1] > maxY) maxY = r[1];
                if (r[2] > maxZ) maxZ = r[2];
            }

            double sx = maxX - minX + eps;
            double sy = maxY - minY + eps;
            double sz = maxZ - minZ + eps;

            if (sx > W || sy > H || sz > D) continue;

            int nx = (int)(W / sx);
            int ny = (int)(H / sy);
            int nz = (int)(D / sz);

            if (nx < 1 || ny < 1 || nz < 1) continue;

            long count = (long)nx * ny * nz;
            if (count > bestCount)
            {
                bestCount = count;
                bestQuat = q;
                bestSx = sx; bestSy = sy; bestSz = sz;
                bestMinX = minX; bestMinY = minY; bestMinZ = minZ;
                bestNx = nx; bestNy = ny; bestNz = nz;
            }
        }

        using (var stdout = new BufferedStream(Console.OpenStandardOutput(), 1 << 20))
        using (var writer = new StreamWriter(stdout, System.Text.Encoding.ASCII, 1 << 16))
        {
            writer.NewLine = "\n";
            writer.WriteLine(bestCount);

            string qStr = string.Format(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture,
                "{0:F6} {1:F6} {2:F6} {3:F6}",
                bestQuat[0], bestQuat[1], bestQuat[2], bestQuat[3]);

            for (int ix = 0; ix < bestNx; ix++)
            {
                double tx = ix * bestSx - bestMinX;
                for (int iy = 0; iy < bestNy; iy++)
                {
                    double ty = iy * bestSy - bestMinY;
                    for (int iz = 0; iz < bestNz; iz++)
                    {
                        double tz = iz * bestSz - bestMinZ;
                        writer.Write(tx.ToString("F6", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture));
                        writer.Write(' ');
                        writer.Write(ty.ToString("F6", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture));
                        writer.Write(' ');
                        writer.Write(tz.ToString("F6", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture));
                        writer.Write(' ');
                        writer.WriteLine(qStr);
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }

    static double[] RotatePoint(double[] q, double[] p)
    {
        double qw = q[0], qx = q[1], qy = q[2], qz = q[3];
        double px = p[0], py = p[1], pz = p[2];

        double tx = 2.0 * (qy * pz - qz * py);
        double ty = 2.0 * (qz * px - qx * pz);
        double tz = 2.0 * (qx * py - qy * px);

        return new double[] {
            px + qw * tx + (qy * tz - qz * ty),
            py + qw * ty + (qz * tx - qx * tz),
            pz + qw * tz + (qx * ty - qy * tx)
        };
    }

    static List<double[]> Generate24Rotations()
    {
        var result = new List<double[]>();
        var seen = new HashSet<string>();

        double s = Math.Sqrt(2.0) / 2.0;

        double[][] bases = new double[][] {
            new double[] {1, 0, 0, 0},
            new double[] {s, s, 0, 0},
            new double[] {0, 1, 0, 0},
            new double[] {s, -s, 0, 0},
            new double[] {s, 0, s, 0},
            new double[] {0, 0, 1, 0},
            new double[] {s, 0, -s, 0},
            new double[] {s, 0, 0, s},
            new double[] {0, 0, 0, 1},
            new double[] {s, 0, 0, -s},
        };

        foreach (var q1 in bases)
        {
            foreach (var q2 in bases)
            {
                var q = QuatMul(q1, q2);
                double len = Math.Sqrt(q[0]*q[0]+q[1]*q[1]+q[2]*q[2]+q[3]*q[3]);
                q[0]/=len; q[1]/=len; q[2]/=len; q[3]/=len;

                if (q[0] < -1e-9 || (Math.Abs(q[0]) < 1e-9 && q[1] < -1e-9) ||
                    (Math.Abs(q[0]) < 1e-9 && Math.Abs(q[1]) < 1e-9 && q[2] < -1e-9) ||
                    (Math.Abs(q[0]) < 1e-9 && Math.Abs(q[1]) < 1e-9 && Math.Abs(q[2]) < 1e-9 && q[3] < -1e-9))
                {
                    q[0] = -q[0]; q[1] = -q[1]; q[2] = -q[2]; q[3] = -q[3];
                }

                string key = $"{Math.Round(q[0],3)},{Math.Round(q[1],3)},{Math.Round(q[2],3)},{Math.Round(q[3],3)}";
                if (seen.Add(key))
                    result.Add(q);
            }
        }

        return result;
    }

    static double[] QuatMul(double[] a, double[] b)
    {
        return new double[] {
            a[0]*b[0] - a[1]*b[1] - a[2]*b[2] - a[3]*b[3],
            a[0]*b[1] + a[1]*b[0] + a[2]*b[3] - a[3]*b[2],
            a[0]*b[2] - a[1]*b[3] + a[2]*b[0] + a[3]*b[1],
            a[0]*b[3] + a[1]*b[2] - a[2]*b[1] + a[3]*b[0]
        };
    }
}

Test 13: Longest Common Substring (C++) Score: 0.00 / 12
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the Longest Common Substring problem in C++.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY problem size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 2-3 short strings (10-50 chars each)
- **Medium**: 3-5 medium strings (100-500 chars each)
- **Large**: 5-10 long strings (1000-5000 chars each)
- **Extreme**: 100 very long strings (10MB+ each), massive search space

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N (number of strings)
Next N lines: one string per line

**Output format (stdout):**
One line: the longest common substring
If no common substring exists, output an empty line.

**Example:**
Input:
3
abcdef
xbcdey
zbcdew

Output:
bcde

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on number and length of strings
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 300 seconds even for very long strings
3. **Correctness**: Must find the actual longest common substring

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on problem scale.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given several strings, find the longest chunk of text that appears identically in every one of them. For example, if the strings are “abcdef” and “xbcdey”, the answer is “bcde”.

Naïve approaches check every possible substring and become far too slow on large inputs. Subpasses increase the string lengths into the millions, so the AI must use efficient data structures or hashing. The baseline uses a simple library call that times out on the bigger cases.

 0.0000
[3 short strings (7 chars)] Invalid: Result must be string, got dict

Longest Common Substring - 3 short strings (7 chars)

View Code (4148 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <string>
#include <vector>
#include <unordered_set>
#include <algorithm>

using namespace std;
using ll = long long;

static const ll MOD1 = 1000000007LL;
static const ll MOD2 = 998244353LL;
static const ll BASE1 = 131;
static const ll BASE2 = 137;

int main() {
    ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int n;
    cin >> n;
    cin.ignore();

    vector<string> strings(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        getline(cin, strings[i]);
    }

    if (n == 0) {
        cout << "\n";
        return 0;
    }
    if (n == 1) {
        cout << strings[0] << "\n";
        return 0;
    }

    sort(strings.begin(), strings.end(), [](const string& a, const string& b) {
        return a.size() < b.size();
    });

    int maxLen = (int)strings[0].size();
    if (maxLen == 0) {
        cout << "\n";
        return 0;
    }

    string result;
    int lo = 1, hi = maxLen;

    while (lo <= hi) {
        int len = (lo + hi) / 2;

        ll pow1 = 1, pow2 = 1;
        for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) {
            pow1 = pow1 * BASE1 % MOD1;
            pow2 = pow2 * BASE2 % MOD2;
        }

        const string& s0 = strings[0];
        unordered_set<ll> current;
        current.reserve(s0.size() - len + 2);

        {
            ll h1 = 0, h2 = 0;
            for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) {
                h1 = (h1 * BASE1 + (unsigned char)s0[i]) % MOD1;
                h2 = (h2 * BASE2 + (unsigned char)s0[i]) % MOD2;
            }
            current.insert(h1 * MOD2 + h2);
            for (int i = len; i < (int)s0.size(); i++) {
                h1 = (h1 * BASE1 + (unsigned char)s0[i] - (unsigned char)s0[i - len] * pow1 % MOD1 + MOD1 * 2) % MOD1;
                h2 = (h2 * BASE2 + (unsigned char)s0[i] - (unsigned char)s0[i - len] * pow2 % MOD2 + MOD2 * 2) % MOD2;
                current.insert(h1 * MOD2 + h2);
            }
        }

        bool found = !current.empty();
        for (int si = 1; si < n && found; si++) {
            const string& s = strings[si];
            if (len > (int)s.size()) { found = false; break; }

            unordered_set<ll> next;
            ll h1 = 0, h2 = 0;
            for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) {
                h1 = (h1 * BASE1 + (unsigned char)s[i]) % MOD1;
                h2 = (h2 * BASE2 + (unsigned char)s[i]) % MOD2;
            }
            ll c = h1 * MOD2 + h2;
            if (current.count(c)) next.insert(c);
            for (int i = len; i < (int)s.size(); i++) {
                h1 = (h1 * BASE1 + (unsigned char)s[i] - (unsigned char)s[i - len] * pow1 % MOD1 + MOD1 * 2) % MOD1;
                h2 = (h2 * BASE2 + (unsigned char)s[i] - (unsigned char)s[i - len] * pow2 % MOD2 + MOD2 * 2) % MOD2;
                c = h1 * MOD2 + h2;
                if (current.count(c)) next.insert(c);
            }
            current = move(next);
            if (current.empty()) found = false;
        }

        if (found) {
            ll h1 = 0, h2 = 0;
            for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) {
                h1 = (h1 * BASE1 + (unsigned char)s0[i]) % MOD1;
                h2 = (h2 * BASE2 + (unsigned char)s0[i]) % MOD2;
            }
            if (current.count(h1 * MOD2 + h2)) {
                result = s0.substr(0, len);
            } else {
                for (int i = len; i < (int)s0.size(); i++) {
                    h1 = (h1 * BASE1 + (unsigned char)s0[i] - (unsigned char)s0[i - len] * pow1 % MOD1 + MOD1 * 2) % MOD1;
                    h2 = (h2 * BASE2 + (unsigned char)s0[i] - (unsigned char)s0[i - len] * pow2 % MOD2 + MOD2 * 2) % MOD2;
                    if (current.count(h1 * MOD2 + h2)) {
                        result = s0.substr(i - len + 1, len);
                        break;
                    }
                }
            }
            lo = len + 1;
        } else {
            hi = len - 1;
        }
    }

    if (!result.empty()) {
        bool valid = true;
        for (const auto& s : strings) {
            if (s.find(result) == string::npos) { valid = false; break; }
        }
        if (!valid) result.clear();
    }

    cout << result << "\n";
    return 0;
}
