Test 14: Minesweeper Solver (C#) Score: 1.00 / 10
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a Minesweeper solver in C# that must handle ANY board complexity from trivial to ludicrous scale.

- **Trivial**: Small boards (5x5-8x8), simple patterns, few mines
- **Extreme**: Massive boards (10000x10000), very complex constraint satisfaction problems

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: H W (height and width)
Next H lines: the board row strings (each W characters)

**Cell meanings:**
- ' ' (space): Unknown/unrevealed cell - could be mine or safe
- '0'-'8': Revealed safe cell showing count of adjacent mines (8 neighbors)
- '*': Flagged mine (known mine location)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: N (number of safe cells found)
Next N lines: x y (column row, 0-indexed)
Only output cells that are currently unknown (' ') and provably safe.
Do NOT guess - only return cells you can prove are safe.

**Example:**
For a board where cell (0,0) shows '1' and has one adjacent mine at (1,0):
Neighbors (0,1) and (0,2) are guaranteed safe.

**Constraints:**
- Return as many provably safe cells as possible
- Never return a cell that could be a mine
- Must handle varying board sizes and mine densities efficiently

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on board complexity.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a partially-revealed Minesweeper board, deduce which hidden cells are safe to click and which contain mines. Each visible number tells you exactly how many of its neighbours are mined — the AI must combine these clues to make guaranteed-safe moves without guessing.

Larger boards and denser mine layouts make the logic harder. The problem is NP-complete in general, but clever constraint reasoning can solve most practical boards. The baseline uses only the simplest single-cell deduction rules.

 1.0000
[8x8 board, 10 mines] Found: 27 safe, Baseline: 23, Total safe: 33, Time: 0.1s - excellent (≥ baseline)

Minesweeper Solver - 8x8 board, 10 mines

View Code (17227 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using System.Linq;

class MinesweeperSolver
{
    static int H, W;
    static char[,] board;
    static bool[,] knownSafe;
    static bool[,] knownMine;

    static readonly int[] dx = { -1, -1, -1, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1 };
    static readonly int[] dy = { -1, 0, 1, -1, 1, -1, 0, 1 };

    static void Main()
    {
        var input = Console.In.ReadToEnd().Replace("\r\n", "\n").Replace("\r", "\n");
        var lines = input.Split('\n');
        var hw = lines[0].Split(' ');
        H = int.Parse(hw[0]);
        W = int.Parse(hw[1]);
        board = new char[H, W];
        knownSafe = new bool[H, W];
        knownMine = new bool[H, W];

        for (int r = 0; r < H; r++)
        {
            string row = r + 1 < lines.Length ? lines[r + 1] : "";
            for (int c = 0; c < W; c++)
            {
                char ch = c < row.Length ? row[c] : ' ';
                board[r, c] = ch;
                if (ch >= '0' && ch <= '8') knownSafe[r, c] = true;
                else if (ch == '*') knownMine[r, c] = true;
            }
        }

        // Phase 1: Simple constraint propagation (works for all board sizes)
        bool changed = true;
        while (changed)
        {
            changed = false;
            for (int r = 0; r < H; r++)
            {
                for (int c = 0; c < W; c++)
                {
                    if (board[r, c] < '0' || board[r, c] > '8') continue;
                    int num = board[r, c] - '0';
                    var unknowns = new List<(int, int)>();
                    int mineCount = 0;
                    for (int d = 0; d < 8; d++)
                    {
                        int nr = r + dx[d], nc = c + dy[d];
                        if (nr < 0 || nr >= H || nc < 0 || nc >= W) continue;
                        if (knownMine[nr, nc]) mineCount++;
                        else if (!knownSafe[nr, nc]) unknowns.Add((nr, nc));
                    }
                    if (unknowns.Count == 0) continue;
                    int remaining = num - mineCount;
                    if (remaining == 0)
                    {
                        foreach (var (ur, uc) in unknowns)
                            if (!knownSafe[ur, uc]) { knownSafe[ur, uc] = true; changed = true; }
                    }
                    else if (remaining == unknowns.Count)
                    {
                        foreach (var (ur, uc) in unknowns)
                            if (!knownMine[ur, uc]) { knownMine[ur, uc] = true; changed = true; }
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        // Phase 2: Constraint subset reduction
        var frontierSet = new HashSet<(int, int)>();
        var constraints = BuildConstraints(frontierSet);

        if (frontierSet.Count > 0 && frontierSet.Count <= 2000)
        {
            bool cChanged = true;
            int iterations = 0;
            while (cChanged && iterations < 20)
            {
                cChanged = false;
                iterations++;
                int count = constraints.Count;
                for (int i = 0; i < count && constraints.Count < 100000; i++)
                {
                    for (int j = 0; j < count && constraints.Count < 100000; j++)
                    {
                        if (i == j) continue;
                        var (ci, mi) = constraints[i];
                        var (cj, mj) = constraints[j];
                        if (ci.Count == 0 || cj.Count == 0 || ci.Count >= cj.Count) continue;
                        if (ci.IsSubsetOf(cj))
                        {
                            var diff = new HashSet<(int, int)>(cj);
                            diff.ExceptWith(ci);
                            int diffMines = mj - mi;
                            if (diff.Count > 0 && diffMines >= 0)
                            {
                                bool isNew = true;
                                foreach (var (ec, em) in constraints)
                                {
                                    if (em == diffMines && ec.Count == diff.Count && ec.SetEquals(diff))
                                    { isNew = false; break; }
                                }
                                if (isNew)
                                {
                                    constraints.Add((diff, diffMines));
                                    cChanged = true;
                                }
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }

                // Apply deductions
                bool deduced = true;
                while (deduced)
                {
                    deduced = false;
                    foreach (var (cells, mines) in constraints)
                    {
                        if (cells.Count == 0) continue;
                        if (mines == 0)
                        {
                            foreach (var (cr, cc) in cells)
                                if (!knownSafe[cr, cc]) { knownSafe[cr, cc] = true; deduced = true; }
                        }
                        else if (mines == cells.Count)
                        {
                            foreach (var (cr, cc) in cells)
                                if (!knownMine[cr, cc]) { knownMine[cr, cc] = true; deduced = true; }
                        }
                    }
                    if (deduced)
                    {
                        for (int i = 0; i < constraints.Count; i++)
                        {
                            var (cells, mines) = constraints[i];
                            int removedMines = 0;
                            var toRemove = new List<(int, int)>();
                            foreach (var cell in cells)
                            {
                                if (knownSafe[cell.Item1, cell.Item2]) toRemove.Add(cell);
                                else if (knownMine[cell.Item1, cell.Item2]) { toRemove.Add(cell); removedMines++; }
                            }
                            foreach (var cell in toRemove) cells.Remove(cell);
                            constraints[i] = (cells, mines - removedMines);
                        }
                    }
                }
            }

            // Re-run simple propagation after subset deductions
            changed = true;
            while (changed)
            {
                changed = false;
                for (int r = 0; r < H; r++)
                {
                    for (int c = 0; c < W; c++)
                    {
                        if (board[r, c] < '0' || board[r, c] > '8') continue;
                        int num = board[r, c] - '0';
                        var unknowns = new List<(int, int)>();
                        int mineCount = 0;
                        for (int d = 0; d < 8; d++)
                        {
                            int nr = r + dx[d], nc = c + dy[d];
                            if (nr < 0 || nr >= H || nc < 0 || nc >= W) continue;
                            if (knownMine[nr, nc]) mineCount++;
                            else if (!knownSafe[nr, nc]) unknowns.Add((nr, nc));
                        }
                        if (unknowns.Count == 0) continue;
                        int remaining = num - mineCount;
                        if (remaining == 0)
                        {
                            foreach (var (ur, uc) in unknowns)
                                if (!knownSafe[ur, uc]) { knownSafe[ur, uc] = true; changed = true; }
                        }
                        else if (remaining == unknowns.Count)
                        {
                            foreach (var (ur, uc) in unknowns)
                                if (!knownMine[ur, uc]) { knownMine[ur, uc] = true; changed = true; }
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        // Phase 3: Backtracking enumeration on connected components
        frontierSet.Clear();
        var activeConstraints = BuildConstraints(frontierSet);

        // Remove already-resolved cells
        var remainingFrontier = new HashSet<(int, int)>();
        foreach (var f in frontierSet)
            if (!knownSafe[f.Item1, f.Item2] && !knownMine[f.Item1, f.Item2])
                remainingFrontier.Add(f);

        if (remainingFrontier.Count > 0 && remainingFrontier.Count <= 500)
        {
            // Rebuild constraints for remaining frontier only
            activeConstraints.Clear();
            for (int r = 0; r < H; r++)
            {
                for (int c = 0; c < W; c++)
                {
                    if (board[r, c] < '0' || board[r, c] > '8') continue;
                    int num = board[r, c] - '0';
                    var unknowns = new HashSet<(int, int)>();
                    int mineCount = 0;
                    for (int d = 0; d < 8; d++)
                    {
                        int nr = r + dx[d], nc = c + dy[d];
                        if (nr < 0 || nr >= H || nc < 0 || nc >= W) continue;
                        if (knownMine[nr, nc]) mineCount++;
                        else if (!knownSafe[nr, nc] && remainingFrontier.Contains((nr, nc)))
                            unknowns.Add((nr, nc));
                    }
                    int remaining = num - mineCount;
                    if (unknowns.Count > 0 && remaining >= 0)
                        activeConstraints.Add((unknowns, remaining));
                }
            }

            var cellToIdx = new Dictionary<(int, int), int>();
            var idxToCell = new List<(int, int)>();
            foreach (var f in remainingFrontier)
            {
                cellToIdx[f] = idxToCell.Count;
                idxToCell.Add(f);
            }

            int n = idxToCell.Count;
            if (n > 0)
            {
                // Find connected components via union-find
                var parent = new int[n];
                for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) parent[i] = i;
                int Find(int x) { while (parent[x] != x) { parent[x] = parent[parent[x]]; x = parent[x]; } return x; }
                void Union(int a, int b) { parent[Find(a)] = Find(b); }

                foreach (var (cells, _) in activeConstraints)
                {
                    int first = -1;
                    foreach (var cell in cells)
                    {
                        if (cellToIdx.TryGetValue(cell, out int idx))
                        {
                            if (first == -1) first = idx;
                            else Union(first, idx);
                        }
                    }
                }

                var components = new Dictionary<int, List<int>>();
                for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
                {
                    int root = Find(i);
                    if (!components.ContainsKey(root)) components[root] = new List<int>();
                    components[root].Add(i);
                }

                foreach (var comp in components.Values)
                {
                    if (comp.Count > 30) continue;
                    var compCells = comp.Select(i => idxToCell[i]).ToList();
                    var compCellSet = new HashSet<(int, int)>(compCells);
                    var compCellToIdx = new Dictionary<(int, int), int>();
                    for (int i = 0; i < compCells.Count; i++)
                        compCellToIdx[compCells[i]] = i;

                    var compConstraints = new List<(HashSet<(int, int)> cells, int mines)>();
                    foreach (var (cells, mines) in activeConstraints)
                    {
                        var inter = new HashSet<(int, int)>(cells);
                        inter.IntersectWith(compCellSet);
                        if (inter.Count > 0)
                        {
                            int interMines = mines;
                            foreach (var cell in cells)
                                if (!compCellSet.Contains(cell) && knownMine[cell.Item1, cell.Item2])
                                    interMines--;
                            compConstraints.Add((inter, mines));
                        }
                    }

                    var canBeSafe = new bool[compCells.Count];
                    var canBeMine = new bool[compCells.Count];
                    EnumerateAll(compCells, compCellToIdx, compConstraints, canBeSafe, canBeMine);
                    for (int i = 0; i < compCells.Count; i++)
                    {
                        if (canBeSafe[i] && !canBeMine[i])
                            knownSafe[compCells[i].Item1, compCells[i].Item2] = true;
                        if (canBeMine[i] && !canBeSafe[i])
                            knownMine[compCells[i].Item1, compCells[i].Item2] = true;
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        var results = new List<(int, int)>();
        for (int r = 0; r < H; r++)
            for (int c = 0; c < W; c++)
                if (board[r, c] == ' ' && knownSafe[r, c])
                    results.Add((c, r));

        var sb = new System.Text.StringBuilder();
        sb.AppendLine(results.Count.ToString());
        foreach (var (x, y) in results)
            sb.AppendLine(x + " " + y);
        Console.Write(sb);
    }

    static List<(HashSet<(int, int)> cells, int mines)> BuildConstraints(HashSet<(int, int)> frontierSet)
    {
        var constraints = new List<(HashSet<(int, int)> cells, int mines)>();
        for (int r = 0; r < H; r++)
        {
            for (int c = 0; c < W; c++)
            {
                if (board[r, c] < '0' || board[r, c] > '8') continue;
                int num = board[r, c] - '0';
                var unknowns = new HashSet<(int, int)>();
                int mineCount = 0;
                for (int d = 0; d < 8; d++)
                {
                    int nr = r + dx[d], nc = c + dy[d];
                    if (nr < 0 || nr >= H || nc < 0 || nc >= W) continue;
                    if (knownMine[nr, nc]) mineCount++;
                    else if (!knownSafe[nr, nc]) unknowns.Add((nr, nc));
                }
                int remaining = num - mineCount;
                if (unknowns.Count > 0 && remaining >= 0)
                {
                    constraints.Add((unknowns, remaining));
                    foreach (var u in unknowns) frontierSet.Add(u);
                }
            }
        }
        return constraints;
    }

    static void EnumerateAll(List<(int, int)> cells, Dictionary<(int, int), int> cellToIdx,
        List<(HashSet<(int, int)> cells, int mines)> constraints, bool[] canBeSafe, bool[] canBeMine)
    {
        int n = cells.Count;
        var idxConstraints = new List<(int[] indices, int mines)>();
        foreach (var (cCells, mines) in constraints)
        {
            var indices = new List<int>();
            foreach (var c in cCells)
                if (cellToIdx.TryGetValue(c, out int idx))
                    indices.Add(idx);
            if (indices.Count > 0)
                idxConstraints.Add((indices.ToArray(), mines));
        }

        var assignment = new int[n]; // 0=unassigned, 1=safe, 2=mine

        // Precompute which constraints involve each cell for faster pruning
        var cellConstraints = new List<int>[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) cellConstraints[i] = new List<int>();
        for (int ci = 0; ci < idxConstraints.Count; ci++)
            foreach (int idx in idxConstraints[ci].indices)
                cellConstraints[idx].Add(ci);

        void Backtrack(int pos)
        {
            if (pos == n)
            {
                for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
                {
                    if (assignment[i] == 1) canBeSafe[i] = true;
                    else canBeMine[i] = true;
                }
                return;
            }

            for (int val = 1; val <= 2; val++)
            {
                assignment[pos] = val;
                bool ok = true;
                foreach (int ci in cellConstraints[pos])
                {
                    var (indices, mines) = idxConstraints[ci];
                    int mc = 0, unassigned = 0;
                    foreach (int idx in indices)
                    {
                        if (assignment[idx] == 2) mc++;
                        else if (assignment[idx] == 0) unassigned++;
                    }
                    if (mc > mines || mc + unassigned < mines) { ok = false; break; }
                }
                if (ok) Backtrack(pos + 1);
                if (canBeSafe[pos] && canBeMine[pos])
                {
                    // Early termination: this cell already determined to be both
                    // Check if ALL cells already have both set
                    bool allDetermined = true;
                    for (int i = pos + 1; i < n; i++)
                        if (!canBeSafe[i] || !canBeMine[i]) { allDetermined = false; break; }
                    if (allDetermined) { assignment[pos] = 0; return; }
                }
            }
            assignment[pos] = 0;
        }

        Backtrack(0);
    }
}

Test 15: Tetrahedron Shadow Covering (Rust) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a Shadow Covering problem with tetrahedrons in Rust.

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with polygons ranging from simple shapes to very complex geometries.
The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: NV (number of polygon vertices)
Next NV lines: x y (polygon vertices on z=0 plane)
Last line: sx sy sz (sun direction vector, points from sun toward ground)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: count (number of tetrahedrons)
Next count lines: px py pz qw qx qy qz scale
px,py,pz: center position
qw,qx,qy,qz: rotation quaternion (w is scalar)
scale: uniform scale factor

**Standard Tetrahedron Reference:**
Vertices (before transform):
[1.0, 0.0, -0.707]
[-1.0, 0.0, -0.707]
[0.0, 1.0, 0.707]
[0.0, -1.0, 0.707]

**Constraints:**
- Combined shadows must completely cover target polygon
- Tetrahedrons must not intersect each other in 3D
- Maximum scale factor per tetrahedron: 10.0
- Minimize number of tetrahedrons

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**

Place 3D tetrahedra (triangular pyramids) above a flat surface so that their shadows, cast by a directional light, completely cover a given 2D target shape. Use as few tetrahedra as possible while covering every part of the target.

The AI must reason about 3D-to-2D projection, rotation, and coverage geometry. Subpasses use more complex target shapes and trickier light angles. The baseline uses a simple grid of tetrahedra above the target.

Test 16: Job-Shop Scheduling (C++) Score: 11.00 / 14
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a Job-Shop Scheduling problem in C++.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY problem size from trivial to extremely large scale:
- **Trivial**: 2-3 jobs, 2-3 machines, 5-10 tasks total
- **Extreme**: 1000+ jobs, 50+ machines, 50k+ tasks total

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: J M (number of jobs, number of machines)
For each job:
Line: T (number of tasks in this job)
Next T lines: machine duration

**Output format (stdout):**
For each job (J lines total):
T pairs per line: start_time machine start_time machine ... (all tasks for that job)

**Example:**
Input:
2 2
2
0 3
1 2
2
1 2
0 4

Output:
0 0 3 1
0 1 5 0

(Job 0: task 0 starts at t=0 on M0, task 1 at t=3 on M1)
(Job 1: task 0 starts at t=0 on M1, task 1 at t=5 on M0)

**Key Constraints:**
1. Each task in a job must run on its specified machine
2. Tasks within a job must execute in order (task i before task i+1)
3. A machine can only run one task at a time
4. Tasks cannot be interrupted once started

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on problem scale.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Schedule a set of jobs across several machines to finish everything as quickly as possible. Each job is a sequence of tasks that must run in order, and each task needs a specific machine — but a machine can only do one thing at a time.

This classic factory-scheduling problem is NP-hard. Subpasses increase the number of jobs and machines. The baseline greedily starts whichever task can begin earliest.

 1.0000
[3 jobs, 2 machines] Makespan: 9, Baseline: 12, Time: 0.9s - excellent (≤ baseline)

Job-Shop Scheduling - 3 jobs, 2 machines

View Code (10046 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <algorithm>
#include <queue>
#include <chrono>
#include <random>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <cmath>

using namespace std;
using namespace chrono;

static int J, M, N;
static vector<int> gJ, gT, gM, gD, oO;
static vector<vector<pair<int,int>>> jobs;

int computeSchedule(vector<vector<int>>& mo, vector<int>& st) {
    st.assign(N, 0);
    vector<int> inD(N, 0), mp(N, -1), mn(N, -1);
    for (int m = 0; m < M; m++) {
        for (int i = 1; i < (int)mo[m].size(); i++) {
            mp[mo[m][i]] = mo[m][i-1];
            inD[mo[m][i]]++;
        }
        for (int i = 0; i+1 < (int)mo[m].size(); i++)
            mn[mo[m][i]] = mo[m][i+1];
    }
    for (int j = 0; j < J; j++)
        for (int t = 1; t < (int)jobs[j].size(); t++)
            inD[oO[j]+t]++;

    queue<int> q;
    for (int i = 0; i < N; i++)
        if (!inD[i]) q.push(i);

    int ms = 0, cnt = 0;
    while (!q.empty()) {
        int u = q.front(); q.pop(); cnt++;
        int s = 0;
        if (gT[u] > 0) {
            int p = oO[gJ[u]] + gT[u] - 1;
            s = st[p] + gD[p];
        }
        if (mp[u] >= 0)
            s = max(s, st[mp[u]] + gD[mp[u]]);
        st[u] = s;
        ms = max(ms, s + gD[u]);
        if (gT[u]+1 < (int)jobs[gJ[u]].size()) {
            int nx = oO[gJ[u]] + gT[u] + 1;
            if (--inD[nx] == 0) q.push(nx);
        }
        if (mn[u] >= 0 && --inD[mn[u]] == 0)
            q.push(mn[u]);
    }
    return cnt == N ? ms : -1;
}

vector<pair<int,int>> findSwaps(vector<vector<int>>& mo, vector<int>& st) {
    vector<int> mp(N, -1), mn(N, -1);
    for (int m = 0; m < M; m++) {
        for (int i = 1; i < (int)mo[m].size(); i++)
            mp[mo[m][i]] = mo[m][i-1];
        for (int i = 0; i+1 < (int)mo[m].size(); i++)
            mn[mo[m][i]] = mo[m][i+1];
    }

    int ms = 0, last = -1;
    for (int i = 0; i < N; i++)
        if (st[i] + gD[i] > ms) { ms = st[i] + gD[i]; last = i; }
    if (last < 0) return {};

    vector<int> path;
    int cur = last;
    while (cur >= 0) {
        path.push_back(cur);
        int nxt = -1;
        if (mp[cur] >= 0 && st[mp[cur]] + gD[mp[cur]] == st[cur])
            nxt = mp[cur];
        else if (gT[cur] > 0) {
            int p = oO[gJ[cur]] + gT[cur] - 1;
            if (st[p] + gD[p] == st[cur]) nxt = p;
        }
        cur = nxt;
    }
    reverse(path.begin(), path.end());

    vector<pair<int,int>> swaps;
    int bs = 0;
    for (int i = 1; i <= (int)path.size(); i++) {
        bool same = false;
        if (i < (int)path.size()) {
            int u = path[i-1], v = path[i];
            if (gM[u] == gM[v] && mn[u] == v) same = true;
        }
        if (!same) {
            int be = i - 1, blen = be - bs + 1;
            if (blen >= 2) {
                int u = path[bs], v = path[bs+1];
                if (gJ[u] != gJ[v]) swaps.push_back({u, v});
                if (blen >= 3) {
                    u = path[be-1]; v = path[be];
                    if (gJ[u] != gJ[v]) swaps.push_back({u, v});
                }
            }
            bs = i;
        }
    }
    return swaps;
}

vector<vector<int>> buildGreedy(int rule, mt19937* rng = nullptr) {
    vector<vector<int>> mo(M);
    vector<int> jnxt(J, 0), jrdy(J, 0), mrdy(M, 0), rw(J, 0);
    for (int j = 0; j < J; j++)
        for (auto& p : jobs[j]) rw[j] += p.second;

    for (int it = 0; it < N; it++) {
        struct C { int j, s; long long p; };
        vector<C> cs;
        int minS = INT_MAX;

        for (int j = 0; j < J; j++) {
            int t = jnxt[j];
            if (t >= (int)jobs[j].size()) continue;
            int m = jobs[j][t].first, d = jobs[j][t].second;
            int s = max(jrdy[j], mrdy[m]);
            minS = min(minS, s);
            long long pr;
            switch(rule) {
                case 0: pr = d; break;
                case 1: pr = -d; break;
                case 2: pr = -(long long)rw[j]; break;
                case 3: pr = rw[j]; break;
                case 4: pr = j; break;
                case 5: pr = -(long long)((int)jobs[j].size()-t); break;
                default: pr = 0; break;
            }
            cs.push_back({j, s, pr});
        }

        int bi = 0;
        if (rng && (int)cs.size() > 1) {
            vector<int> el;
            for (int i = 0; i < (int)cs.size(); i++)
                if (cs[i].s <= minS) el.push_back(i);
            sort(el.begin(), el.end(), [&](int a, int b){ return cs[a].p < cs[b].p; });
            int k = min((int)el.size(), 3);
            bi = el[(*rng)() % k];
        } else {
            for (int i = 1; i < (int)cs.size(); i++)
                if (cs[i].s < cs[bi].s || (cs[i].s == cs[bi].s && cs[i].p < cs[bi].p))
                    bi = i;
        }

        int j = cs[bi].j, t = jnxt[j];
        int m = jobs[j][t].first, d = jobs[j][t].second;
        int s = max(jrdy[j], mrdy[m]);
        mo[m].push_back(oO[j]+t);
        jnxt[j]++; jrdy[j] = s+d; mrdy[m] = s+d; rw[j] -= d;
    }
    return mo;
}

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);
    auto t0 = steady_clock::now();
    auto ems = [&]() -> long long {
        return duration_cast<milliseconds>(steady_clock::now()-t0).count();
    };

    cin >> J >> M;
    jobs.resize(J);
    N = 0;
    for (int j = 0; j < J; j++) {
        int T; cin >> T;
        jobs[j].resize(T);
        for (int t = 0; t < T; t++)
            cin >> jobs[j][t].first >> jobs[j][t].second;
        N += T;
    }

    if (N == 0) {
        for (int j = 0; j < J; j++) cout << '\n';
        return 0;
    }

    oO.resize(J, 0);
    for (int j = 1; j < J; j++) oO[j] = oO[j-1]+(int)jobs[j-1].size();
    gJ.resize(N); gT.resize(N); gM.resize(N); gD.resize(N);
    for (int j = 0; j < J; j++)
        for (int t = 0; t < (int)jobs[j].size(); t++) {
            int id = oO[j]+t;
            gJ[id]=j; gT[id]=t; gM[id]=jobs[j][t].first; gD[id]=jobs[j][t].second;
        }

    vector<vector<int>> bestMO;
    vector<int> bestSt;
    int bestMs = INT_MAX;

    for (int r = 0; r <= 6; r++) {
        auto mo = buildGreedy(r);
        vector<int> st;
        int ms = computeSchedule(mo, st);
        if (ms >= 0 && ms < bestMs) { bestMs = ms; bestMO = mo; bestSt = st; }
    }

    long long tLim;
    if (N <= 50) tLim = 1000;
    else if (N <= 500) tLim = 3000;
    else if (N <= 5000) tLim = 8000;
    else tLim = 25000;

    {
        auto mo = bestMO;
        auto st = bestSt;
        int ms = bestMs;
        int tenure = max(7, min((int)sqrt((double)N), 50));

        unordered_map<long long, int> tabu;
        auto tk = [](int u, int v) -> long long {
            return (long long)min(u,v)*200001LL + max(u,v);
        };

        vector<int> posM(N);
        for (int m = 0; m < M; m++)
            for (int i = 0; i < (int)mo[m].size(); i++)
                posM[mo[m][i]] = i;

        long long tabuLim = ems() + (tLim - ems()) * 7 / 10;
        int noImpr = 0, maxNoImpr = min(3000, max(100, N*2));

        for (int iter = 0; ems() < tabuLim && noImpr < maxNoImpr; iter++) {
            auto swaps = findSwaps(mo, st);
            if (swaps.empty()) break;

            int bestI = -1, bestNewMs = INT_MAX;
            vector<int> bestNewSt;

            for (int i = 0; i < (int)swaps.size(); i++) {
                auto [u,v] = swaps[i];
                if (gJ[u] == gJ[v]) continue;
                int m2 = gM[u];
                int pu = posM[u], pv = posM[v];

                swap(mo[m2][pu], mo[m2][pv]);
                vector<int> tst;
                int tms = computeSchedule(mo, tst);
                swap(mo[m2][pu], mo[m2][pv]);

                if (tms < 0) continue;
                bool isTabu = tabu.count(tk(u,v)) && tabu[tk(u,v)] > iter;
                bool aspire = tms < bestMs;

                if (!isTabu || aspire) {
                    if (tms < bestNewMs) {
                        bestI = i; bestNewMs = tms; bestNewSt = tst;
                    }
                }
            }

            if (bestI < 0) break;

            auto [u,v] = swaps[bestI];
            int m2 = gM[u];
            int pu = posM[u], pv = posM[v];
            swap(mo[m2][pu], mo[m2][pv]);
            posM[u] = pv; posM[v] = pu;
            ms = bestNewMs;
            st = bestNewSt;
            tabu[tk(u,v)] = iter + tenure;

            if (ms < bestMs) {
                bestMs = ms; bestMO = mo; bestSt = st;
                noImpr = 0;
            } else {
                noImpr++;
            }
        }
    }

    mt19937 rng(42);
    while (ems() < tLim - 200) {
        int rule = (int)(rng() % 7);
        auto mo = buildGreedy(rule, &rng);
        vector<int> st;
        int ms = computeSchedule(mo, st);
        if (ms < 0) continue;

        long long hcLim = min(ems() + 500, tLim - 100);
        bool improved = true;
        while (improved && ems() < hcLim) {
            improved = false;
            auto swaps = findSwaps(mo, st);
            vector<int> pm(N);
            for (int m = 0; m < M; m++)
                for (int i = 0; i < (int)mo[m].size(); i++)
                    pm[mo[m][i]] = i;

            for (auto [u,v] : swaps) {
                if (gJ[u] == gJ[v]) continue;
                int m2 = gM[u];
                int pu = pm[u], pv = pm[v];
                swap(mo[m2][pu], mo[m2][pv]);
                vector<int> tst;
                int tms = computeSchedule(mo, tst);
                if (tms >= 0 && tms < ms) {
                    ms = tms; st = tst;
                    pm[u] = pv; pm[v] = pu;
                    improved = true;
                    break;
                }
                swap(mo[m2][pu], mo[m2][pv]);
            }
        }

        if (ms < bestMs) {
            bestMs = ms; bestMO = mo; bestSt = st;
        }
    }

    for (int j = 0; j < J; j++) {
        for (int t = 0; t < (int)jobs[j].size(); t++) {
            if (t) cout << ' ';
            cout << bestSt[oO[j]+t] << ' ' << jobs[j][t].first;
        }
        cout << '\n';
    }
    return 0;
}

Test 17: 3D AABB Bin Packing (C#) Score: 0.00 / 8
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a 3D Bin Packing problem with axis-aligned boxes in C#.

Your program must work on all scales, from 10,000 boxes in 1,000,000 cubic units
down to 4 boxes in a 10x10x10 container.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N W H D (number of boxes, container width, height, depth)
Next N lines: w h d (box dimensions)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: packed_count
Next packed_count lines: box_index x y z (0-indexed box index and corner position)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on container size and number of boxes
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for very large containers
3. **Quality**: Maximize number of boxes packed while ensuring valid placements

**Constraints:**
- Boxes must be entirely within container bounds
- No two boxes may overlap
- Boxes are axis-aligned (no arbitrary rotation, but you may reorder w/h/d)
- Maximize number of boxes packed

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Pack a collection of rectangular boxes into a shipping container, fitting as much total volume in as possible. Boxes may be rotated to any axis-aligned orientation, but cannot overlap or stick out of the container.

This 3D packing problem is NP-hard. Subpasses increase the number and variety of box sizes. The baseline uses a greedy first-fit approach.

 0.0000
[4 boxes in 10x10x10] Packed: 3/4, Baseline: 4, Time: 0.2s - failed

3D AABB Packing - 4 boxes in 10x10x10

View Code (8123 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;

class Program
{
    static int ContW, ContH, ContD;

    struct Box
    {
        public int Index, W, H, D;
        public long Volume => (long)W * H * D;
    }

    struct Placement
    {
        public int Index, X, Y, Z, W, H, D;
    }

    struct Point : IComparable<Point>
    {
        public int X, Y, Z;
        public int CompareTo(Point o)
        {
            int c = Y.CompareTo(o.Y);
            if (c != 0) return c;
            c = X.CompareTo(o.X);
            if (c != 0) return c;
            return Z.CompareTo(o.Z);
        }
    }

    static int cellSize;
    static int gridNX, gridNY, gridNZ;
    static List<int>[] grid;

    static void InitGrid()
    {
        long vol = (long)ContW * ContH * ContD;
        cellSize = Math.Max(1, (int)Math.Cbrt(vol / 5000.0));
        if (cellSize < 1) cellSize = 1;
        gridNX = (ContW + cellSize - 1) / cellSize;
        gridNY = (ContH + cellSize - 1) / cellSize;
        gridNZ = (ContD + cellSize - 1) / cellSize;
        long total = (long)gridNX * gridNY * gridNZ;
        if (total > 8_000_000)
        {
            cellSize = (int)Math.Cbrt((double)vol / 8_000_000) + 1;
            gridNX = (ContW + cellSize - 1) / cellSize;
            gridNY = (ContH + cellSize - 1) / cellSize;
            gridNZ = (ContD + cellSize - 1) / cellSize;
            total = (long)gridNX * gridNY * gridNZ;
        }
        grid = new List<int>[total];
    }

    static List<Placement> placements = new List<Placement>();

    static void AddToGrid(int idx)
    {
        var p = placements[idx];
        int x0 = p.X / cellSize, x1 = (p.X + p.W - 1) / cellSize;
        int y0 = p.Y / cellSize, y1 = (p.Y + p.H - 1) / cellSize;
        int z0 = p.Z / cellSize, z1 = (p.Z + p.D - 1) / cellSize;
        for (int gx = x0; gx <= x1; gx++)
            for (int gy = y0; gy <= y1; gy++)
                for (int gz = z0; gz <= z1; gz++)
                {
                    long gi = (long)gx * gridNY * gridNZ + (long)gy * gridNZ + gz;
                    if (grid[gi] == null) grid[gi] = new List<int>();
                    grid[gi].Add(idx);
                }
    }

    static bool Overlaps(int x, int y, int z, int w, int h, int d)
    {
        int x0 = x / cellSize, x1 = (x + w - 1) / cellSize;
        int y0 = y / cellSize, y1 = (y + h - 1) / cellSize;
        int z0 = z / cellSize, z1 = (z + d - 1) / cellSize;
        for (int gx = x0; gx <= x1; gx++)
            for (int gy = y0; gy <= y1; gy++)
                for (int gz = z0; gz <= z1; gz++)
                {
                    long gi = (long)gx * gridNY * gridNZ + (long)gy * gridNZ + gz;
                    var cell = grid[gi];
                    if (cell == null) continue;
                    for (int i = 0; i < cell.Count; i++)
                    {
                        var p = placements[cell[i]];
                        if (x < p.X + p.W && x + w > p.X &&
                            y < p.Y + p.H && y + h > p.Y &&
                            z < p.Z + p.D && z + d > p.Z)
                            return true;
                    }
                }
        return false;
    }

    static void Main()
    {
        var firstLine = Console.ReadLine().Trim().Split();
        int N = int.Parse(firstLine[0]);
        ContW = int.Parse(firstLine[1]);
        ContH = int.Parse(firstLine[2]);
        ContD = int.Parse(firstLine[3]);

        var boxes = new Box[N];
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++)
        {
            var parts = Console.ReadLine().Trim().Split();
            int bw = int.Parse(parts[0]);
            int bh = int.Parse(parts[1]);
            int bd = int.Parse(parts[2]);
            boxes[i] = new Box { Index = i, W = bw, H = bh, D = bd };
        }

        InitGrid();

        var sortedIndices = Enumerable.Range(0, N)
            .OrderByDescending(i => boxes[i].Volume)
            .ThenByDescending(i => Math.Max(boxes[i].W, Math.Max(boxes[i].H, boxes[i].D)))
            .ToArray();

        var eps = new SortedSet<Point>();
        eps.Add(new Point { X = 0, Y = 0, Z = 0 });

        bool[] packed = new bool[N];
        int packedCount = 0;

        int maxEPs = N < 100 ? 2000 : (N < 1000 ? 800 : 400);

        foreach (int si in sortedIndices)
        {
            var box = boxes[si];
            if (box.W > ContW && box.W > ContH && box.W > ContD &&
                box.H > ContW && box.H > ContH && box.H > ContD &&
                box.D > ContW && box.D > ContH && box.D > ContD)
                continue;

            int[] dims = { box.W, box.H, box.D };
            Array.Sort(dims);
            var orientations = new HashSet<(int, int, int)>();
            orientations.Add((dims[0], dims[1], dims[2]));
            orientations.Add((dims[0], dims[2], dims[1]));
            orientations.Add((dims[1], dims[0], dims[2]));
            orientations.Add((dims[1], dims[2], dims[0]));
            orientations.Add((dims[2], dims[0], dims[1]));
            orientations.Add((dims[2], dims[1], dims[0]));

            var validOrients = new List<(int w, int h, int d)>();
            foreach (var (ow, oh, od) in orientations)
            {
                if (ow <= ContW && oh <= ContH && od <= ContD)
                    validOrients.Add((ow, oh, od));
            }
            if (validOrients.Count == 0) continue;

            validOrients.Sort((a, b) => a.h.CompareTo(b.h));

            bool placed = false;
            Point bestPt = default;
            int bestW = 0, bestH = 0, bestD = 0;
            long bestScore = long.MaxValue;

            foreach (var ep in eps)
            {
                if (placed && ep.Y > bestPt.Y) break;

                foreach (var (ow, oh, od) in validOrients)
                {
                    if (ep.X + ow > ContW || ep.Y + oh > ContH || ep.Z + od > ContD)
                        continue;

                    if (!Overlaps(ep.X, ep.Y, ep.Z, ow, oh, od))
                    {
                        long score = (long)ep.Y * 1000000L + (long)ep.X * 1000L + ep.Z;
                        if (score < bestScore)
                        {
                            bestScore = score;
                            bestPt = ep;
                            bestW = ow; bestH = oh; bestD = od;
                            placed = true;
                        }
                        break;
                    }
                }
            }

            if (placed)
            {
                var pl = new Placement
                {
                    Index = si,
                    X = bestPt.X, Y = bestPt.Y, Z = bestPt.Z,
                    W = bestW, H = bestH, D = bestD
                };
                int pidx = placements.Count;
                placements.Add(pl);
                AddToGrid(pidx);
                packed[si] = true;
                packedCount++;

                eps.Remove(bestPt);

                var newPts = new Point[]
                {
                    new Point { X = bestPt.X + bestW, Y = bestPt.Y, Z = bestPt.Z },
                    new Point { X = bestPt.X, Y = bestPt.Y + bestH, Z = bestPt.Z },
                    new Point { X = bestPt.X, Y = bestPt.Y, Z = bestPt.Z + bestD }
                };

                foreach (var np in newPts)
                {
                    if (np.X < ContW && np.Y < ContH && np.Z < ContD)
                        eps.Add(np);
                }

                if (eps.Count > maxEPs)
                {
                    var keep = eps.Take(maxEPs).ToList();
                    eps.Clear();
                    foreach (var p in keep) eps.Add(p);
                }
            }
        }

        var output = new System.Text.StringBuilder();
        output.AppendLine(packedCount.ToString());
        foreach (var p in placements)
        {
            output.Append(p.Index); output.Append(' ');
            output.Append(p.X); output.Append(' ');
            output.Append(p.Y); output.Append(' ');
            output.AppendLine(p.Z.ToString());
        }
        Console.Write(output);
    }
}

Test 18: Minimum Cut Problem (C++) Score: 0.00 / 9
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a C++ program to solve the Minimum Edge Cut problem.

**Target environment specs**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler information**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY graph size from trivial to multi-gb.

**The Challenge:**
Your C++ program will be tested with graphs ranging from small toy examples to massive real-world networks.
The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Problem:**
Given an undirected connected graph, find the minimum number of edges to remove
such that the graph becomes disconnected (split into two non-empty components).

**Input format (stdin):**
- Line 1: N M (number of nodes, number of edges)
- Lines 2 to M+1: u v (undirected edge between nodes u and v, 0-indexed)

**Output format (stdout):**
- Line 1: K (number of edges in the minimum cut)
- Lines 2 to K+1: u v (each edge in the cut, in any order)

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with this compiler, and run on this environment.
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle graphs even if they don't fit in memory.
4. Complete as fast as possible.
5. Output must be a valid cut that disconnects the graph
6. Minimize the number of cut edges
7. Must handle varying graph sizes efficiently

**Example:**
Input:
```
4 5
0 1
1 2
2 3
3 0
0 2
```

Output (one possible answer):
```
2
0 1
3 0
```

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different algorithms based on graph scale.

Subpasses increase the graph size from small networks to very large ones. The AI must implement an efficient algorithm; naïve approaches are far too slow on the bigger inputs.

Test 19: 3D Voxel Mining (C#) Score: 0.00 / 13
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a C# program to solve a mining problem in a simplified 3D voxel space.

**Constraints:**
1. Can only dig dirt that is exposed from above or a side.
2. Dug dirt must be dumped somewhere - at a location not blocking future digging
3. Maximum uphill slope - 1 Z voxel per X/Y voxel.
4. Dumped dirt becomes a new obstacle (plan carefully!)
5. Goal is to get valuable ore to a processing plant located at x=0, y=0
6. Ground is stable and solid, and you can dig under any voxel without fear of it collapsing
so long as it's connected to the earth. If you disconnect a voxel(s), it will collapse.

The voxel grid size has 10x more precision in Z than X/Y. So 10x10x100 is a cube.

**Input header format (stdin - space delimited. Ends with newline):**
```
XVoxelCount YVoxelCount ZVoxelCount XBlockSize YBlockSize ZBlockSize
```
Counts are human readable integers. BlockSize are human readable decimal numbers.

After the newline, voxels then follow in bytes, like this python psuedocode:
```
for x in range(XVoxelCount):
for y in range(YVoxelCount):
for z in range(ZVoxelCount):
d = readByte(stdin)
massInTonnes = d % 16
valuePerTonne = d // 16
addVoxel(d)
```

Each peice of dirt needs to be moved to its new location - you need to trace the
path in X and Y (4-connected / manhatten steps) and ensure that the path doesn't climb uphill
or downhill more than 1 Z voxel for every X/Y voxel.

**Output format (stdout / newline seperated):**
```
M (number of operations)
N digX digY digZ stepX1 stepY1 stepX2 stepY2 (N steps) destX destY (for each operation)
```

If dumping valuable ore at the processing plant, the voxel ceases to exist. If a voxel is
dumped elsewhere, it is dumped on top of the ground, raising the ground by 1 voxel. It can
be dug again and moved later if required.

Every unit of mass requires $1 of fuel to move one voxel in X or Y when flat, or $5 of fuel
to go up a 10% slope. Downhill is free.

Getting valuable ore to your processing plant pays $1000 per unit of mass per unit of value.
The most valuable ore (#15) pays $15,000 per ton, so the most a single dump truck load can gross
is $225,000.

(Dumping junk ore at the processing plant causes damage to the plant, costing $1000 per ton.)

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with .NET/csc
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. World size could be anything from 10x10x10 to 5000x5000x5000, your code should handle both extremes.
4. Your code should complete in under 5 minutes
5. Maximize profit.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14


Write complete, compilable C# code with a Main method.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on problem scale.

The AI must balance revenue against haulage costs, respect slope constraints for road access, and avoid dumping waste where it blocks future digging. Subpasses increase the pit size and complexity.

Test 20: Drillhole Data Validation (Rust) Score: 8.29 / 14
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a Rust program to validate drillhole assay data.


**The Challenge:**
Your Rust program will be tested with drillhole datasets ranging from small exploration projects to
massive mining surveys. The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales from 10 holes to
200,000 holes.

**Problem:**
Given drillhole data from a mining exploration project, identify samples that likely contain typos or
measurement errors. These outliers don't correlate well with spatially neighboring data points.

**Target environment specs**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler information**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

**Drillhole Data:**
- Each hole has a 3D path: start point (x,y,z) + direction vector + length
- Samples are taken at intervals along each hole
- Each sample has measurements for multiple properties

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_holes num_properties
property_name_1 property_name_2 ...
hole_id start_x start_y start_z dir_x dir_y dir_z length num_samples
depth prop1 prop2 ...
depth prop1 prop2 ...
etc.
hole_id start_x start_y start_z dir_x dir_y dir_z length num_samples
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
M (number of suspect entries found)
hole_id sample_idx property_name confidence (for each suspect, sorted by confidence desc)
```

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with rustc and only the standard rust library.
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle up to thousands of holes with thousands of samples each
4. Complete within 5 minutes
5. Report at least the most obvious outliers
6. Confidence score should reflect how anomalous the value is
7. Must handle varying dataset sizes efficiently

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on dataset scale.

The program should be robust enough to handle edge cases like missing data, duplicate entries, and varying data distributions.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a set of mining drillholes — each a 3D path with measurements taken at intervals along its length — identify the data entries that are most likely to be errors (typos, instrument glitches, or transcription mistakes).

The AI must compare each measurement against its spatial neighbours: a reading that disagrees with nearby drillholes is suspect. Subpasses increase the number of drillholes and properties, requiring efficient spatial reasoning at scale.

 0.8222
[5 holes, 3 properties, 50 samples] Found: 4, Actual errors: 8, TP: 4, FP: 0, FN: 4, P: 1.00, R: 0.50, F1: 0.67, Time: 0.05s

Subpass 0: 5 holes, 3 properties, 50 samples

Score: 0.82

Details: [5 holes, 3 properties, 50 samples] Found: 4, Actual errors: 8, TP: 4, FP: 0, FN: 4, P: 1.00, R: 0.50, F1: 0.67, Time: 0.05s

Reasoning 
No reasoning provided
Rust Code 
use std::io::{self, BufRead, Write, BufWriter};

struct Sample {
    hole_idx: usize,
    sample_idx: usize,
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
    z: f64,
    values: Vec<f64>,
}

struct Suspect {
    hole_id: String,
    sample_idx: usize,
    property_name: String,
    confidence: f64,
}

struct KdNode {
    sample_idx: usize,
    split_dim: usize,
    left: Option<Box<KdNode>>,
    right: Option<Box<KdNode>>,
}

fn build_kdtree(indices: &mut [usize], samples: &[Sample], depth: usize) -> Option<Box<KdNode>> {
    if indices.is_empty() {
        return None;
    }
    let dim = depth % 3;
    indices.sort_unstable_by(|&a, &b| {
        let va = match dim { 0 => samples[a].x, 1 => samples[a].y, _ => samples[a].z };
        let vb = match dim { 0 => samples[b].x, 1 => samples[b].y, _ => samples[b].z };
        va.partial_cmp(&vb).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal)
    });
    let mid = indices.len() / 2;
    let node_idx = indices[mid];
    let (left_slice, right_slice) = {
        let (left, rest) = indices.split_at_mut(mid);
        let right = if rest.len() > 1 { &mut rest[1..] } else { &mut [] as &mut [usize] };
        (left, right)
    };
    Some(Box::new(KdNode {
        sample_idx: node_idx,
        split_dim: dim,
        left: build_kdtree(left_slice, samples, depth + 1),
        right: build_kdtree(right_slice, samples, depth + 1),
    }))
}

fn knn_search(
    node: &Option<Box<KdNode>>,
    samples: &[Sample],
    target: &Sample,
    k: usize,
    neighbors: &mut Vec<(usize, f64)>,
    skip_idx: usize,
) {
    let node = match node {
        Some(n) => n,
        None => return,
    };
    let s = &samples[node.sample_idx];
    let dx = s.x - target.x;
    let dy = s.y - target.y;
    let dz = s.z - target.z;
    let dist_sq = dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz;

    if node.sample_idx != skip_idx {
        if neighbors.len() < k {
            neighbors.push((node.sample_idx, dist_sq));
            if neighbors.len() == k {
                neighbors.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| a.1.partial_cmp(&b.1).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));
            }
        } else if dist_sq < neighbors[k - 1].1 {
            neighbors[k - 1] = (node.sample_idx, dist_sq);
            neighbors.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| a.1.partial_cmp(&b.1).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));
        }
    }

    let target_val = match node.split_dim { 0 => target.x, 1 => target.y, _ => target.z };
    let node_val = match node.split_dim { 0 => s.x, 1 => s.y, _ => s.z };
    let diff = target_val - node_val;
    let diff_sq = diff * diff;

    let (first, second) = if diff < 0.0 {
        (&node.left, &node.right)
    } else {
        (&node.right, &node.left)
    };

    knn_search(first, samples, target, k, neighbors, skip_idx);
    if neighbors.len() < k || diff_sq < neighbors[k - 1].1 {
        knn_search(second, samples, target, k, neighbors, skip_idx);
    }
}

fn main() {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let stdout = io::stdout();
    let mut out = BufWriter::new(stdout.lock());

    let mut lines = stdin.lock().lines();

    let first_line = match lines.next() {
        Some(Ok(l)) => l,
        _ => { writeln!(out, "0").ok(); return; }
    };
    let parts: Vec<&str> = first_line.split_whitespace().collect();
    if parts.len() < 2 {
        writeln!(out, "0").ok();
        return;
    }
    let num_holes: usize = parts[0].parse().unwrap_or(0);
    let num_properties: usize = parts[1].parse().unwrap_or(0);

    if num_holes == 0 || num_properties == 0 {
        writeln!(out, "0").ok();
        return;
    }

    let prop_line = match lines.next() {
        Some(Ok(l)) => l,
        _ => { writeln!(out, "0").ok(); return; }
    };
    let property_names: Vec<String> = prop_line.split_whitespace().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect();

    let mut samples: Vec<Sample> = Vec::new();
    let mut hole_ids: Vec<String> = Vec::new();

    for _ in 0..num_holes {
        let hole_line = match lines.next() {
            Some(Ok(l)) => l,
            _ => break,
        };
        let hp: Vec<&str> = hole_line.split_whitespace().collect();
        if hp.len() < 9 { continue; }

        let hole_id = hp[0].to_string();
        let sx: f64 = hp[1].parse().unwrap_or(0.0);
        let sy: f64 = hp[2].parse().unwrap_or(0.0);
        let sz: f64 = hp[3].parse().unwrap_or(0.0);
        let dx: f64 = hp[4].parse().unwrap_or(0.0);
        let dy: f64 = hp[5].parse().unwrap_or(0.0);
        let dz: f64 = hp[6].parse().unwrap_or(0.0);
        let _length: f64 = hp[7].parse().unwrap_or(0.0);
        let n_samples: usize = hp[8].parse().unwrap_or(0);

        let hole_idx = hole_ids.len();
        hole_ids.push(hole_id);

        let dir_len = (dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz).sqrt();
        let (ndx, ndy, ndz) = if dir_len > 1e-12 {
            (dx / dir_len, dy / dir_len, dz / dir_len)
        } else {
            (0.0, 0.0, -1.0)
        };

        for si in 0..n_samples {
            let sample_line = match lines.next() {
                Some(Ok(l)) => l,
                _ => break,
            };
            let sp: Vec<&str> = sample_line.split_whitespace().collect();
            if sp.is_empty() { continue; }

            let depth: f64 = sp[0].parse().unwrap_or(0.0);
            let mut values = Vec::with_capacity(num_properties);
            for pi in 0..num_properties {
                let v = if pi + 1 < sp.len() {
                    sp[pi + 1].parse().unwrap_or(f64::NAN)
                } else {
                    f64::NAN
                };
                values.push(v);
            }

            samples.push(Sample {
                hole_idx,
                sample_idx: si,
                x: sx + ndx * depth,
                y: sy + ndy * depth,
                z: sz + ndz * depth,
                values,
            });
        }
    }

    let total_samples = samples.len();
    if total_samples < 2 {
        writeln!(out, "0").ok();
        return;
    }

    let mut prop_mean = vec![0.0f64; num_properties];
    let mut prop_var = vec![0.0f64; num_properties];
    let mut prop_count = vec![0usize; num_properties];

    for s in &samples {
        for pi in 0..num_properties {
            let v = s.values[pi];
            if v.is_finite() {
                prop_mean[pi] += v;
                prop_count[pi] += 1;
            }
        }
    }
    for pi in 0..num_properties {
        if prop_count[pi] > 0 {
            prop_mean[pi] /= prop_count[pi] as f64;
        }
    }
    for s in &samples {
        for pi in 0..num_properties {
            let v = s.values[pi];
            if v.is_finite() {
                let d = v - prop_mean[pi];
                prop_var[pi] += d * d;
            }
        }
    }
    for pi in 0..num_properties {
        if prop_count[pi] > 1 {
            prop_var[pi] /= prop_count[pi] as f64;
        }
    }
    let prop_std: Vec<f64> = prop_var.iter().map(|v| v.sqrt()).collect();

    let k = if total_samples < 50 {
        (total_samples - 1).min(5).max(2)
    } else if total_samples < 1000 {
        8
    } else if total_samples < 50000 {
        12
    } else {
        16
    };

    let mut indices: Vec<usize> = (0..total_samples).collect();
    let tree = build_kdtree(&mut indices, &samples, 0);

    let mut suspects: Vec<Suspect> = Vec::new();

    let z_threshold = if total_samples < 100 { 2.5 } else { 3.0 };

    for i in 0..total_samples {
        let mut neighbors = Vec::with_capacity(k + 1);
        knn_search(&tree, &samples, &samples[i], k, &mut neighbors, i);

        if neighbors.is_empty() { continue; }

        for pi in 0..num_properties {
            let v = samples[i].values[pi];
            if !v.is_finite() { continue; }
            if prop_std[pi] < 1e-12 { continue; }

            let mut n_mean = 0.0f64;
            let mut n_count = 0usize;
            let mut n_vals: Vec<f64> = Vec::new();

            for &(ni, _dist_sq) in &neighbors {
                let nv = samples[ni].values[pi];
                if nv.is_finite() {
                    n_vals.push(nv);
                    n_mean += nv;
                    n_count += 1;
                }
            }

            if n_count < 2 { continue; }

            n_mean /= n_count as f64;
            let mut n_var = 0.0f64;
            for &nv in &n_vals {
                let d = nv - n_mean;
                n_var += d * d;
            }
            n_var /= n_count as f64;
            let n_std = n_var.sqrt();

            let effective_std = n_std.max(prop_std[pi] * 0.1).max(1e-12);

            let local_z = (v - n_mean).abs() / effective_std;
            let global_z = (v - prop_mean[pi]).abs() / prop_std[pi].max(1e-12);

            let combined = local_z * 0.7 + global_z * 0.3;

            if combined > z_threshold {
                let confidence = ((combined - z_threshold) * 25.0).min(100.0).max(0.1);
                let prop_name = if pi < property_names.len() {
                    property_names[pi].clone()
                } else {
                    format!("prop_{}", pi)
                };
                suspects.push(Suspect {
                    hole_id: hole_ids[samples[i].hole_idx].clone(),
                    sample_idx: samples[i].sample_idx,
                    property_name: prop_name,
                    confidence,
                });
            }
        }
    }

    suspects.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| b.confidence.partial_cmp(&a.confidence).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));

    let max_output = if total_samples > 100000 { 10000 } else if total_samples > 10000 { 5000 } else { suspects.len() };
    let output_count = suspects.len().min(max_output);

    writeln!(out, "{}", output_count).ok();
    for s in suspects.iter().take(output_count) {
        writeln!(out, "{} {} {} {:.2}", s.hole_id, s.sample_idx, s.property_name, s.confidence).ok();
    }
}

Test 21: N-Dimensional Snake Game (C++) Score: 3.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to play an N-Dimensional Snake Game.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY dimensional complexity from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 2D games (eg 8x8), few obstacles, basic pathfinding
- **Medium**: 3D-4D games (eg 6x6x6 to 8x8x8x8), moderate obstacles, intermediate planning
- **Large**: 6D-8D games (eg 4x4x4x4x4x4 to 6x6x6x6x6x6x6x6), many obstacles, complex pathfinding
- **Extreme**: 10D-20D games (eg 3x3x3... to 4x4x4...)

**The Challenge:**
Your C++ snake player will be tested with games ranging from simple 2D boards to massive 20D hypercubes.
The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL dimensionalities.

**Problem:**
Like classic Nokia snake, but generalized to arbitrary dimensions. The snake occupies a series of
connected integer grid points in N-D space, moves one step per turn along one axis (+1 or -1),
grows when eating food, and dies if it hits an obstacle or itself.

**Input format (stdin, whitespace / newline seperated):**
```
D max_turns (D = number of dimensions, max_turns = turn limit)
bounds (D integers: size of space in each dimension)
snake_length start_pos (length, then D integers for head position)
F (number of food items)
F lines: food positions (D integers each)
O (number of obstacles)
O lines: obstacle positions (D integers each)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
Each turn, output: axis direction
- axis: 0 to D-1 (which dimension to move along)
- direction: -1 or +1 (which way to move)

**Game ends when:**
- Snake eats all food (win)
- Snake hits obstacle or itself (lose)
- Max turns reached

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Play the classic Snake game, but in more than two dimensions. The snake moves through an N-dimensional grid, eating food to grow while avoiding its own body and the walls. In higher dimensions the grid is enormous but self-collision becomes far trickier to reason about.

Subpasses increase the number of dimensions, making the state space explode. The AI must write a strategy that plans a safe path to food without boxing itself in.

 1.0000
[2D 20x20, 5 food, 10 obstacles] Food: 5/5, Turns: 34, End: won, Time: 0.12s

N-Dimensional Snake - 2D 20x20, 5 food, 10 obstacles

View C++ Code (5762 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <deque>
#include <queue>
#include <unordered_set>
#include <algorithm>
#include <climits>

using namespace std;
using Pos = vector<int>;

struct PosHash {
    size_t operator()(const Pos& p) const {
        size_t h = 0;
        for (int x : p)
            h ^= hash<int>()(x) + 0x9e3779b9 + (h << 6) + (h >> 2);
        return h;
    }
};

static int D;
static vector<int> bounds;
static deque<Pos> snake;
static unordered_set<Pos, PosHash> snake_set, obstacles, food_set;
static int target_len;

bool in_bounds(const Pos& p) {
    for (int i = 0; i < D; i++)
        if (p[i] < 0 || p[i] >= bounds[i]) return false;
    return true;
}

bool is_safe(const Pos& p) {
    return in_bounds(p) && !obstacles.count(p) && !snake_set.count(p);
}

pair<int,int> find_move() {
    const Pos& h = snake.front();

    int search_limit;
    if (D <= 3) search_limit = 500000;
    else if (D <= 6) search_limit = 100000;
    else if (D <= 10) search_limit = 20000;
    else search_limit = 0;

    if (search_limit > 0) {
        vector<const Pos*> targets;
        {
            vector<pair<int, const Pos*>> fd;
            for (const auto& f : food_set) {
                int d = 0;
                for (int i = 0; i < D; i++) d += abs(h[i] - f[i]);
                fd.push_back({d, &f});
            }
            sort(fd.begin(), fd.end());
            for (int i = 0; i < min((int)fd.size(), 5); i++)
                targets.push_back(fd[i].second);
        }

        auto heuristic = [&](const Pos& p) -> int {
            int best = INT_MAX;
            for (auto* t : targets) {
                int d = 0;
                for (int i = 0; i < D; i++) d += abs(p[i] - (*t)[i]);
                if (d < best) best = d;
            }
            return best;
        };

        struct Node {
            int f, g, first_axis, first_dir;
            Pos pos;
            bool operator>(const Node& o) const { return f > o.f; }
        };

        priority_queue<Node, vector<Node>, greater<Node>> pq;
        unordered_set<Pos, PosHash> visited;
        visited.insert(h);

        for (int axis = 0; axis < D; axis++) {
            for (int dir : {-1, 1}) {
                Pos next = h;
                next[axis] += dir;
                if (is_safe(next) && !visited.count(next)) {
                    visited.insert(next);
                    if (food_set.count(next)) return {axis, dir};
                    pq.push({1 + heuristic(next), 1, axis, dir, next});
                }
            }
        }

        int explored = 0;
        while (!pq.empty() && explored < search_limit) {
            Node node = pq.top();
            pq.pop();
            explored++;

            for (int axis = 0; axis < D; axis++) {
                for (int dir : {-1, 1}) {
                    Pos next = node.pos;
                    next[axis] += dir;
                    if (is_safe(next) && !visited.count(next)) {
                        visited.insert(next);
                        if (food_set.count(next))
                            return {node.first_axis, node.first_dir};
                        int ng = node.g + 1;
                        pq.push({ng + heuristic(next), ng, node.first_axis, node.first_dir, next});
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }

    int best_score = INT_MIN;
    int result_axis = -1, result_dir = 0;

    for (int axis = 0; axis < D; axis++) {
        for (int dir : {-1, 1}) {
            Pos next = h;
            next[axis] += dir;
            if (!is_safe(next)) continue;

            if (food_set.count(next)) return {axis, dir};

            int dist = INT_MAX;
            for (const auto& f : food_set) {
                int d = 0;
                for (int i = 0; i < D; i++) d += abs(next[i] - f[i]);
                dist = min(dist, d);
            }

            int neighbors = 0;
            Pos nn = next;
            for (int a = 0; a < D; a++) {
                nn[a]++;
                if (is_safe(nn)) neighbors++;
                nn[a] -= 2;
                if (is_safe(nn)) neighbors++;
                nn[a]++;
            }

            int score = -dist * 100 + neighbors;
            if (score > best_score) {
                best_score = score;
                result_axis = axis;
                result_dir = dir;
            }
        }
    }

    if (result_axis == -1) return {0, 1};
    return {result_axis, result_dir};
}

int main() {
    ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int max_turns;
    cin >> D >> max_turns;

    bounds.resize(D);
    for (int i = 0; i < D; i++) cin >> bounds[i];

    int snake_len;
    cin >> snake_len;
    Pos head(D);
    for (int i = 0; i < D; i++) cin >> head[i];

    snake.push_back(head);
    snake_set.insert(head);
    target_len = snake_len;

    int F;
    cin >> F;
    for (int i = 0; i < F; i++) {
        Pos f(D);
        for (int j = 0; j < D; j++) cin >> f[j];
        food_set.insert(f);
    }

    int O;
    cin >> O;
    for (int i = 0; i < O; i++) {
        Pos ob(D);
        for (int j = 0; j < D; j++) cin >> ob[j];
        obstacles.insert(ob);
    }

    for (int turn = 0; turn < max_turns && !food_set.empty(); turn++) {
        auto [axis, dir] = find_move();

        cout << axis << " " << dir << "\n";
        cout.flush();

        Pos new_head = snake.front();
        new_head[axis] += dir;

        snake.push_front(new_head);
        snake_set.insert(new_head);

        if (food_set.count(new_head)) {
            food_set.erase(new_head);
            target_len++;
        }

        while ((int)snake.size() > target_len) {
            snake_set.erase(snake.back());
            snake.pop_back();
        }
    }

    return 0;
}
Subpass 1
Subpass configurationView exact prompt
View raw AI output

#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <deque>
#include <queue>
#include <unordered_set>
#include <algorithm>
#include <climits>

using namespace std;
using Pos = vector<int>;

struct PosHash {
    size_t operator()(const Pos& p) const {
        size_t h = 0;
        for (int x : p)
            h ^= hash<int>()(x) + 0x9e3779b9 + (h << 6) + (h >> 2);
        return h;
    }
};

static int D;
static vector<int> bounds;
static deque<Pos> snake;
static unordered_set<Pos, PosHash> snake_set, obstacles, food_set;
static int target_len;

bool in_bounds(const Pos& p) {
    for (int i = 0; i < D; i++)
        if (p[i] < 0 || p[i] >= bounds[i]) return false;
    return true;
}

bool is_safe(const Pos& p) {
    return in_bounds(p) && !obstacles.count(p) && !snake_set.count(p);
}

pair<int,int> find_move() {
    const Pos& h = snake.front();

    int search_limit;
    if (D <= 3) search_limit = 500000;
    else if (D <= 6) search_limit = 100000;
    else if (D <= 10) search_limit = 20000;
    else search_limit = 0;

    if (search_limit > 0) {
        vector<const Pos*> targets;
        {
            vector<pair<int, const Pos*>> fd;
            for (const auto& f : food_set) {
                int d = 0;
                for (int i = 0; i < D; i++) d += abs(h[i] - f[i]);
                fd.push_back({d, &f});
            }
            sort(fd.begin(), fd.end());
            for (int i = 0; i < min((int)fd.size(), 5); i++)
                targets.push_back(fd[i].second);
        }

        auto heuristic = [&](const Pos& p) -> int {
            int best = INT_MAX;
            for (auto* t : targets) {
                int d = 0;
                for (int i = 0; i < D; i++) d += abs(p[i] - (*t)[i]);
                if (d < best) best = d;
            }
            return best;
        };

        struct Node {
            int f, g, first_axis, first_dir;
            Pos pos;
            bool operator>(const Node& o) const { return f > o.f; }
        };

        priority_queue<Node, vector<Node>, greater<Node>> pq;
        unordered_set<Pos, PosHash> visited;
        visited.insert(h);

        for (int axis = 0; axis < D; axis++) {
            for (int dir : {-1, 1}) {
                Pos next = h;
                next[axis] += dir;
                if (is_safe(next) && !visited.count(next)) {
                    visited.insert(next);
                    if (food_set.count(next)) return {axis, dir};
                    pq.push({1 + heuristic(next), 1, axis, dir, next});
                }
            }
        }

        int explored = 0;
        while (!pq.empty() && explored < search_limit) {
            Node node = pq.top();
            pq.pop();
            explored++;

            for (int axis = 0; axis < D; axis++) {
                for (int dir : {-1, 1}) {
                    Pos next = node.pos;
                    next[axis] += dir;
                    if (is_safe(next) && !visited.count(next)) {
                        visited.insert(next);
                        if (food_set.count(next))
                            return {node.first_axis, node.first_dir};
                        int ng = node.g + 1;
                        pq.push({ng + heuristic(next), ng, node.first_axis, node.first_dir, next});
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }

    int best_score = INT_MIN;
    int result_axis = -1, result_dir = 0;

    for (int axis = 0; axis < D; axis++) {
        for (int dir : {-1, 1}) {
            Pos next = h;
            next[axis] += dir;
            if (!is_safe(next)) continue;

            if (food_set.count(next)) return {axis, dir};

            int dist = INT_MAX;
            for (const auto& f : food_set) {
                int d = 0;
                for (int i = 0; i < D; i++) d += abs(next[i] - f[i]);
                dist = min(dist, d);
            }

            int neighbors = 0;
            Pos nn = next;
            for (int a = 0; a < D; a++) {
                nn[a]++;
                if (is_safe(nn)) neighbors++;
                nn[a] -= 2;
                if (is_safe(nn)) neighbors++;
                nn[a]++;
            }

            int score = -dist * 100 + neighbors;
            if (score > best_score) {
                best_score = score;
                result_axis = axis;
                result_dir = dir;
            }
        }
    }

    if (result_axis == -1) return {0, 1};
    return {result_axis, result_dir};
}

int main() {
    ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int max_turns;
    cin >> D >> max_turns;

    bounds.resize(D);
    for (int i = 0; i < D; i++) cin >> bounds[i];

    int snake_len;
    cin >> snake_len;
    Pos head(D);
    for (int i = 0; i < D; i++) cin >> head[i];

    target_len = snake_len;

        for (int j = 0; j < D; j++) cin >> f[j];

        snake_set.insert(new_head);

        }

        while ((int)snake.size() > target_len) {

            snake.pop_back();
        }
    }

    return 0;

#include <vector>
#include <deque>
#include <queue>
#include <unordered_set>
#include <algorithm>
#include <climits>

using namespace std;
using Pos = vector<int>;

struct PosHash {
    size_t operator()(const Pos& p) const {
        size_t h = 0;
        for (int x : p)
            h ^= hash<int>()(x) + 0x9e3779b9 + (h << 6) + (h >> 2);
        return h;
    }
};

static int D;
static vector<int> bounds;
static deque<Pos> snake;
static unordered_set<Pos, PosHash> snake_set, obstacles, food_set;
static int target_len;

bool in_bounds(const Pos& p) {
    for (int i = 0; i < D; i++)
        if (p[i] < 0 || p[i] >= bounds[i]) return false;
    return true;
}

bool is_safe(const Pos& p) {
    return in_bounds(p) && !obstacles.count(p) && !snake_set.count(p);
}

    const Pos& h = snake.front();

    int search_limit;

        unordered_set<Pos, PosHash> visited;
        visited.insert(h);

                }
            }
        }

            Node node = pq.top();
            pq.pop();
            explored++;

            for (int axis = 0; axis < D; axis++) {
                for (int dir : {-1, 1}) {
                    Pos next = node.pos;
                    next[axis] += dir;
                    if (is_safe(next) && !visited.count(next)) {
                        visited.insert(next);
                        if (food_set.count(next))
                            return {node.first_axis, node.first_dir};
                        int ng = node.g + 1;
                        pq.push({ng + heuristic(next), ng, node.first_axis, node.first_dir, next});

        }
    }

    int result_axis = -1, result_dir = 0;

        for (int dir : {-1, 1}) {
            Pos next = h;
            next[axis] += dir;
            if (!is_safe(next)) continue;

            if (food_set.count(next)) return {axis, dir};

            int dist = INT_MAX;
            for (const auto& f : food_set) {
                int d = 0;
                for (int i = 0; i < D; i++) d += abs(next[i] - f[i]);
                dist = min(dist, d);
