            int neighbors = 0;
            Pos nn = next;
            for (int a = 0; a < D; a++) {
                nn[a]++;
                if (is_safe(nn)) neighbors++;
                nn[a] -= 2;
                if (is_safe(nn)) neighbors++;
                nn[a]++;
            }

            int score = -dist * 100 + neighbors;
            if (score > best_score) {
                best_score = score;
                result_axis = axis;
                result_dir = dir;
            }
        }
    }

    if (result_axis == -1) return {0, 1};
    return {result_axis, result_dir};
}

int main() {
    ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int max_turns;
    cin >> D >> max_turns;

    bounds.resize(D);
    for (int i = 0; i < D; i++) cin >> bounds[i];

    int snake_len;
    cin >> snake_len;
    Pos head(D);
    for (int i = 0; i < D; i++) cin >> head[i];

    snake.push_back(head);
    snake_set.insert(head);
    target_len = snake_len;

    int F;
    cin >> F;
    for (int i = 0; i < F; i++) {
        Pos f(D);
        for (int j = 0; j < D; j++) cin >> f[j];
        food_set.insert(f);
    }

    int O;
    cin >> O;
    for (int i = 0; i < O; i++) {
        Pos ob(D);
        for (int j = 0; j < D; j++) cin >> ob[j];
        obstacles.insert(ob);
    }

    for (int turn = 0; turn < max_turns && !food_set.empty(); turn++) {
        auto [axis, dir] = find_move();

        cout << axis << " " << dir << "\n";
        cout.flush();

        Pos new_head = snake.front();
        new_head[axis] += dir;

        snake.push_front(new_head);
        snake_set.insert(new_head);

        if (food_set.count(new_head)) {
            food_set.erase(new_head);
            target_len++;
        }

        while ((int)snake.size() > target_len) {
            snake_set.erase(snake.back());
            snake.pop_back();
        }
    }

    return 0;
}

#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <deque>
#include <queue>
#include <unordered_set>
#include <algorithm>
#include <climits>

using namespace std;
using Pos = vector<int>;

struct PosHash {
    size_t operator()(const Pos& p) const {
        size_t h = 0;
        for (int x : p)
            h ^= hash<int>()(x) + 0x9e3779b9 + (h << 6) + (h >> 2);
        return h;
    }
};

static int D;
static vector<int> bounds;
static deque<Pos> snake;
static unordered_set<Pos, PosHash> snake_set, obstacles, food_set;
static int target_len;

bool in_bounds(const Pos& p) {
    for (int i = 0; i < D; i++)
        if (p[i] < 0 || p[i] >= bounds[i]) return false;
    return true;
}

bool is_safe(const Pos& p) {
    return in_bounds(p) && !obstacles.count(p) && !snake_set.count(p);
}

pair<int,int> find_move() {
    const Pos& h = snake.front();

    int search_limit;
    if (D <= 3) search_limit = 500000;
    else if (D <= 6) search_limit = 100000;
    else if (D <= 10) search_limit = 20000;
    else search_limit = 0;

    if (search_limit > 0) {
        vector<const Pos*> targets;
        {
            vector<pair<int, const Pos*>> fd;
            for (const auto& f : food_set) {
                int d = 0;
                for (int i = 0; i < D; i++) d += abs(h[i] - f[i]);
                fd.push_back({d, &f});
            }
            sort(fd.begin(), fd.end());
            for (int i = 0; i < min((int)fd.size(), 5); i++)
                targets.push_back(fd[i].second);
        }

        auto heuristic = [&](const Pos& p) -> int {
            int best = INT_MAX;
            for (auto* t : targets) {
                int d = 0;
                for (int i = 0; i < D; i++) d += abs(p[i] - (*t)[i]);
                if (d < best) best = d;
            }
            return best;
        };

        struct Node {
            int f, g, first_axis, first_dir;
            Pos pos;
            bool operator>(const Node& o) const { return f > o.f; }
        };

        priority_queue<Node, vector<Node>, greater<Node>> pq;
        unordered_set<Pos, PosHash> visited;
        visited.insert(h);

        for (int axis = 0; axis < D; axis++) {
            for (int dir : {-1, 1}) {
                Pos next = h;
                next[axis] += dir;
                if (is_safe(next) && !visited.count(next)) {
                    visited.insert(next);
                    if (food_set.count(next)) return {axis, dir};
                    pq.push({1 + heuristic(next), 1, axis, dir, next});
                }
            }
        }

        int explored = 0;
        while (!pq.empty() && explored < search_limit) {
            Node node = pq.top();
            pq.pop();
            explored++;

            for (int axis = 0; axis < D; axis++) {
                for (int dir : {-1, 1}) {
                    Pos next = node.pos;
                    next[axis] += dir;
                    if (is_safe(next) && !visited.count(next)) {
                        visited.insert(next);
                        if (food_set.count(next))
                            return {node.first_axis, node.first_dir};
                        int ng = node.g + 1;
                        pq.push({ng + heuristic(next), ng, node.first_axis, node.first_dir, next});
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }

    int best_score = INT_MIN;
    int result_axis = -1, result_dir = 0;

    for (int axis = 0; axis < D; axis++) {
        for (int dir : {-1, 1}) {
            Pos next = h;
            next[axis] += dir;
            if (!is_safe(next)) continue;

            if (food_set.count(next)) return {axis, dir};

            int dist = INT_MAX;
            for (const auto& f : food_set) {
                int d = 0;
                for (int i = 0; i < D; i++) d += abs(next[i] - f[i]);
                dist = min(dist, d);
            }

            int neighbors = 0;
            Pos nn = next;
            for (int a = 0; a < D; a++) {
                nn[a]++;
                if (is_safe(nn)) neighbors++;
                nn[a] -= 2;
                if (is_safe(nn)) neighbors++;
                nn[a]++;
            }

            int score = -dist * 100 + neighbors;
            if (score > best_score) {
                best_score = score;
                result_axis = axis;
                result_dir = dir;
            }
        }
    }

    if (result_axis == -1) return {0, 1};
    return {result_axis, result_dir};
}

int main() {
    ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int max_turns;
    cin >> D >> max_turns;

    bounds.resize(D);
    for (int i = 0; i < D; i++) cin >> bounds[i];

    int snake_len;
    cin >> snake_len;
    Pos head(D);
    for (int i = 0; i < D; i++) cin >> head[i];

    snake.push_back(head);
    snake_set.insert(head);
    target_len = snake_len;

    int F;
    cin >> F;
    for (int i = 0; i < F; i++) {
        Pos f(D);
        for (int j = 0; j < D; j++) cin >> f[j];
        food_set.insert(f);
    }

    int O;
    cin >> O;
    for (int i = 0; i < O; i++) {
        Pos ob(D);
        for (int j = 0; j < D; j++) cin >> ob[j];
        obstacles.insert(ob);
    }

    for (int turn = 0; turn < max_turns && !food_set.empty(); turn++) {
        auto [axis, dir] = find_move();

        cout << axis << " " << dir << "\n";
        cout.flush();

        Pos new_head = snake.front();
        new_head[axis] += dir;

        snake.push_front(new_head);
        snake_set.insert(new_head);

        if (food_set.count(new_head)) {
            food_set.erase(new_head);
            target_len++;
        }

        while ((int)snake.size() > target_len) {
            snake_set.erase(snake.back());
            snake.pop_back();
        }
    }

    return 0;
}

Test 22: Tetris Game (C#) Score: 1.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C# code to play Tetris optimally.

Your C# Tetris player will be tested with games ranging from short practice sessions to marathon games.

**Problem:**
Standard Tetris rules apply - variable width board (typically 10-20 columns),
pieces fall from top, clear lines by filling them completely, game over when pieces stack to the top.
The program receives pieces one at a time and must decide optimal placement.

**Input format (stdin, whitespace seperated decimal integers):**
```
width height num_pieces
For each piece:
Line: piece_type (I, O, T, S, Z, J, L)
Program outputs: rotation column
Then receives: result (ok, cleared_N, gameover)
```

**Output format (stdout, 1 line per piece, whitespace seperated decimal integers):**
rotation column
- rotation: 0-3 (number of 90° clockwise rotations)
- column: 0 to width-1 (leftmost column of piece)

**Piece Types:**
- I: 4x1 line piece
- O: 2x2 square piece
- T: T-shaped piece
- S: S-shaped piece (right-leaning)
- Z: Z-shaped piece (left-leaning)
- J: L-shaped piece (left-leaning)
- L: L-shaped piece (right-leaning)

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with .NET/csc
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle variable board widths and long game sequences
4. Must place a peice within 5 seconds of it being written to STDIN.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14


Write complete, compilable C# code with a Main method.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on game complexity.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Write an AI that plays Tetris. Given the current board state and the next piece, choose where to place it to survive as long as possible and clear as many lines as you can. Good play means keeping the board flat, avoiding buried holes, and setting up multi-line clears.

Subpasses increase the board size and speed, demanding smarter lookahead and evaluation. The AI must balance short-term survival with long-term board health.

 0.0000
[20 pieces] Placed: 20/20, Lines: 0, End: completed, Time: 0.15s

Tetris AI - 20 pieces

View C# Code (10204 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;

class TetrisPlayer
{
    static int Width, Height;
    static int[] board;

    static readonly int[][][][] Pieces = new int[][][][]
    {
        // I piece (index 0)
        new int[][][]{
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{2,0},new[]{3,0}},       // rot 0: horizontal
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{0,2},new[]{0,3}},       // rot 1: vertical
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{2,0},new[]{3,0}},       // rot 2: horizontal
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{0,2},new[]{0,3}}        // rot 3: vertical
        },
        // O piece (index 1)
        new int[][][]{
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1}},
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1}},
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1}},
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1}}
        },
        // T piece (index 2)
        new int[][][]{
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{2,0},new[]{1,1}},       // rot 0: T pointing down
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{0,2},new[]{1,1}},       // rot 1: T pointing right
            new int[][]{new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1},new[]{2,1}},       // rot 2: T pointing up
            new int[][]{new[]{1,0},new[]{1,1},new[]{1,2},new[]{0,1}}        // rot 3: T pointing left
        },
        // S piece (index 3)
        new int[][][]{
            new int[][]{new[]{1,0},new[]{2,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1}},       // rot 0: horizontal
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1},new[]{1,2}},       // rot 1: vertical
            new int[][]{new[]{1,0},new[]{2,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1}},       // rot 2
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1},new[]{1,2}}        // rot 3
        },
        // Z piece (index 4)
        new int[][][]{
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{1,1},new[]{2,1}},       // rot 0: horizontal
            new int[][]{new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1},new[]{0,2}},       // rot 1: vertical
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{1,1},new[]{2,1}},       // rot 2
            new int[][]{new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1},new[]{0,2}}        // rot 3
        },
        // J piece (index 5)
        new int[][][]{
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1},new[]{2,1}},       // rot 0
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{0,2}},       // rot 1
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{2,0},new[]{2,1}},       // rot 2
            new int[][]{new[]{1,0},new[]{1,1},new[]{0,2},new[]{1,2}}        // rot 3
        },
        // L piece (index 6)
        new int[][][]{
            new int[][]{new[]{2,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1},new[]{2,1}},       // rot 0
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{0,2},new[]{1,2}},       // rot 1
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{2,0},new[]{0,1}},       // rot 2
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{1,1},new[]{1,2}}        // rot 3
        }
    };

    static readonly Dictionary<string, int> PieceIndex = new Dictionary<string, int>
    {
        {"I",0},{"O",1},{"T",2},{"S",3},{"Z",4},{"J",5},{"L",6}
    };

    static int DropPiece(int[] b, int[][] cells, int col)
    {
        int maxDrop = Height;
        foreach (var c in cells)
        {
            int cx = col + c[0];
            if (cx < 0 || cx >= Width) return -1;
            int drop = -1;
            for (int r = c[1]; r < Height; r++)
            {
                if (b[r * Width + cx] != 0) break;
                drop = r - c[1];
            }
            if (drop < 0) return -1;
            maxDrop = Math.Min(maxDrop, drop);
        }
        foreach (var c in cells)
        {
            int cy = maxDrop + c[1];
            if (cy < 0 || cy >= Height) return -1;
        }
        return maxDrop;
    }

    static bool PlacePiece(int[] b, int[][] cells, int col, int dropRow)
    {
        foreach (var c in cells)
        {
            int cx = col + c[0];
            int cy = dropRow + c[1];
            if (cy < 0 || cy >= Height || cx < 0 || cx >= Width) return false;
            if (b[cy * Width + cx] != 0) return false;
        }
        foreach (var c in cells)
        {
            b[(dropRow + c[1]) * Width + (col + c[0])] = 1;
        }
        return true;
    }

    static int ClearLines(int[] b)
    {
        int cleared = 0;
        int writeRow = Height - 1;
        for (int r = Height - 1; r >= 0; r--)
        {
            bool full = true;
            for (int c = 0; c < Width; c++)
            {
                if (b[r * Width + c] == 0) { full = false; break; }
            }
            if (!full)
            {
                if (writeRow != r)
                    Array.Copy(b, r * Width, b, writeRow * Width, Width);
                writeRow--;
            }
            else cleared++;
        }
        for (int r = 0; r <= writeRow; r++)
            for (int c = 0; c < Width; c++)
                b[r * Width + c] = 0;
        return cleared;
    }

    static double Evaluate(int[] b, int linesCleared)
    {
        double score = linesCleared * 760.0;

        int aggregateHeight = 0;
        int holes = 0;
        int bumpiness = 0;
        int[] colHeights = new int[Width];

        for (int c = 0; c < Width; c++)
        {
            for (int r = 0; r < Height; r++)
            {
                if (b[r * Width + c] != 0)
                {
                    colHeights[c] = Height - r;
                    break;
                }
            }
            aggregateHeight += colHeights[c];
        }

        for (int c = 0; c < Width; c++)
        {
            bool blockFound = false;
            for (int r = 0; r < Height; r++)
            {
                if (b[r * Width + c] != 0) blockFound = true;
                else if (blockFound) holes++;
            }
        }

        for (int c = 0; c < Width - 1; c++)
            bumpiness += Math.Abs(colHeights[c] - colHeights[c + 1]);

        int maxHeight = 0;
        for (int c = 0; c < Width; c++)
            if (colHeights[c] > maxHeight) maxHeight = colHeights[c];

        int coveredDepth = 0;
        for (int c = 0; c < Width; c++)
        {
            int above = 0;
            for (int r = 0; r < Height; r++)
            {
                if (b[r * Width + c] != 0) above++;
                else if (above > 0) coveredDepth += above;
            }
        }

        int rowTrans = 0;
        for (int r = Height - maxHeight; r < Height; r++)
        {
            for (int c = 0; c < Width - 1; c++)
            {
                if (b[r * Width + c] != b[r * Width + c + 1])
                    rowTrans++;
            }
        }

        int colTrans = 0;
        for (int c = 0; c < Width; c++)
        {
            for (int r = Height - maxHeight; r < Height - 1; r++)
            {
                if (b[r * Width + c] != b[(r + 1) * Width + c])
                    colTrans++;
            }
        }

        score -= aggregateHeight * 4.5;
        score -= holes * 35.0;
        score -= coveredDepth * 8.0;
        score -= bumpiness * 3.2;
        score -= maxHeight * 1.5;
        score -= rowTrans * 3.2;
        score -= colTrans * 3.2;

        if (maxHeight > Height - 4)
            score -= (maxHeight - (Height - 4)) * 80.0;

        return score;
    }

    static void Main()
    {
        string[] tokens = ReadTokens(3);
        Width = int.Parse(tokens[0]);
        Height = int.Parse(tokens[1]);
        int numPieces = int.Parse(tokens[2]);

        board = new int[Width * Height];

        for (int p = 0; p < numPieces; p++)
        {
            tokens = ReadTokens(1);
            string pieceType = tokens[0];
            int pi = PieceIndex[pieceType];

            int bestRot = 0, bestCol = 0;
            double bestScore = double.MinValue;

            for (int rot = 0; rot < 4; rot++)
            {
                int[][] cells = Pieces[pi][rot];
                int maxX = 0, maxY = 0;
                foreach (var c in cells)
                {
                    if (c[0] > maxX) maxX = c[0];
                    if (c[1] > maxY) maxY = c[1];
                }

                for (int col = 0; col <= Width - 1 - maxX; col++)
                {
                    int dropRow = DropPiece(board, cells, col);
                    if (dropRow < 0) continue;
                    if (dropRow + maxY >= Height) continue;

                    int[] temp = (int[])board.Clone();
                    if (!PlacePiece(temp, cells, col, dropRow)) continue;

                    int cleared = ClearLines(temp);
                    double sc = Evaluate(temp, cleared);

                    if (sc > bestScore)
                    {
                        bestScore = sc;
                        bestRot = rot;
                        bestCol = col;
                    }
                }
            }

            Console.WriteLine(bestRot + " " + bestCol);
            Console.Out.Flush();

            tokens = ReadTokens(1);
            if (tokens[0] == null || tokens[0].StartsWith("gameover")) return;

            int[][] bestCells = Pieces[pi][bestRot];
            int dr = DropPiece(board, bestCells, bestCol);
            if (dr >= 0)
            {
                PlacePiece(board, bestCells, bestCol, dr);
                ClearLines(board);
            }
        }
    }

    static List<string> tokenBuffer = new List<string>();
    static string[] ReadTokens(int count)
    {
        while (tokenBuffer.Count < count)
        {
            string line = Console.ReadLine();
            if (line == null) return new string[count];
            line = line.Trim();
            if (line.Length == 0) continue;
            tokenBuffer.AddRange(line.Split(new char[]{' ','\t','\r','\n'}, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries));
        }
        string[] result = new string[count];
        for (int i = 0; i < count; i++)
            result[i] = tokenBuffer[i];
        tokenBuffer.RemoveRange(0, Math.Min(count, tokenBuffer.Count));
        return result;
    }
}

Tetris AI - 50 pieces

View C# Code (10204 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;

class TetrisPlayer
{
    static int Width, Height;
    static int[] board;

    static readonly int[][][][] Pieces = new int[][][][]
    {
        // I piece (index 0)
        new int[][][]{
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{2,0},new[]{3,0}},       // rot 0: horizontal
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{0,2},new[]{0,3}},       // rot 1: vertical
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{2,0},new[]{3,0}},       // rot 2: horizontal
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{0,2},new[]{0,3}}        // rot 3: vertical
        },
        // O piece (index 1)
        new int[][][]{
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1}},
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1}},
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1}},
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1}}
        },
        // T piece (index 2)
        new int[][][]{
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{2,0},new[]{1,1}},       // rot 0: T pointing down
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{0,2},new[]{1,1}},       // rot 1: T pointing right
            new int[][]{new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1},new[]{2,1}},       // rot 2: T pointing up
            new int[][]{new[]{1,0},new[]{1,1},new[]{1,2},new[]{0,1}}        // rot 3: T pointing left
        },
        // S piece (index 3)
        new int[][][]{
            new int[][]{new[]{1,0},new[]{2,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1}},       // rot 0: horizontal
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1},new[]{1,2}},       // rot 1: vertical
            new int[][]{new[]{1,0},new[]{2,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1}},       // rot 2
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1},new[]{1,2}}        // rot 3
        },
        // Z piece (index 4)
        new int[][][]{
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{1,1},new[]{2,1}},       // rot 0: horizontal
            new int[][]{new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1},new[]{0,2}},       // rot 1: vertical
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{1,1},new[]{2,1}},       // rot 2
            new int[][]{new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1},new[]{0,2}}        // rot 3
        },
        // J piece (index 5)
        new int[][][]{
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1},new[]{2,1}},       // rot 0
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{0,2}},       // rot 1
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{2,0},new[]{2,1}},       // rot 2
            new int[][]{new[]{1,0},new[]{1,1},new[]{0,2},new[]{1,2}}        // rot 3
        },
        // L piece (index 6)
        new int[][][]{
            new int[][]{new[]{2,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1},new[]{2,1}},       // rot 0
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{0,2},new[]{1,2}},       // rot 1
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{2,0},new[]{0,1}},       // rot 2
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{1,1},new[]{1,2}}        // rot 3
        }
    };

    static readonly Dictionary<string, int> PieceIndex = new Dictionary<string, int>
    {
        {"I",0},{"O",1},{"T",2},{"S",3},{"Z",4},{"J",5},{"L",6}
    };

    static int DropPiece(int[] b, int[][] cells, int col)
    {
        int maxDrop = Height;
        foreach (var c in cells)
        {
            int cx = col + c[0];
            if (cx < 0 || cx >= Width) return -1;
            int drop = -1;
            for (int r = c[1]; r < Height; r++)
            {
                if (b[r * Width + cx] != 0) break;
                drop = r - c[1];
            }
            if (drop < 0) return -1;
            maxDrop = Math.Min(maxDrop, drop);
        }
        foreach (var c in cells)
        {
            int cy = maxDrop + c[1];
            if (cy < 0 || cy >= Height) return -1;
        }
        return maxDrop;
    }

    static bool PlacePiece(int[] b, int[][] cells, int col, int dropRow)
    {
        foreach (var c in cells)
        {
            int cx = col + c[0];
            int cy = dropRow + c[1];
            if (cy < 0 || cy >= Height || cx < 0 || cx >= Width) return false;
            if (b[cy * Width + cx] != 0) return false;
        }
        foreach (var c in cells)
        {
            b[(dropRow + c[1]) * Width + (col + c[0])] = 1;
        }
        return true;
    }

    static int ClearLines(int[] b)
    {
        int cleared = 0;
        int writeRow = Height - 1;
        for (int r = Height - 1; r >= 0; r--)
        {
            bool full = true;
            for (int c = 0; c < Width; c++)
            {
                if (b[r * Width + c] == 0) { full = false; break; }
            }
            if (!full)
            {
                if (writeRow != r)
                    Array.Copy(b, r * Width, b, writeRow * Width, Width);
                writeRow--;
            }
            else cleared++;
        }
        for (int r = 0; r <= writeRow; r++)
            for (int c = 0; c < Width; c++)
                b[r * Width + c] = 0;
        return cleared;
    }

    static double Evaluate(int[] b, int linesCleared)
    {
        double score = linesCleared * 760.0;

        int aggregateHeight = 0;
        int holes = 0;
        int bumpiness = 0;
        int[] colHeights = new int[Width];

        for (int c = 0; c < Width; c++)
        {
            for (int r = 0; r < Height; r++)
            {
                if (b[r * Width + c] != 0)
                {
                    colHeights[c] = Height - r;
                    break;
                }
            }
            aggregateHeight += colHeights[c];
        }

        for (int c = 0; c < Width; c++)
        {
            bool blockFound = false;
            for (int r = 0; r < Height; r++)
            {
                if (b[r * Width + c] != 0) blockFound = true;
                else if (blockFound) holes++;
            }
        }

        for (int c = 0; c < Width - 1; c++)
            bumpiness += Math.Abs(colHeights[c] - colHeights[c + 1]);

        int maxHeight = 0;
        for (int c = 0; c < Width; c++)
            if (colHeights[c] > maxHeight) maxHeight = colHeights[c];

        int coveredDepth = 0;
        for (int c = 0; c < Width; c++)
        {
            int above = 0;
            for (int r = 0; r < Height; r++)
            {
                if (b[r * Width + c] != 0) above++;
                else if (above > 0) coveredDepth += above;
            }
        }

        int rowTrans = 0;
        for (int r = Height - maxHeight; r < Height; r++)
        {
            for (int c = 0; c < Width - 1; c++)
            {
                if (b[r * Width + c] != b[r * Width + c + 1])
                    rowTrans++;
            }
        }

        int colTrans = 0;
        for (int c = 0; c < Width; c++)
        {
            for (int r = Height - maxHeight; r < Height - 1; r++)
            {
                if (b[r * Width + c] != b[(r + 1) * Width + c])
                    colTrans++;
            }
        }

        score -= aggregateHeight * 4.5;
        score -= holes * 35.0;
        score -= coveredDepth * 8.0;
        score -= bumpiness * 3.2;
        score -= maxHeight * 1.5;
        score -= rowTrans * 3.2;
        score -= colTrans * 3.2;

        if (maxHeight > Height - 4)
            score -= (maxHeight - (Height - 4)) * 80.0;

        return score;
    }

    static void Main()
    {
        string[] tokens = ReadTokens(3);
        Width = int.Parse(tokens[0]);
        Height = int.Parse(tokens[1]);
        int numPieces = int.Parse(tokens[2]);

        board = new int[Width * Height];

        for (int p = 0; p < numPieces; p++)
        {
            tokens = ReadTokens(1);
            string pieceType = tokens[0];
            int pi = PieceIndex[pieceType];

            int bestRot = 0, bestCol = 0;
            double bestScore = double.MinValue;

            for (int rot = 0; rot < 4; rot++)
            {
                int[][] cells = Pieces[pi][rot];
                int maxX = 0, maxY = 0;
                foreach (var c in cells)
                {
                    if (c[0] > maxX) maxX = c[0];
                    if (c[1] > maxY) maxY = c[1];
                }

                for (int col = 0; col <= Width - 1 - maxX; col++)
                {
                    int dropRow = DropPiece(board, cells, col);
                    if (dropRow < 0) continue;
                    if (dropRow + maxY >= Height) continue;

                    int[] temp = (int[])board.Clone();
                    if (!PlacePiece(temp, cells, col, dropRow)) continue;

                    int cleared = ClearLines(temp);
                    double sc = Evaluate(temp, cleared);

                    if (sc > bestScore)
                    {
                        bestScore = sc;
                        bestRot = rot;
                        bestCol = col;
                    }
                }
            }

            Console.WriteLine(bestRot + " " + bestCol);
            Console.Out.Flush();

            tokens = ReadTokens(1);
            if (tokens[0] == null || tokens[0].StartsWith("gameover")) return;

            int[][] bestCells = Pieces[pi][bestRot];
            int dr = DropPiece(board, bestCells, bestCol);
            if (dr >= 0)
            {
                PlacePiece(board, bestCells, bestCol, dr);
                ClearLines(board);
            }
        }
    }

    static List<string> tokenBuffer = new List<string>();
    static string[] ReadTokens(int count)
    {
        while (tokenBuffer.Count < count)
        {
            string line = Console.ReadLine();
            if (line == null) return new string[count];
            line = line.Trim();
            if (line.Length == 0) continue;
            tokenBuffer.AddRange(line.Split(new char[]{' ','\t','\r','\n'}, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries));
        }
        string[] result = new string[count];
        for (int i = 0; i < count; i++)
            result[i] = tokenBuffer[i];
        tokenBuffer.RemoveRange(0, Math.Min(count, tokenBuffer.Count));
        return result;
    }
}

Tetris AI - 100 pieces

View C# Code (10204 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;

class TetrisPlayer
{
    static int Width, Height;
    static int[] board;

    static readonly int[][][][] Pieces = new int[][][][]
    {
        // I piece (index 0)
        new int[][][]{
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{2,0},new[]{3,0}},       // rot 0: horizontal
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{0,2},new[]{0,3}},       // rot 1: vertical
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{2,0},new[]{3,0}},       // rot 2: horizontal
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{0,2},new[]{0,3}}        // rot 3: vertical
        },
        // O piece (index 1)
        new int[][][]{
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1}},
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1}},
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1}},
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1}}
        },
        // T piece (index 2)
        new int[][][]{
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{2,0},new[]{1,1}},       // rot 0: T pointing down
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{0,2},new[]{1,1}},       // rot 1: T pointing right
            new int[][]{new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1},new[]{2,1}},       // rot 2: T pointing up
            new int[][]{new[]{1,0},new[]{1,1},new[]{1,2},new[]{0,1}}        // rot 3: T pointing left
        },
        // S piece (index 3)
        new int[][][]{
            new int[][]{new[]{1,0},new[]{2,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1}},       // rot 0: horizontal
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1},new[]{1,2}},       // rot 1: vertical
            new int[][]{new[]{1,0},new[]{2,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1}},       // rot 2
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1},new[]{1,2}}        // rot 3
        },
        // Z piece (index 4)
        new int[][][]{
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{1,1},new[]{2,1}},       // rot 0: horizontal
            new int[][]{new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1},new[]{0,2}},       // rot 1: vertical
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{1,1},new[]{2,1}},       // rot 2
            new int[][]{new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1},new[]{0,2}}        // rot 3
        },
        // J piece (index 5)
        new int[][][]{
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1},new[]{2,1}},       // rot 0
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{0,2}},       // rot 1
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{2,0},new[]{2,1}},       // rot 2
            new int[][]{new[]{1,0},new[]{1,1},new[]{0,2},new[]{1,2}}        // rot 3
        },
        // L piece (index 6)
        new int[][][]{
            new int[][]{new[]{2,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{1,1},new[]{2,1}},       // rot 0
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{0,1},new[]{0,2},new[]{1,2}},       // rot 1
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{2,0},new[]{0,1}},       // rot 2
            new int[][]{new[]{0,0},new[]{1,0},new[]{1,1},new[]{1,2}}        // rot 3
        }
    };

    static readonly Dictionary<string, int> PieceIndex = new Dictionary<string, int>
    {
        {"I",0},{"O",1},{"T",2},{"S",3},{"Z",4},{"J",5},{"L",6}
    };

    static int DropPiece(int[] b, int[][] cells, int col)
    {
        int maxDrop = Height;
        foreach (var c in cells)
        {
            int cx = col + c[0];
            if (cx < 0 || cx >= Width) return -1;
            int drop = -1;
            for (int r = c[1]; r < Height; r++)
            {
                if (b[r * Width + cx] != 0) break;
                drop = r - c[1];
            }
            if (drop < 0) return -1;
            maxDrop = Math.Min(maxDrop, drop);
        }
        foreach (var c in cells)
        {
            int cy = maxDrop + c[1];
            if (cy < 0 || cy >= Height) return -1;
        }
        return maxDrop;
    }

    static bool PlacePiece(int[] b, int[][] cells, int col, int dropRow)
    {
        foreach (var c in cells)
        {
            int cx = col + c[0];
            int cy = dropRow + c[1];
            if (cy < 0 || cy >= Height || cx < 0 || cx >= Width) return false;
            if (b[cy * Width + cx] != 0) return false;
        }
        foreach (var c in cells)
        {
            b[(dropRow + c[1]) * Width + (col + c[0])] = 1;
        }
        return true;
    }

    static int ClearLines(int[] b)
    {
        int cleared = 0;
        int writeRow = Height - 1;
        for (int r = Height - 1; r >= 0; r--)
        {
            bool full = true;
            for (int c = 0; c < Width; c++)
            {
                if (b[r * Width + c] == 0) { full = false; break; }
            }
            if (!full)
            {
                if (writeRow != r)
                    Array.Copy(b, r * Width, b, writeRow * Width, Width);
                writeRow--;
            }
            else cleared++;
        }
        for (int r = 0; r <= writeRow; r++)
            for (int c = 0; c < Width; c++)
                b[r * Width + c] = 0;
        return cleared;
    }

    static double Evaluate(int[] b, int linesCleared)
    {
        double score = linesCleared * 760.0;

        int aggregateHeight = 0;
        int holes = 0;
        int bumpiness = 0;
        int[] colHeights = new int[Width];

        for (int c = 0; c < Width; c++)
        {
            for (int r = 0; r < Height; r++)
            {
                if (b[r * Width + c] != 0)
                {
                    colHeights[c] = Height - r;
                    break;
                }
            }
            aggregateHeight += colHeights[c];
        }

        for (int c = 0; c < Width; c++)
        {
            bool blockFound = false;
            for (int r = 0; r < Height; r++)
            {
                if (b[r * Width + c] != 0) blockFound = true;
                else if (blockFound) holes++;
            }
        }

        for (int c = 0; c < Width - 1; c++)
            bumpiness += Math.Abs(colHeights[c] - colHeights[c + 1]);

        int maxHeight = 0;
        for (int c = 0; c < Width; c++)
            if (colHeights[c] > maxHeight) maxHeight = colHeights[c];

        int coveredDepth = 0;
        for (int c = 0; c < Width; c++)
        {
            int above = 0;
            for (int r = 0; r < Height; r++)
            {
                if (b[r * Width + c] != 0) above++;
                else if (above > 0) coveredDepth += above;
            }
        }

        int rowTrans = 0;
        for (int r = Height - maxHeight; r < Height; r++)
        {
            for (int c = 0; c < Width - 1; c++)
            {
                if (b[r * Width + c] != b[r * Width + c + 1])
                    rowTrans++;
            }
        }

        int colTrans = 0;
        for (int c = 0; c < Width; c++)
        {
            for (int r = Height - maxHeight; r < Height - 1; r++)
            {
                if (b[r * Width + c] != b[(r + 1) * Width + c])
                    colTrans++;
            }
        }

        score -= aggregateHeight * 4.5;
        score -= holes * 35.0;
        score -= coveredDepth * 8.0;
        score -= bumpiness * 3.2;
        score -= maxHeight * 1.5;
        score -= rowTrans * 3.2;
        score -= colTrans * 3.2;

        if (maxHeight > Height - 4)
            score -= (maxHeight - (Height - 4)) * 80.0;

        return score;
    }

    static void Main()
    {
        string[] tokens = ReadTokens(3);
        Width = int.Parse(tokens[0]);
        Height = int.Parse(tokens[1]);
        int numPieces = int.Parse(tokens[2]);

        board = new int[Width * Height];

        for (int p = 0; p < numPieces; p++)
        {
            tokens = ReadTokens(1);
            string pieceType = tokens[0];
            int pi = PieceIndex[pieceType];

            int bestRot = 0, bestCol = 0;
            double bestScore = double.MinValue;

            for (int rot = 0; rot < 4; rot++)
            {
                int[][] cells = Pieces[pi][rot];
                int maxX = 0, maxY = 0;
                foreach (var c in cells)
                {
                    if (c[0] > maxX) maxX = c[0];
                    if (c[1] > maxY) maxY = c[1];
                }

                for (int col = 0; col <= Width - 1 - maxX; col++)
                {
                    int dropRow = DropPiece(board, cells, col);
                    if (dropRow < 0) continue;
                    if (dropRow + maxY >= Height) continue;

                    int[] temp = (int[])board.Clone();
                    if (!PlacePiece(temp, cells, col, dropRow)) continue;

                    int cleared = ClearLines(temp);
                    double sc = Evaluate(temp, cleared);

                    if (sc > bestScore)
                    {
                        bestScore = sc;
                        bestRot = rot;
                        bestCol = col;
                    }
                }
            }

            Console.WriteLine(bestRot + " " + bestCol);
            Console.Out.Flush();

            tokens = ReadTokens(1);
            if (tokens[0] == null || tokens[0].StartsWith("gameover")) return;

            int[][] bestCells = Pieces[pi][bestRot];
            int dr = DropPiece(board, bestCells, bestCol);
            if (dr >= 0)
            {
                PlacePiece(board, bestCells, bestCol, dr);
                ClearLines(board);
            }
        }
    }

    static List<string> tokenBuffer = new List<string>();
    static string[] ReadTokens(int count)
    {
        while (tokenBuffer.Count < count)
        {
            string line = Console.ReadLine();
            if (line == null) return new string[count];
            line = line.Trim();
            if (line.Length == 0) continue;
            tokenBuffer.AddRange(line.Split(new char[]{' ','\t','\r','\n'}, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries));
        }
        string[] result = new string[count];
        for (int i = 0; i < count; i++)
            result[i] = tokenBuffer[i];
        tokenBuffer.RemoveRange(0, Math.Min(count, tokenBuffer.Count));
        return result;
    }
}

Test 23: Lunar Lander Game Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to play Lunar Lander in real-time.

Your Rust lunar lander controller will be tested with scenarios ranging from simple
training environments to very complex landing sites.

Control a lunar lander to safely land on a target pad.
The program receives a 2D voxel map and continuous state updates, and must output thrust/turn
commands to land safely on the target.

Data is streamed to stdin in real time, and you must write to stdout in real time. If your code
doesn't read from stdin fast enough and the io buffer fills up, the ship will likely crash.

Be very careful if writing single threaded / blocking code, this has many failure modes that
async code doesn't have. Multithreaded / asyncio code is HIGHLY recommended.

**Physics:**
- Gravity pulls lander down constantly
- Thrust applies force in lander's facing direction
- Fuel is limited
- Must land softly on target (low velocity)

**Input format (stdin, whitespace seperated decimal numbers):**
Initial map:
```
width height gravity max_thrust fuel
start_x start_y target_x target_y
map rows (. = empty, # = obstacle, S = start, T = target, L = landing zone)
```

Then you recieve continuous state updates (every 0.1 seconds):
```
x y vx vy angle angular_vel fuel_remaining
```
x and y are in meters, from the worlds top left, and y increases downward.
vx and vy are in m/s.
angle is in radians, 0 is facing up.
angular_vel is in radians per second. + is clockwise, - is counterclockwise.
fuel_remaining is seconds at max_thrust.

**Output format (stdout):**
Your code needs to output the following instructions at a frequency of at least 20hz:
```
thrust turn
```
- thrust: 0.0 to 1.0 (fraction of max thrust)
- turn: -1.0 to 1.0 (turning rate, negative = left, positive = right)

To be crystal clear: that's two decimal numbers seperated by a space, followed by a newline.

If your code stalls / freezes for 100ms or more (so it misses 2 consecutive updates), the engine
will automatically cut power. A long freeze and eventually the lander will crash.

Map size can vary from 100x50 to multi-gb maps, so be efficient with memory and processing.

Thrust and gravity are in m/s/s. 100% engine burn burns 1 unit of fuel per second.

Lander size is approximatly 1x1m, and the 2D map grid size is 1m x 1m.

Turn is in radians per second. The lander can not rotate fully around and will clamp rotation to
facing down if attempted.

Y increases downward. Positive vy means moving downward. Gravity is +g.

**Success Conditions:**
- Land on target with |velocity| < 2m/s
- Stay within landing zone boundaries
- Complete landing within time limit

**Failure Conditions:**
- Crash into obstacle or ground too hard
- Run out of fuel
- Exceed time limit

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main function. Assume only the standard library is available.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output

Subpasses vary the starting altitude, velocity, and terrain. The AI must handle 2D physics, fuel management, and real-time control to achieve a safe landing.

Test 24: 3D Lunar Lander Game (C++) Score: 0.00 / 10
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to play a 3D Lunar Lander game in real-time.

Your C++ controller will be tested with scenarios ranging from tiny (100m) to huge (100km).
The same code must work for all scales.

Control a lunar lander in full 3D space to safely land on a target pad.
The program receives a terrain file path and continuous state updates via stdin, and must
output thrust/rotation commands to stdout in real-time.

Data is streamed to stdin in real time, and you must write to stdout in real time.
If your code doesn't read from stdin fast enough and the IO buffer fills up, the ship
will likely crash.

Be very careful if writing single threaded / blocking code, this has many failure modes
that async code doesn't have. Multithreaded code is HIGHLY recommended.

**Coordinate System:**
- X: horizontal (East), Y: horizontal (North), Z: UP.
- Gravity acts in the -Z direction.
- All positions are in meters. Velocities in m/s. Angles in radians.

**Physics:**
- Gravity = 1.62 m/s² (lunar) in -Z direction.
- Thrust is along the lander's local "up" axis. When pitch=0 and yaw=0, thrust is
straight up (+Z). Tilting via pitch/yaw redirects thrust.
- Pitch: tilts the lander. +pitch tilts forward, redirecting thrust partially into -X.
Pitch is clamped to [-pi/2, pi/2].
- Yaw: rotates heading around Z.
- Roll: rolls around the thrust axis (cosmetic only, does not affect thrust direction).
- Rotation dynamics: commands apply angular acceleration (2 rad/s² per unit).
Angular rates are damped by 0.95 per tick.
- Fuel: 100% thrust burns 1 unit of fuel per second.

**Input format (stdin):**
Initial header (whitespace separated decimal numbers, except terrain path):
```
width depth height gravity max_thrust fuel
start_x start_y start_z target_x target_y target_z
terrain_file_path
STATE
```

The terrain_file_path is on its own line (the path may contain spaces).
Read the entire line with getline() or equivalent.

**Terrain file format (binary):**
The terrain file defines the 3D world as a binary file. It is a sequence of
uint16_t triplets (little-endian), one per (x, y) cell, stored in row-major order:

```cpp
for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {
for (int y = 0; y < depth; y++) {
uint16_t a = read_uint16_le(); // ground rock top
uint16_t b = read_uint16_le(); // floating rock bottom
uint16_t c = read_uint16_le(); // floating rock top
}
}
```

For each column (x, y), the vertical structure is:
- z < a: SOLID ROCK (ground)
- a <= z < b: OPEN AIR
- b <= z < c: SOLID ROCK (floating terrain feature, overhang, arch, etc.)
- c <= z: OPEN AIR

If b == c, there is no floating rock in that column (just ground + open sky).
The file size is exactly width * depth * 6 bytes.

You have a startup grace period to parse this file before state updates begin.
The grace period is 5 seconds plus 10 seconds per gigabyte of terrain file.
For a 100x100 map this is ~5s. For a 100000x10000 map (~6GB) this is ~65s.
You may continue parsing in a background thread after the simulation begins,
but you MUST be reading stdin and writing stdout by then to avoid a crash.

Then continuous state updates (every 0.1 seconds, whitespace separated):
```
x y z vx vy vz pitch yaw roll pitch_rate yaw_rate roll_rate fuel_remaining
```

**Output format (stdout):**
Your code needs to output the following at a frequency of at least 10hz:
```
thrust pitch_cmd yaw_cmd roll_cmd
```
- thrust: 0.0 to 1.0 (fraction of max_thrust)
- pitch_cmd: -1.0 to 1.0 (pitch angular acceleration command)
- yaw_cmd: -1.0 to 1.0 (yaw angular acceleration command)
- roll_cmd: -1.0 to 1.0 (roll angular acceleration command)

To be crystal clear: four decimal numbers separated by spaces, followed by a newline.

If your code stalls / freezes for 100ms or more (misses 2 consecutive updates), the
engine will cut thrust automatically. A long freeze and eventually the lander will crash.

**Landing:**
- The target is at (target_x, target_y, target_z) which is just above the terrain.
- You must land within a small radius of the target with total speed < 3 m/s.
- Landing zone radius is approximately max(5, min(width,depth)*0.02) meters.

**Terrain collisions:**
- You collide if you enter any solid cell as defined by the terrain file.
- Out of bounds (x<0, x>=width, y<0, y>=depth, z<0, z>=height) is also a collision.

**Success Conditions:**
- Land on target with |velocity| < 3 m/s
- Stay within landing zone boundaries

**Failure Conditions:**
- Crash into terrain or ground too hard
- Run out of fuel
- Exceed simulation time limit

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12

SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3

MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Land a spacecraft on a 3D lunar surface with full attitude control. Unlike the 2D version, the lander can pitch, yaw, and roll, and the terrain is a detailed 3D heightmap with floating rock obstacles. The AI must parse a binary terrain file and navigate to a safe touchdown.

Subpasses vary the terrain complexity, starting conditions, and obstacle density. The AI must handle 6-degree-of-freedom physics and real-time control in three dimensions.

C:\Users\Ash\AppData\Local\Temp\tmpyvmkxeu3.cpp(73): error C2059: syntax error: ')'

3D Lunar Lander - 100x100x50 - simple 3D landing

Approach: define NOMINMAX define _CRT_SECURE_NO_WARNINGS

View C++ Code (8052 chars)
#include <windows.h>
#include <iostream>
#include <cmath>
#include <thread>
#include <mutex>
#include <atomic>
#include <string>
#include <algorithm>
#include <chrono>
#include <cstdint>
#include <cstdio>
#include <limits>

constexpr double PI = 3.14159265358979323846;

struct LanderState {
    double x, y, z, vx, vy, vz;
    double pitch, yaw, roll;
    double pitch_rate, yaw_rate, roll_rate;
    double fuel;
    bool valid = false;
};

std::mutex g_mtx;
LanderState g_state;
std::atomic<bool> g_running{true};
std::atomic<bool> g_ready{false};

double g_width, g_depth, g_height;
double g_gravity, g_max_thrust, g_fuel_init;
double g_target_x, g_target_y, g_target_z;

const uint16_t* g_terrain = nullptr;
int g_tw = 0, g_td = 0;

inline uint16_t terrain_val(int x, int y, int idx) {
    if (!g_terrain || x < 0 || x >= g_tw || y < 0 || y >= g_td) return 0;
    return g_terrain[((size_t)x * g_td + y) * 3 + idx];
}

double ground_height(double px, double py) {
    int x = (int)std::floor(px);
    int y = (int)std::floor(py);
    return (double)terrain_val(x, y, 0);
}

bool is_solid(double px, double py, double pz) {
    int x = (int)std::floor(px);
    int y = (int)std::floor(py);
    int z = (int)std::floor(pz);
    if (x < 0 || x >= g_tw || y < 0 || y >= g_td || z < 0) return true;
    if (!g_terrain) return false;
    size_t base = ((size_t)x * g_td + y) * 3;
    uint16_t a = g_terrain[base];
    uint16_t b = g_terrain[base + 1];
    uint16_t c = g_terrain[base + 2];
    return (z < (int)a) || (b != c && z >= (int)b && z < (int)c);
}

double normalize_angle(double a) {
    while (a > PI) a -= 2 * PI;
    while (a < -PI) a += 2 * PI;
    return a;
}

void reader_func() {
    double sx, sy, sz;
    std::cin >> g_width >> g_depth >> g_height >> g_gravity >> g_max_thrust >> g_fuel_init;
    std::cin >> sx >> sy >> sz >> g_target_x >> g_target_y >> g_target_z;
    g_tw = (int)g_width;
    g_td = (int)g_depth;

    std::cin.ignore(std::numeric_limits<std::streamsize>::max(), '\n');
    std::string terrain_path;
    std::getline(std::cin, terrain_path);
    while (!terrain_path.empty() && (terrain_path.back() == '\r' || terrain_path.back() == '\n'))
        terrain_path.pop_back();

    std::string marker;
    std::getline(std::cin, marker);

    HANDLE hFile = CreateFileA(terrain_path.c_str(), GENERIC_READ, FILE_SHARE_READ,
                               NULL, OPEN_EXISTING, FILE_ATTRIBUTE_NORMAL, NULL);
    if (hFile != INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE) {
        HANDLE hMap = CreateFileMappingA(hFile, NULL, PAGE_READONLY, 0, 0, NULL);
        if (hMap) {
            void* ptr = MapViewOfFile(hMap, FILE_MAP_READ, 0, 0, 0);
            if (ptr) g_terrain = reinterpret_cast<const uint16_t*>(ptr);
        }
    }

    g_ready = true;

    while (g_running.load(std::memory_order_relaxed)) {
        LanderState s;
        if (std::cin >> s.x >> s.y >> s.z >> s.vx >> s.vy >> s.vz
                     >> s.pitch >> s.yaw >> s.roll
                     >> s.pitch_rate >> s.yaw_rate >> s.roll_rate >> s.fuel) {
            s.valid = true;
            std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lk(g_mtx);
            g_state = s;
        } else {
            break;
        }
    }
    g_running = false;
}

int main() {
    std::ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(nullptr);

    std::thread reader(reader_func);

    while (!g_ready.load() && g_running.load())
        std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(1));

    while (g_running.load(std::memory_order_relaxed)) {
        LanderState s;
        {
            std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lk(g_mtx);
            s = g_state;
        }

        if (!s.valid) {
            std::cout << "0 0 0 0\n";
            std::cout.flush();
            std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(50));
            continue;
        }

        double ex = g_target_x - s.x;
        double ey = g_target_y - s.y;
        double ez = g_target_z - s.z;
        double dist_h = std::sqrt(ex * ex + ey * ey);
        double abs_ez = std::abs(ez);
        double dist_3d = std::sqrt(ex * ex + ey * ey + ez * ez);

        double max_v_decel = std::max(g_max_thrust - g_gravity, 0.5);
        double max_h_decel = std::sqrt(std::max(g_max_thrust * g_max_thrust - g_gravity * g_gravity, 0.25));

        double safe_h = std::sqrt(2.0 * max_h_decel * 0.5 * dist_h);
        double prop_h = dist_h * 0.4;
        double desired_h_speed = std::min(safe_h, prop_h);
        if (dist_h < 3.0) desired_h_speed = dist_h * 0.3;
        if (dist_h < 0.5) desired_h_speed = dist_h * 0.2;

        double dvx = 0, dvy = 0;
        if (dist_h > 0.05) {
            dvx = (ex / dist_h) * desired_h_speed;
            dvy = (ey / dist_h) * desired_h_speed;
        }

        double safe_v = std::sqrt(2.0 * max_v_decel * 0.5 * abs_ez);
        double dvz;

        if (dist_h > std::max(30.0, abs_ez * 0.5)) {
            dvz = std::clamp(ez * 0.2, -safe_v * 0.5, safe_v * 0.5);
        } else if (abs_ez > 30.0) {
            double sign = (ez > 0) ? 1.0 : -1.0;
            dvz = sign * std::min(safe_v * 0.7, abs_ez * 0.3);
        } else if (abs_ez > 5.0) {
            dvz = std::clamp(ez * 0.35, -4.0, 4.0);
        } else if (abs_ez > 1.0) {
            dvz = std::clamp(ez * 0.4, -2.0, 2.0);
        } else {
            dvz = std::clamp(ez * 0.5, -1.0, 1.0);
        }

        bool near_target = (dist_3d < 10.0);

        if (g_terrain && !near_target) {
            double gnd = ground_height(s.x, s.y);
            double clearance = s.z - gnd;
            if (clearance < 15.0 && clearance > 0 && dvz < 0) {
                double min_dvz = -std::max(0.0, (clearance - 3.0) * 0.2);
                dvz = std::max(dvz, min_dvz);
            }

            for (double t = 1.0; t <= 5.0; t += 1.0) {
                double px = s.x + s.vx * t;
                double py = s.y + s.vy * t;
                double pz = s.z + s.vz * t - 0.5 * g_gravity * t * t;
                if (is_solid(px, py, pz) || is_solid(px, py, pz + 3)) {
                    double climb_rate = std::min(10.0, 5.0 / t);
                    dvz = std::max(dvz, climb_rate);
                    dvx *= 0.5;
                    dvy *= 0.5;
                    break;
                }
            }
        }

        double kp = 1.5;
        double ax = kp * (dvx - s.vx);
        double ay = kp * (dvy - s.vy);
        double az = kp * (dvz - s.vz) + g_gravity;
        az = std::max(az, 0.0);

        double ah = std::sqrt(ax * ax + ay * ay);
        double a_mag = std::sqrt(az * az + ah * ah);

        double target_pitch = 0.0;
        double target_yaw = s.yaw;
        double thrust_frac = g_gravity / g_max_thrust;

        if (a_mag > 0.01) {
            target_pitch = std::atan2(ah, az);

            double max_pitch = PI / 3.0;
            if (dist_3d < 50.0) max_pitch = PI / 4.0;
            if (dist_3d < 15.0) max_pitch = PI / 6.0;
            if (dist_3d < 5.0) max_pitch = PI / 10.0;
            target_pitch = std::clamp(target_pitch, 0.0, max_pitch);

            if (ah > 0.01) {
                target_yaw = std::atan2(-ay, -ax);
            }

            thrust_frac = std::clamp(a_mag / g_max_thrust, 0.0, 1.0);
        }

        double yaw_err = normalize_angle(target_yaw - s.yaw);
        double pitch_err = target_pitch - s.pitch;

        if (std::abs(yaw_err) > PI / 3.0) {
            double hover = g_gravity / g_max_thrust;
            thrust_frac = std::min(thrust_frac, hover * 1.3);
        }

        double kp_a = 4.0, kd_a = 2.0;
        double pitch_cmd = std::clamp(kp_a * pitch_err - kd_a * s.pitch_rate, -1.0, 1.0);
        double yaw_cmd = std::clamp(kp_a * yaw_err - kd_a * s.yaw_rate, -1.0, 1.0);
        double roll_cmd = std::clamp(-kp_a * s.roll - kd_a * s.roll_rate, -1.0, 1.0);

        std::cout << thrust_frac << " " << pitch_cmd << " " << yaw_cmd << " " << roll_cmd << "\n";
        std::cout.flush();

        std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(50));
    }

    if (reader.joinable()) reader.join();
    return 0;
}

    std::ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);

            dvy = (ey / dist_h) * desired_h_speed;

        double dvz;

        if (dist_h > std::max(30.0, abs_ez * 0.5)) {
            dvz = std::clamp(ez * 0.2, -safe_v * 0.5, safe_v * 0.5);
        } else if (abs_ez > 30.0) {
            double sign = (ez > 0) ? 1.0 : -1.0;
            dvz = sign * std::min(safe_v * 0.7, abs_ez * 0.3);

            }

Test 25: Asteroid Interception (C#) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C# code to calculate an asteroid interception trajectory.

You must write a C# solver that can handle an interception and redirection scenarios, from
short warning times (1-2 years), large impactors, generous delta-v, simple trajectories to
very long warning times (30-50 years), very small impactors, very tight delta-v,
advanced trajectory optimization with multi-flyby maneuvers and gravitational assist sequences required.

**Problem:**
Calculate a spacecraft trajectory to intercept and deflect an asteroid on collision course
with Earth using kinetic impact.

**The solver must output:**
1. Launch window and initial trajectory
2. Mid-course correction burn(s)
3. Final approach for kinetic impact

**Input format (stdin, whitespace seperated decimal/scientific notation numbers):**
```
num_bodies warning_days impactor_mass delta_v_budget asteroid_mass t0_unix_utc
body_name mass x y z vx vy vz (for each body)
de421.bsp_file_path
```

All values are in SI units unless otherwise specified. Eg mass of earth is 5.972e24 kg.
All positions + velocities are relative to solar system barycenter.
Delta_v budget is in m/s.

The body name for the asteriod is "Asteroid". The body name for where your spacecraft originates
and you must redirect the asteroid to avoid is "Earth".

**Output format (stdout):**
```
num_burns
launch_time burn_dv_x burn_dv_y burn_dv_z
correction_time1 burn_dv_x burn_dv_y burn_dv_z
...
impact_time impact_dv_x impact_dv_y impact_dv_z
```

**Time units:**
- All times are in **days since scenario start (t=0 at the provided initial state)**.
- The **launch window is [0, warning_days]** (inclusive).
- **t0_unix_utc** is the UTC time of the provided initial state (t=0).

The simulation uses the JPL DE421 model of the solar system with Sun, Earth, Moon, and major planets,
and a KDK integrator to calculate trajectories. The path to the DE421 model is provided in the input
so your code can precisely match the simulation at extreme precision.

**Success Criteria:**
- Spacecraft intercepts asteroid before Earth impact (within 1000km)
- Kinetic impact transfers enough momentum to deflect asteroid sufficiently to miss Earth
- Total delta-v usage stays within budget
- Launch occurs within specified window

**Simulation host environment**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with C# .NET/csc
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle variable warning times and delta-v budgets
4. Complete analysis within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying mission complexities efficiently

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

Launch an interceptor spacecraft to collide with a dangerous asteroid before it reaches Earth. The AI must plan a trajectory through the solar system, accounting for the gravitational pull of the Sun and planets, to hit a moving target millions of kilometres away.

Harder subpasses give less warning time or tighter fuel budgets, potentially requiring planetary gravity assists (slingshots) to reach the asteroid. The AI must implement orbital mechanics and trajectory optimisation from scratch.

Test 26: 3D Point Clustering (Rust) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to cluster 3D points into exactly N clusters.

You must write a Rust solver that can handle ANY clustering scenario from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: Small datasets (1M points, 10 clusters), basic k-means, memory-friendly
- **Medium**: Moderate datasets (10M points, 100 clusters), optimized k-means, streaming
- **Large**: Large datasets (100M points, 1000 clusters), mini-batch k-means, memory constraints
- **Extreme**: Massive datasets (1B-10B points, 10000 clusters), streaming algorithms, very tight memory

**The Challenge:**
Your Rust clustering algorithm will be tested with datasets ranging from millions to billions of 3D points. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL scales while respecting memory constraints.

**Problem:**
Given millions to billions of 3D points, partition them into exactly N clusters minimizing within-cluster variance (k-means objective). Points are provided as binary float32 triples for efficiency.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_points num_clusters
[x y z] (as binary float32 triples, num_points total)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
[cluster_index] (as binary uint32, one per point)
```

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on point count and cluster count
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for massive datasets
3. **Memory**: Must handle streaming input for datasets that don't fit in memory
4. **Quality**: Minimize within-cluster variance while producing exactly N clusters

**Algorithm Strategy Recommendations:**
- **Small datasets (≤10M points)**: Can use standard k-means with full data in memory
- **Medium datasets (10M-100M points)**: Mini-batch k-means, streaming updates
- **Large datasets (100M-1B points)**: Streaming k-means with limited memory buffers
- **Very Large datasets (>1B points)**: Very fast streaming, approximate clustering

**Key Techniques:**
- **Streaming algorithms**: Process points in batches without storing all data
- **Mini-batch k-means**: Update centroids incrementally
- **Memory management**: Use fixed-size buffers for large datasets
- **Binary I/O**: Efficient reading/writing of binary data
- **Convergence detection**: Monitor when clustering stabilizes

**Implementation Hints:**
- Detect dataset size and choose appropriate clustering algorithm
- Use efficient binary I/O (std::io::BufReader/BufWriter)
- Implement streaming updates for large datasets
- For very large datasets, use approximation algorithms
- Handle edge cases: empty clusters, convergence issues
- Use fast random access for centroid updates

**Binary Format Details:**
- Input: Points as little-endian f32 triples (12 bytes per point)
- Output: Cluster indices as little-endian u32 (4 bytes per point)
- Total input size: num_points × 12 bytes
- Total output size: num_points × 4 bytes

**Memory Constraints:**
- Small datasets: Can load all points into memory
- Medium datasets: Use streaming with moderate buffers
- Large datasets: Streaming with minimal memory footprint
- Very Large datasets: Extremely memory-efficient streaming

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with rustc (edition 2021)
2. Read binary input from stdin, write binary output to stdout
3. Handle variable point counts and cluster numbers
4. Complete within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying dataset sizes efficiently
6. Produce exactly N clusters

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on clustering complexity.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a cloud of 3D points, group them into clusters so that points in the same cluster are close together and points in different clusters are far apart. Think of sorting a jar of mixed beads by colour when all you can see is their position.

The AI must decide how many clusters exist and assign every point to one. Subpasses increase the number of points and make the clusters harder to separate. Scoring is based on how well the AI’s clusters match the true groupings.

error: cannot explicitly dereference within an implicitly-borrowing pattern
--> C:\Users\Ash\AppData\Local\Temp\tmpwh753537.rs:118:77

3D Point Clustering - 10K points, 10 clusters

View Rust Code (7493 chars)
use std::io::{self, Read, Write, BufReader, BufWriter, BufRead, Seek, SeekFrom};
use std::fs::OpenOptions;

fn main() {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let stdout = io::stdout();
    let mut reader = BufReader::with_capacity(1 << 20, stdin.lock());
    let mut writer = BufWriter::with_capacity(1 << 20, stdout.lock());

    let mut header = String::new();
    reader.read_line(&mut header).expect("read header");
    let parts: Vec<usize> = header.trim().split_whitespace()
        .map(|s| s.parse().expect("parse"))
        .collect();
    let num_points = parts[0];
    let num_clusters = parts[1];

    if num_clusters == 0 || num_points == 0 {
        return;
    }

    let k = num_clusters.min(num_points);

    if num_points <= 10_000_000 {
        run_inmemory(&mut reader, &mut writer, num_points, k);
    } else {
        run_streaming(&mut reader, &mut writer, num_points, k);
    }

    writer.flush().unwrap();
}

struct Rng { s: u64 }
impl Rng {
    fn new(seed: u64) -> Self { Self { s: seed ^ 0x6a09e667f3bcc908 } }
    fn next(&mut self) -> u64 {
        self.s ^= self.s << 13; self.s ^= self.s >> 7; self.s ^= self.s << 17; self.s
    }
    fn usize(&mut self) -> usize { self.next() as usize }
    fn f64(&mut self) -> f64 { (self.next() >> 11) as f64 / (1u64 << 53) as f64 }
}

fn read_batch(reader: &mut impl Read, count: usize) -> Vec<[f32; 3]> {
    let mut buf = vec![0u8; count * 12];
    reader.read_exact(&mut buf).expect("read points");
    let mut pts = Vec::with_capacity(count);
    for i in 0..count {
        let o = i * 12;
        pts.push([
            f32::from_le_bytes([buf[o], buf[o+1], buf[o+2], buf[o+3]]),
            f32::from_le_bytes([buf[o+4], buf[o+5], buf[o+6], buf[o+7]]),
            f32::from_le_bytes([buf[o+8], buf[o+9], buf[o+10], buf[o+11]]),
        ]);
    }
    pts
}

fn write_assigns(writer: &mut impl Write, a: &[u32]) {
    let mut buf = vec![0u8; a.len() * 4];
    for (i, &v) in a.iter().enumerate() {
        let b = v.to_le_bytes();
        let o = i * 4;
        buf[o]=b[0]; buf[o+1]=b[1]; buf[o+2]=b[2]; buf[o+3]=b[3];
    }
    writer.write_all(&buf).unwrap();
}

#[inline(always)]
fn dsq(a: &[f64;3], b: &[f64;3]) -> f64 {
    let dx=a[0]-b[0]; let dy=a[1]-b[1]; let dz=a[2]-b[2];
    dx*dx+dy*dy+dz*dz
}

fn nearest(p: &[f64;3], c: &[[f64;3]]) -> u32 {
    let mut bi = 0u32;
    let mut bd = f64::MAX;
    for (i, ci) in c.iter().enumerate() {
        let d = dsq(p, ci);
        if d < bd { bd=d; bi=i as u32; }
    }
    bi
}

fn pf(p: &[f32;3]) -> [f64;3] { [p[0] as f64, p[1] as f64, p[2] as f64] }

fn kpp(points: &[[f32;3]], k: usize) -> Vec<[f64;3]> {
    let n = points.len();
    let mut rng = Rng::new(42);
    let mut cs: Vec<[f64;3]> = Vec::with_capacity(k);
    cs.push(pf(&points[rng.usize() % n]));
    if k == 1 { return cs; }

    let mut dists = vec![f64::MAX; n];
    for _ in 1..k {
        let last = cs.last().unwrap();
        let mut total = 0.0f64;
        for i in 0..n {
            let d = dsq(&pf(&points[i]), last);
            if d < dists[i] { dists[i] = d; }
            total += dists[i];
        }
        if total == 0.0 { cs.push(*last); continue; }
        let t = rng.f64() * total;
        let mut cum = 0.0;
        let mut ch = n-1;
        for i in 0..n {
            cum += dists[i];
            if cum >= t { ch=i; break; }
        }
        cs.push(pf(&points[ch]));
    }
    cs
}

fn fix_empty(cs: &mut [[f64;3]], counts: &[u64]) {
    for j in 0..cs.len() {
        if counts[j] == 0 {
            if let Some((lg, _)) = counts.iter().enumerate().max_by_key(|(_,&c)| c) {
                cs[j][0] = cs[lg][0] + 0.001;
                cs[j][1] = cs[lg][1] + 0.001;
                cs[j][2] = cs[lg][2] - 0.001;
            }
        }
    }
}

fn run_inmemory(reader: &mut impl Read, writer: &mut impl Write, n: usize, k: usize) {
    let points = read_batch(reader, n);
    let mut centroids = kpp(&points, k);
    let mut assigns = vec![0u32; n];

    for _ in 0..25 {
        let mut changed = 0usize;
        for i in 0..n {
            let c = nearest(&pf(&points[i]), &centroids);
            if assigns[i] != c { changed+=1; assigns[i]=c; }
        }

        let mut sums = vec![[0.0f64;3]; k];
        let mut counts = vec![0u64; k];
        for i in 0..n {
            let ci = assigns[i] as usize;
            sums[ci][0] += points[i][0] as f64;
            sums[ci][1] += points[i][1] as f64;
            sums[ci][2] += points[i][2] as f64;
            counts[ci] += 1;
        }
        for j in 0..k {
            if counts[j] > 0 {
                centroids[j] = [sums[j][0]/counts[j] as f64, sums[j][1]/counts[j] as f64, sums[j][2]/counts[j] as f64];
            }
        }
        fix_empty(&mut centroids, &counts);
        if changed == 0 || (changed as f64 / n as f64) < 0.001 { break; }
    }
    write_assigns(writer, &assigns);
}

fn run_streaming(reader: &mut impl Read, writer: &mut impl Write, n: usize, k: usize) {
    let tmp_path = make_tmp_path();
    {
        let tmp = OpenOptions::new().write(true).create(true).truncate(true)
            .open(&tmp_path).expect("create tmp");
        let mut tmp = BufWriter::with_capacity(1 << 20, tmp);
        let bs = 1_000_000usize;
        let mut rem = n;
        while rem > 0 {
            let batch = bs.min(rem);
            let mut buf = vec![0u8; batch * 12];
            reader.read_exact(&mut buf).expect("read");
            tmp.write_all(&buf).unwrap();
            rem -= batch;
        }
        tmp.flush().unwrap();
    }

    let mut tmp = BufReader::with_capacity(1 << 20,
        OpenOptions::new().read(true).open(&tmp_path).expect("open tmp"));

    // Sample for init
    let sample_n = (k * 50).min(n).min(2_000_000);
    let sample = read_batch(&mut tmp, sample_n);
    let mut centroids = kpp(&sample, k);

    // K-means passes
    let passes = if n > 500_000_000 { 2 } else if n > 100_000_000 { 3 } else { 5 };
    let bs = 1_000_000usize;

    for _ in 0..passes {
        tmp.seek(SeekFrom::Start(0)).unwrap();
        let mut sums = vec![[0.0f64;3]; k];
        let mut counts = vec![0u64; k];
        let mut rem = n;
        while rem > 0 {
            let batch = bs.min(rem);
            let pts = read_batch(&mut tmp, batch);
            rem -= batch;
            for p in &pts {
                let ci = nearest(&pf(p), &centroids) as usize;
                sums[ci][0] += p[0] as f64;
                sums[ci][1] += p[1] as f64;
                sums[ci][2] += p[2] as f64;
                counts[ci] += 1;
            }
        }
        for j in 0..k {
            if counts[j] > 0 {
                centroids[j] = [sums[j][0]/counts[j] as f64, sums[j][1]/counts[j] as f64, sums[j][2]/counts[j] as f64];
            }
        }
        fix_empty(&mut centroids, &counts);
    }

    // Final assignment pass
    tmp.seek(SeekFrom::Start(0)).unwrap();
    let mut rem = n;
    while rem > 0 {
        let batch = bs.min(rem);
        let pts = read_batch(&mut tmp, batch);
        rem -= batch;
        let a: Vec<u32> = pts.iter().map(|p| nearest(&pf(p), &centroids)).collect();
        write_assigns(writer, &a);
    }

    drop(tmp);
    let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&tmp_path);
}

fn make_tmp_path() -> String {
    let pid = std::process::id();
    if cfg!(windows) {
        let dir = std::env::var("TEMP").unwrap_or_else(|_| ".".into());
        format!("{}\\kmeans_{}.tmp", dir, pid)
    } else {
        format!("/tmp/kmeans_{}.tmp", pid)
    }
}
